The size is easy to hold and the edges aren’t sharp. I wash with mild soap and warm water and they clean up easily. Packaging was very simple, but mine arrived clean with no chips or cracks.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: Glowa Sonic Facial Cleansing Brush with Gentle Exfoliating|Deep Cleansing|Mix Massaging 3 Acoustic Modes, Type-c Rechargeable, Waterproof,Includes Nylon and Silicone Brush Heads.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: Really love the one that is more like silicone. Massages the face and cleans at the same time. Like using it in the shower with my purity facial cleaner. Easy to change out to the brush head. You charged it via USB so you will need an adapter for your outlet if you don't have USB in the bathroom. Would make a wonderful gift for anyone really who enjoys clean skin.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review: Older sensitive skin here and I wanted something that cleans without feeling like I sanded my face. For me the silicone head is the winner, the nylon one feels too scrubby. It does take off sunscreen/makeup better than just fingers and the 3 modes are simple, I keep it on the gentlest. It’s Type-C rechargeable which I like, just note you may need your own wall plug in the bathroom. Battery has been fine so far between charges.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: BEAM be amazing Collagen Peptides (Unflavored, 20 Servings)
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: I use Collagen in my coffee everyday. I love the unflavored kind that dissolves fast like this.  There is no taste at all. My hair and skin have been so much better since starting this daily usage. Good skin comes from the inside out. We loos collagen starting around age 30. I do wish I had not waited so long to start using it.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review: This collagen is truly unflavored for me. No weird smell and it disappears in my morning coffee with just a quick stir, no gritty clumps. That makes it easy to take daily and I actually stick with it. After about 6 weeks I have noticed my skin looks better and less dry/papery, my fine thin hair seems a bit thicker, and I was getting little skin tears that have stopped. No stomach upset.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: DAVEMED Mini First Aid Kits,Small Waterproof Emergency Medical Survival Kit for Camping,Hiking,Travel,Backpacking,Vehicle,Outdoors & Sports（78 Pieces）
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: Not the highest quality as far as scissors or tweezers but in an emergency situation it will be very helpful. Going to take on my bike when I go on longer rides. Wish I had it on a ride a friend fell off her bike, would of been great to bandage her cuts. Always wise to have first aid kit around.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review: Good little kit for the car and my day pack. Case feels sturdy and the zipper closes tight so it stays compact, and I can see what I need fast without dumping everything out. For small cuts/blisters it has the basics I actually reach for first (bandages, gauze, wipes) and they did the job on a finger cut. I would not trust the “waterproof” claim if it sat in water, more like splash resistant. No instructions, so you need basic first aid knowledge.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: skyn ICELAND Nordic Renewal Pre + Probiotic Face Cream Starter Set: Deeply Nourishing for Dry, Dull, Lackluster Complexions, 50ml
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: Have had no irritation to my skin. The cream goes on cool and smooth. I sometimes get very hot read face due to auto immune issues . Put this on and it was calming and soothing. I am adding this cream to my new system and watching the fine lines diminish. Very happy with the product. You just have to use a tiny bit so it goes a long way.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review: My cheeks get hot/red easily so my main test is no stinging and no heavy perfume. This skyn ICELAND Nordic Renewal cream feels rich but it soaks in and doesn’t leave me greasy. I noticed by the next morning my skin felt calmer and less tight, and after a few nights my face looked less dull. It is pricey for 50ml, but you don’t need much, so for me it may last long enough to be worth it.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: Elaine Wellness Hydrolyzed Collagen Powder Packets for Women, Convenient Single Travel Size Supplement for Skin, Hair, Joints and Nails, Protein Peptides Dissolve Easily, Unflavored (10 Gram, 7 Pack)
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: Convenient take with you doses of collagen.  It adds no taste to your coffee in the morning. My skin has improved greatly from daily doses of collagen. I have always had fine thin hair. As I have gotten older it was thinning more. I feel like it has thickened up a bit. I was getting skin tears too and that has stopped. I started to see results in about 6 weeks.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review:
I use collagen in my coffee every day so these single packets are perfect for travel and just keeping my routine simple. Each packet is 10 grams so there’s no guessing. It really is unflavored to me and the taste doesn’t change as I drink the whole cup (no weird aftertaste), and it dissolves fast with no grit.

I noticed my nails peeling less after about 2 weeks of daily use, and my hair/skin seem about the same kind of “inside out” support I get with my usual collagen. Easy way to not skip days.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: Oatmeal Protein Pie Powder, Vegan Oat Based Plant Protein Drink, 20+ Vitamins and Minerals, Promote Muscle Growth (Original Marshmallow, 2.2 pounds)
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: I so wanted this to be an answer for me. It is overly sweet when using a full scoop. The sweetener leaves a rather unpleasant aftertaste. It does taste like an oatmeal marshmallow pie. The first sip is really good. The more I drank the sweetener aftertaste got too strong to enjoy. The flavor is basically good, just that aftertaste that turned me off. On a positive note it did help me feel full for a long time. Next time I try it perhaps I will try a half scoop, maybe won't be too sweet.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review:
Trying to add more plant protein and this oatmeal based powder is workable, but the gate for me is flavor over a full serving. First few sips of the Original Marshmallow are like sweet cereal milk, then the aftertaste builds into that fake sweet note and I can’t finish it. Half a scoop in a bigger glass of water is the only way I can drink it, and I do better with it blended with ice.

As far as “does it do what I bought it for,” the smaller amount still keeps me surprisingly satisfied and it’s an easy way to add protein without dairy. Full scoop is just too sweet for me to use daily.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: Replacement Toothbrush Heads Compatible with Philips Sonicare, 10 Pack Electric Toothbrush Heads C2 for 2 Series Plaque Control ProtectiveClean 4100 5100 6100 Handles,with Hygienic Caps HX9024
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: These work just like the name brand ones from Sonic. They are just a tad bigger. I have noticed they pull off the brush sometimes when I take the cover off as it fits snuggly. Otherwise they are the same.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review: These fit my Philips Sonicare ProtectiveClean handle, but a couple heads don’t click on as securely as my OEM ones. They’ll work for brushing, but if you push too hard or pull it off to rinse, it can feel loose and I don’t trust that long term. The ones that seat tight clean well around the gumline and are easy to swap.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: Small Pill Box - 3 Compartment Travel Pill Box for Purse, Victorian Pattern Pill Container for Purse, Small Pill Case for Vitamins, Fish Oils, Capsules (Silver)
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: Small size is great. It has a very retro look, reminds me of something my grandmother had. Fits in the palm of your hand. It latches closed tightly so no worries of pills in the bottom of the purse.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review: Really pretty little pill box and easy to find in my purse. The snap lid closes tight and has not popped open on me, which is the main thing I need when I’m carrying it around. The 3 compartments make it simple to separate morning/noon/night, and it feels sturdy, not flimsy, for the size.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: NatureWorks HydroMATE Electrolytes Powder Packets Hydration Accelerator Drink Mix Rapid Party Relief Recovery Vitamin C Orange Citrus 30 Count
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: I know this increases my hydration and I am drinking it. I just don't care for the taste. Citrus is always what I choose first but this just doesn't taste good to me. Has an aftertaste that I don't care for. I have found using a half a pack makes it much more tolerable.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review: These do help me feel better and more “caught up” hydration wise than plain water, especially when I wake up feeling dried out. The Orange Citrus is the only issue for me. A full packet is too sweet and the aftertaste builds as you keep drinking it. My workable routine is half a packet in a bottle of water, then it’s fine and I will actually finish it.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: Nature Fresh Bamboo Charcoal Air Purifying Bags, Activated Charcoal Odor Eliminator, Natural Odor Absorber Freshener for Home Pets Car Closet Basement, 8 Packs (4X200 G) (2X100 G) (2X75 G)
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text:
First of all these come in a thin black plastic bag inside a plastic mailer. There are no instructions included. After doing some research I found they need to be renewed approximately once a month in sunlight for 2 hours. So first I placed one in each room. Not that I had any glaring odors. I did have a bag of yarn that I was given that has the person's perfume scent on it that gives me asthma. Put the largest bag in with the open bag. No change. Going to put the whole set in with it and see if it helps but not holding my breath. The two with buttons and attached loops are my favorites. Updated: they totally worked in the yarn bag. I can now use the yarn.

Be careful if you cut open the bag. I cut both bags.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review:
These are NOT like a spray freshener. They are just charcoal in cloth bags, so the “job” is taking the edge off odors, not making things smell nice.

I put two in my hall closet where shoes can get musty and one in the car. First day I thought no change, but after a couple days the stale smell was definitely toned down and the spaces just smelled more neutral (which I prefer, especially since strong scents can bother me).

The bags are a good size, feel well stitched, and the little loops make them easy to hang. I do wish the listing/packaging was clearer about the routine to “recharge” them in the sun, because you need that for them to keep working.

Not a miracle, but for mild-to-moderate musty odors these have been helpful.

AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.product_description: 2 Pack Essential Oil,100% Pure Organic Essential Oils for Diffuser, Aromatherapy, Massage, Soap Making - 10ML (Vetiver)
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.review_text: I find in the diffuser that 1 drop is enough of this potent essential oil.  It's strong to me and had to re set up diffuser after doing my normal 3 drops. You can always add more after you see how you like it.
AECUQNTYGADM6XKCDKWEJPDQGNVQ.best_prediction_review: Vetiver is one of my favorites and this is very earthy/woody, not sweet or perfumey. Start low because it is strong. I did 1 drop in my diffuser first, then went to 2 drops and that was plenty to fill the room fast. The dropper top makes it easy to control so you don’t overdo it.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: AVCOO 4 Channel TENS EMS Unit 24 Modes Muscle Stimulator for Pain Relief Therapy, Electronic Pulse Massager Machine with 12 Pads, Dust-Proof Bag, Fastening Cable Ties.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: First off, I have to say that I approach this type of device with skepticism. I didn't have any experience with TENS units before, but wanted to give this a try for some shoulder pain that I experience. The pads that come with the unit are very sticky, and easy to apply. I also did like the size of the unit, as it's extremely small and portable, and with a little practice, not too difficult to figure out. I must admit, my skepticism has been tempered a little bit, because I would find that after a session, my pain would be reduced. That relief lasted a short while however, as it tended to reactivate not that long after. However, I was expecting no results at all. From that perspective, I can say that there is something to this technology. I wouldn't describe it as a cure, or absolute relief, but it certainly can provide a measure of relief that for some people may qualify as invaluable. I think that the size of the unit is ideal, and makes it extremely portable. I will continue to use it in the hopes that perhaps with prolonged use, I can achieve better results. My initial experimentations with it have been more positive than I expected. From that perspective, I'll say that I was pleasantly surprised.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve tried a few Braini products before and had good results, so when I saw this “memory/focus” formula I had my 80 year old father try it for a full 30 days. He deals with the normal aging stuff (repeating himself, losing a thought mid-sentence), and with everything else he has going on medically, I wasn’t expecting a miracle here. What I was looking for was a noticeable day-to-day difference in focus and fewer “where was I?” moments.

By about the second week it was pretty clear something was changing. He was more on track in conversation and less frustrated, which for him is a big quality-of-life thing because the foggy moments tend to put him in a bad mood. I can’t claim lab-style proof (we didn’t do any baseline testing), but as the person around him the most, the change was obvious enough that I reordered.

As far as actually taking it, it’s easy to stick with. He’s already on a pill routine so adding this was straightforward, and neither of us noticed any stomach upset or weird aftertaste/burps, which is usually the first reason a supplement gets abandoned in our house.

It is on the expensive side for a 30 day bottle, but if you’re seeing a real functional payoff like we did, I think it’s worth it. If you’re expecting a “feel it in 20 minutes” stimulant effect, that wasn’t our experience—it was more of a steady improvement over the month.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Dr Pen M8 - Microneedling Pen 0.25mm Electric Auto Microneedle Derma Pen with 5 Pcs 16 Pins Cartridges
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: My mother-in-law is an esthetician, and I gave it to her for her business. She was looking for a microneedling pen, and I was able to order this one, and thought that she would be the perfect person to test it out. As it turns out, her first few clients that she used the pen on, were my wife and sister-in-law. They always get treatments from their mom for these types of things, as she obviously will not charge them. My wife really liked the effect that she got from it in combination with a hyaluronic acid treatment, as she claims it vastly improved the hydration of her skin, as well as the glowiness of it. I fiddled around with it a bit too, and thought the quality was really excellent. It doesn't feel like a cheap product at all, and watching my mother-in-law use it, seemed fairly straightforward. From that perspective, I think if you're a newbie to microneedling, the learning curve for this particular device is not terribly steep. I notice the technique is to pay attention to one area at a time, while lifting the pen to another area. Never drag, always lift. I think to help gain the most benefit from skin care products, such as serums, microneedling is something that could really accelerate your results. I might even ask my mother-in-law to give it a try on my face, as I'm in my 40s now, and could use all the help I can get, to regain a youthful look. All in all, it's a really high quality microneedling pen, and one that I would certainly recommend.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ll start by saying I approach microneedling pens with some skepticism, mainly because a lot of the “results” people report are hard to separate from just doing better skincare in general. I bought the Dr Pen M8 to keep things conservative (0.25mm only) and to use it for better product absorption and mild texture, not deep at-home resurfacing.

The pen itself feels solid in the hand and the adjustment dial has positive clicks, so I don’t feel like it’s going to drift mid-pass. I did a few short sessions over a couple of weeks on cheeks/forehead, and the 0.25mm setting was very manageable. What I could clearly verify is a short-term “plumper” look and smoother feel the next day, especially when I followed up with a simple hydrating serum. I’m not going to pretend it erased lines or scars in that timeframe, but for a mild boost it did more than I expected.

The included 16-pin cartridges worked consistently for me (no weird snagging), and the motor kept a steady pace without bogging down. It’s also easy enough to control one-handed and get an even pattern without feeling like you’re fighting the device.

If you’re buying this expecting dramatic changes quickly, I think expectations need to be realistic. But if your goal is a conservative, repeatable routine for light microneedling, this has been a good performer so far.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Evolution Advance Nutrition BCAA Formula for Men and Women – Branched-Chain Amino Acids Supplement Support for Muscle Breakdown, Muscle Protein Synthesis and Energy Boost (120 Capsules, 500 mg)
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: There was a period of time where I pretty much stopped working out. For me that's unusual because I've been working out since I was 16 years old and I'm now in my early 40s. I always like to keep in good shape and these days concentrate on achieving a good physical condition to keep my health as a priority. I took an 8 month hiatus from working out, for no particularly good reason really. Needless to say, when restarting, it's always challenging at the beginning, but I'm glad that I did. After working out for about a month, and slowly working my body into the stress of physical training, I decided to get some supplements in order to help my now 42 year old body generate muscle mass. Along with other supplements such as protein, and oftentimes a pre workout drink, I decided to take the BCAA, to help me develop muscle mass and break certain plateaus of my training. I was pretty pleased with how I was able to achieve good gains in a reasonable amount of time, and I feel very strongly that the BCAA did a good job at helping me recover and build muscle mass. I would take two before workouts, two after my workout, and two with dinner. In the month that I used it, I did notice significant gains in muscle mass, as well as shorter recovery times. It's not a miracle worker by any means, but it does give you a nice edge to help with your training. My experience with it was very positive, and so I would recommend it if you're somebody who's looking to improve their muscle mass.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I lift 4-5 days a week and wanted a basic BCAA I could stack with my usual whey without turning it into a full “pre-workout” situation. I’ve been using this Evolution Advance Nutrition BCAA for a little over 3 weeks.

My routine has been 4 capsules about 20-30 minutes before training and 2 capsules after. The capsules are easy enough to swallow and, for me, it’s a lot simpler than messing around with another flavored powder. The main thing I care about with BCAA’s is whether I can feel any kind of same-session benefit and whether it helps me keep my output up once I’m in the workout.

With these, I do notice a cleaner “push through” effect, especially on days where I’d normally start to fade halfway through, and I’ve had a little less of that next-day soreness that makes me want to shorten the next session. I’m not claiming it’s some miracle muscle-builder, but as a support piece in the routine, it’s been consistent enough that I’ve kept taking it. I also haven’t had any stomach issues from it, which is always a dealbreaker for me with amino supplements.

If you’re expecting a jacked-up stim feeling, this isn’t that. If you want something easy to take that seems to help with training endurance and recovery in a pretty straightforward way, it’s been a good fit for me so far.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: OGANA CELL Sky Blue Toner 4.4 fl.oz. (130ml) - Hyaluronic Acid & Damask Rose Water Contained Facial Soothing and Hydrating Toner, Hypoallergenic Skin Care for Sensitive Skin
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: I fully admit I am no expert in toners, but my wife tends to swear by them. I gave this toner a try, as well as asked my wife to give it a go for her opinion too. My experience and hers were very similar. What I noticed is that, I really appreciated how the toner spread very easily across the face and saturated nicely, to almost leave a more penetrable canvas for the rest of my skin care products. I have tried one or two other toners before, but with this one I noticed that it actually did more than just prep my face, it felt as though it hydrated, and smooth out my skin a little bit as well. Like I said before, it almost prepares the canvas of your face for other skin care products to saturate, and work more effectively. At least that was my experience. I have combination type skin, with an oilier t zone, and drier spots around my chin. Sometimes it's difficult for products too walk that fine line of adding positive benefits too both of those areas, being that they are so different. My experience with this Ogana Cell toner was that, it was able to walk that fine line, and not exacerbate either oiliness, or dryness. As a matter of fact for the dry areas, I found it was nicely soothing. All in all, I thought my experience was very good, and my wife would concur with that. My only complaint, was that I didn't like the bottle so much, and how in my experience it was difficult too get the right amount of toner out of it. The toner itself however, is top notch, so I would recommend giving it a try.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I have combination skin (oily T zone, dry around the chin) and I’m pretty picky with toners because a lot of them either feel like fragranced water or they leave me tight and dry. I’ve been using this OGANA CELL Sky Blue Toner for a little over 2 weeks, morning and night, right after cleansing. I either pour a little into my palm and pat it in, or put it on a cotton pad if I feel like I need a bit more wipe-off.

In terms of what it actually does, it’s been a very straightforward hydrator for me. My cheeks/chin don’t get that “tight” feeling after washing, and it layers well under a basic moisturizer without getting sticky or making my T zone feel greasier. The rose scent is there but it doesn’t hang around, and I haven’t had that stinging/tingly feeling some toners give me.

The bottle/dispensing is honestly a bigger part of the experience than I expected. It’s easy to pour too much if you’re not careful, so I ended up using it directly into my palm and just doing a couple of small drops at a time, which makes it more repeatable and less wasteful. I do wish it had a more controlled opening, but once I adjusted the way I dispense it, it’s been simple to stick into my routine.

Not a miracle product, but if you want a toner that’s mainly about consistent hydration and doesn’t leave you feeling stripped, this one has been doing that job for me so far.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Aggressive Labz Behemoth Creatine Power Plexx - Seven Elite Forms of Creatine - Promotes Power, Strength, Size and Explosiveness - 60 Day Supply - Lions Blood Flavor
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: I started lifting weights again in March 2021. I’ve been working out without taking anything as a pre workout. I just drink a protein shake after a workout. I decided to try this creatine because there are days that I feel weak and I have a hard time pushing hard for 45 minutes straight. I never thought that this would actually help. I am impressed by the energy boost I get right after I drink this. The flavor is delicious and it dilutes extremely well. I like to add a few ice cubes after I blend the powder in 8oz of water. This is perfect if you are lifting weights. I often feel fatigued on day 4 of training and this has been the key to keep me going!! I really recommend it.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve used plain creatine monohydrate for years, so I didn’t buy this expecting miracles from the “seven forms” pitch. What I wanted was a creatine I’d actually keep taking, and this one has been easy to stick with.

I’ve been doing one serving most days, usually 20-30 minutes pre-workout, and I do get a more immediate “ready to train” feel, plus a little better strength endurance on my main lifts. Nothing crazy, but noticeable.

Lions Blood flavor is sweet but not gross, and it mixes best for me in 16-20 oz of cold water (or in a blender bottle with ice). If I try to do the label-style small water amount, the aftertaste gets too strong. No stomach issues for me so far, even taking it on an emptier stomach.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Kion Colostrum | Grass-Fed Goat Milk | Gut Health and Immune Support | 30 Servings
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: I consider immunity boosting supplements a very necessary part of my daily routine. I decided to try these immunity boosters as I have never tried any that come from goat milk. I always take my supplements either first thing in the morning, or a hand full before bed. There have only been a few rare occasions where I have experienced any sort of stomach discomfort from taking supplements, but these ones do not apply to that at all. So to clarify, I did not have any sort of stomach disagreement from taking these immunity boosters.  I think we should all recognize how important keeping your immune system nice and healthy is especially in today's day and age and for obvious reasons that I don't think need repeating. Clearly if you are someone who is interested in immunity boosters, the chances are that you are thinking along the same lines as I am. I did find the product to be a little expensive or rather on the expensive side , but I also feel that it's working to operate preventatively in order to maintain good health. So from that perspective it's easy to justify the expense. As I have been taking immunity boosting supplements for a long while now, I don't really know how to quantify the results of these, but I will say that I do feel in good health and have had no issues whatsoever. All in all, I would certainly recommend giving them a try. They worked extremely well in helping to maintain my immunity health.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been curious about colostrum for gut/immune support, but I’m also pretty picky about how my stomach reacts to new supplements, so I didn’t want something that was going to cause bloating or any weird GI issues. I’ve been using this Kion grass-fed goat colostrum daily for a little over two weeks now. I’ve been doing one serving in the morning, and I’ve found it works best for me mixed into a smoothie or shaken hard in a blender bottle with cool water. If you just stir it, it can clump a bit, so the “protocol” matters if you want it to be consistent.

As far as results, I don’t want to overclaim something that’s hard to quantify in a short window, but I can say I’ve had zero negative side effects, and my stomach has been totally fine with it. That alone is a pass for me with anything geared toward gut health. On the immune side, it’s more of a preventive maintenance thing in my mind, but I’ve felt good and haven’t had any of that run-down feeling I sometimes get when life gets hectic.

The price is definitely on the premium side for a 30 serving tub, so I think it’s “worth it if it works for you,” and if you’re consistent with it. For me, the tolerability and how easy it is to keep in my routine has been enough to keep going and see how I feel over a full month.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: PUREMEDICS Bone Strength - Bone Health Supplement with MCHA to Support Bone and Teeth Strength - Formulated by Doctors - Pure & Hypoallergenic - 3rd Party Certified - 120 Capsules
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: I think that supplementing for bone health is very important, especially for women as they age. The prevalence of osteoporosis is very high amongst aging women and men. Oftentimes your doctor will recommend taking calcium, but I find that calcium on it's own can be overdone in some instances. I do like that this supplement contains vitamin K, because vitamin K is known to help in calcium absorbency. I found the pills to be on the large side, but I didn't have any difficulty swallowing them. I will say that taking them 4 times a day is a little cumbersome, and not really something that I would continue to do. What I normally do when it comes to supplementation, is cut whatever the recommendation is that I'm giving in half. So in my case, I decided to do one pill twice a day. I took one before breakfast and one before dinner. It was recommended to take these with a meal, and as I normally take the rest of my supplements without a meal I decided to take their advice, and take this one with food. That being said, I did notice a tinge of an after taste. I wouldn't say that it was terribly pleasant, but at the same time I guess because I ate after having taken it, it certainly lessen the effects. I never tried it without a meal, but if that's what I could taste with a meal, I don't think I'd recommend taking this pill on its own. All in all, it's a good supplement to include as far as increasing your bone density perhaps through supplementation. The price seems very reasonable to me, and I think it's worth a recommendation. Of course I have to mention like I always do when I do any reviews about supplements, make sure you ask your doctor first.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I picked up PUREMEDICS Bone Strength because I wanted a bone support supplement that felt “smart” in how it was put together, not just calcium for the sake of calcium. The MCHA angle made sense to me on paper, and I also liked the hypoallergenic/3rd party certified positioning since this is something you’re taking repeatedly and you don’t want it turning into a daily gamble.

As far as actually taking it, the capsules are very manageable and easy to swallow, and I’ve had no stomach irritation from them. That’s honestly my first gate with anything like this, because if I can’t tolerate it or it’s a pain to get down, it doesn’t matter how good the label looks. I’ve been taking it with a meal and that’s been the most “set it and forget it” way to keep it consistent.

In terms of results, this isn’t the type of supplement where I expect an obvious “I feel it” effect, and I’m not going to pretend I can measure bone density from day to day. For me this is more of a preventive maintenance purchase, and the fact that I can take it without any weird aftertaste or digestive issues is the biggest sign it will actually stay in my routine long enough to matter.

The only real negative is value math: 120 capsules sounds like a lot until you line it up with the serving size, and it can get pricey if you’re following the label long term. If the price works for you and you want an easy-to-stick-with bone/teeth support option that doesn’t cause side effects, I think it’s a solid choice.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Vitamin D3 5000iu Tablets, Super-Strong Immune System Boost. Great Value with 180 Tablets (6 Months Supply)
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: I've been supplementing like crazy quite a bit over the last little while for obvious reasons. One of the most important supplements that I take is vitamin D, and D3 is certainly a part of that routine. 5000iu is definitely an excellent amount to be taking on a daily basis. These tablets are small and easy to swallow I have had absolutely no irritation or side effects from taking them. I think it's very important especially nowadays, to consider supplementation as part of your daily routine, and taking vitamin D is definitely going to be at the top of that list. That's even more true, if you live somewhere where Winter tends to take away the sunlight. With 5000 IU's per tablet, and 180 tablets  per bottle, you're good for 6 months with this bottle.  I think it's an expense that's well worth it, and very reasonably priced in my opinion. I know that for my intentions, and certainly wanting to stay healthy, vitamin D is going to remain as part of my routine. I highly recommend people decide to take vitamin D for themselves, especially if you live up north. I actually happen to live in the tropical South, but I still take vitamin D daily. So for the obvious health benefits, and the mood elevating properties, I would highly recommend taking this Vitamin D supplement.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I started taking these Vitamin D3 5000iu tablets because my labs tend to run low if I’m not supplementing, and I wanted something simple that would last a while. The bottle is a solid value at 180 tablets, and the tablets themselves are small enough that I can swallow them without that “stuck in the throat” feeling that some bigger vitamins give me.

As far as results, I’m not expecting a dramatic “immune boost” feeling, but after about a month of daily use I do feel like my usual winter sluggishness is a bit better. Obviously that’s subjective, and I still plan to confirm with bloodwork, but at minimum I didn’t have any stomach upset or weird side effects, which is always my main concern with higher-dose D3.

The only thing I wish the listing was clearer on is whether there’s any third-party testing or certification, because that matters to me with supplements. Still, for the price per dose and ease of taking it daily, I’d buy it again.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Ever Collagen in&UP Plus, Low Molecular Collagen Peptides Tablets with Vitamin Supplements, - Skin Health for Women - Vitamin C, Vitamin D, Selenium
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: My wife loves to spend an afternoon giving herself a manicure, using those gel UV light kits. As a result, they may look great for a long time, however her nails have been taking the brunt of that manicure lifestyle, and become very brittle and weak. I have gotten her powder collagen before, and she seems not to mind it by mixing it into her coffee, but oftentimes forgets it as well because it's not like it adds flavor that is pleasant enough for her to remember to always take it. She and I do take vitamins, and have some medications as well, so taking collagen in a pill form is a lot easier for us to remember, because we just put it in our weekly pill container. After taking the 28 day supply, my wife did let me know that she was happy with how her nails had began to recover. The strength of her nails had improved quite a bit, as they were almost paper thin at one point. I remember her constantly complaining that she had just broken a nail, but the collagen did a good job of strengthening her nails. This also may be anecdotal, but she did notice an improvement in her skin as well. All in all, I would say that they do work, and taking it in pill form as opposed to a powder that you mix into your drink of choice, is a superior method of delivery, in my opinion. My wife liked it a lot, and I think she's going to make me buy some more. It's also worth mentioning that there are some vitamin added benefits to this collagen pill as well, such as vitamin C & D which are necessary and imperative for improved immune system health. It is rather expensive for a 28 day supply, but perhaps worth it if you're looking to regenerate some damaged nails or skin.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been taking collagen consistently for a while and usually prefer powders, but I wanted tablets that fit a routine without having to mix anything. I used these daily for a little over 3 weeks, and the “real world” test for me wasn’t vague skin claims — it was brittle nails (I do UV gel manicures periodically and they can leave my nails pretty weak after removal).

The main thing I noticed by around the 2–3 week mark was that my nails seemed a bit less prone to splitting and peeling when they’d normally be at their worst. I’m not going to pretend I can measure “skin elasticity” in that time frame, but nails are a pretty binary pass/fail for me, and I did see some improvement there.

As far as the tablets themselves, they went down easily and didn’t give me any stomach upset or weird aftertaste, which matters because if a collagen product is fishy or unpleasant I just won’t stick with it.

Ingredient-wise, I like that it’s not just collagen alone, and includes things like vitamin C and D and selenium, since that’s closer to how I’d build a supplement “stack” anyway. That said, I do wish the listing/label made the actual collagen dose per day and the vitamin amounts a little more obvious at a glance, because that’s what determines whether this can replace other bottles or is just an add-on.

Price-wise, it’s not the cheapest way to do collagen compared to a bulk powder, but if you’re specifically paying for the convenience of tablets and you actually take them daily, it’s a reasonable trade. For me it’s an experiment-in-progress; I’m going to finish the bottle and see if the nail improvement holds before I commit to reordering.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Running Athletic Shoe Insoles All-Day Shock Absorption High Arch Support That Relieve Flat Feet Plantar Fasciitis, Orthotic Inserts Designed to Prevent Foot Pain for Work and Sports Men's 9.5-10
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: I always put insoles into all of my shoes, and was in search of another pair for some work boots that I like to wear in the winter time. Beyond the arch support and the extra comfort level, I do like the fact that it allows for more of a snug fit with larger boots. I was very pleased with these, and found that they slipped pretty easily into my boots, and didn't require any trimming. I would say that they're pretty true to size, if not perhaps a little on the small side of things. For me that's not really an issue in my case, as I sort of lucked out ordering a half size larger being that I take a 10 and a half. So the 11 fit properly into my shoe without any adjustment. I found them to be quite comfortable for prolonged use, and like I mentioned I put them in a pair of work boots which made those boots far more comfortable to wear all day long. If you're somebody who needs strong arch support, I would say that these are not your best option, however they do provide some level of support that was adequate for my needs. I found the cushioning to be quite comfortable, and provides relief to the bottom of my feet for long periods of time. All in all, I'm very pleased with them and happy to have them in my work boots.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I bought these to see if I could get a bit more support in my everyday shoes without committing to custom orthotics. I’m a men’s 10 and ordered the 9.5-10, and the sizing was pretty much spot on. They dropped into my running shoes and my work sneakers without a lot of fuss, and I didn’t have to do any major trimming to make them sit flat.

In use, the main win for me is the general cushioning and how they stabilize my foot when I’m on my feet for a long stretch. They take the edge off that “beat up” feeling after a long day, and they feel more supportive than the thin factory insoles most shoes come with. That said, if you’re expecting an aggressive, rigid arch that forces your foot into position, these aren’t that. The arch support is present, but it’s more of a moderate, all-day support than a hard corrective insert.

So far they’ve held their shape and haven’t started curling up in the heel or sliding around, which is usually my first failure mode with cheaper inserts. If you need something you can swap between shoes easily and you want better comfort without going overboard on stiffness, these have been a solid upgrade for me.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Carlyle Plant-Based Protein: Power your body with natureâs best source of fuel, Carlyle Plant-Based Protein. This vegan protein powder is made with a superb blend of 30 plus superfoods featuring a medley of seeds, sprouts, fruits, and greens. Each serving provides 30g of protein and 15g of fiber with numerous essential vitamins and minerals to help you thrive on a vegetarian diet. Shake, Bake, or Blend! Natural chocolate flavor powder can be enjoyed in many ways- Add to almond milk, coconut milk or any beverage for a nutritious shake, blend with your favorite smoothies, or use while baking. This plant-based formula is ideal for anyone looking to increase their protein intake. Rooted in Wellness: Our expertise in sourcing the purest ingredients combined with advanced technologies makes us proud to deliver premium, honest supplements at the best value. Carlyle Plant Based Protein is a testament to our commitment to excellence, made with powerful ingredients rooted in wellness. Thanks for choosing us in your pursuit of living healthy. WARNING: Consuming this product can expose you to chemicals including lead, which is known to the State of California to cause birth defects or other reproductive harm. For more information go to www.P65Warnings.ca.gov/food.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: What I like about this particular brand of protein powder, is that it can operate as more than just a post workout recovery drink, and do double duty as a meal replacement as well. A couple of years back, I had some surgical procedures in my mouth as a result of some receding gum lines, that required gum grafts. That really is as unpleasant as it sounds. I mentioned that, because at the time it was difficult to eat solid foods for a while, so I was more or less on a liquid diet. I would very much like to have had this protein at that time, as a meal replacement because I feel it would have done a very good job of offering a good overall nutritional value. In this case, I have been using it as a post workout recovery drink. For that purpose, it works very well too. I do fairly high impact resistance training, so by the end of it I'm spent. My muscles certainly benefit from post workout recovery drinks that are protein rich, and will encourage muscle regeneration and synthesis. I found that this protein did nicely at helping me recover from my workouts. I have had the opportunity of trying the vanilla flavored one as well, and I think I actually prefer that one to the chocolate. My wife on the other hand, very much prefers the chocolate. So, I guess you can see that it's obviously going to be subjective. I personally found the chocolate to be a little sweet for my taste. So it's no surprise that my wife liked it, because she has quite the sweet tooth. I did find it to have just a touch of a grainy texture, but certainly nowhere near as bad as others that I have tried. I use a ninja blender that has a smoothie attachment, and that thing does a pretty incredible job of pulverizing the powder. I make my shakes very simply by just adding ice and water, with a couple scoops of this protein powder. As far as the ratios of how much water to powder I use, I usually experiment with that and don't pay much attention to the recommendations by the manufacturer. Personally, I prefer to dilute it a little bit in more water, as I find that makes for a more pleasant drink. All in all, I do like the fact that it has a good nutritional balance of fibers, vitamins, and minerals, as well as the protein content. Like I mentioned, when I had my surgery, it would have been nice to have had this as an option as well as the Ensure drinks that I was always having. I think it's a fine option, and I would recommend it.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been trying to bump up my protein intake without relying on whey all the time (my stomach doesn’t always love it), so I gave this Carlyle plant-based protein a fair test. I used it mainly post-workout, but I also wanted to see if it could realistically function as a “liquid meal” for a short stretch when chewing a full meal wasn’t happening (dental work). For that specific job, the 30g protein / 15g fiber per serving is the part that matters, and it did feel like an actual replacement shake instead of just flavored protein water.

Taste is always the make-or-break with plant blends, and this one lands in the “drinkable chocolate” category, not dessert. It does have that earthy/greens undertone you’d expect from a superfoods-heavy mix, but it’s not offensive, and I didn’t find it crazy sweet. My wife tends to like sweeter shakes than I do, and even she was fine with it once it was blended properly.

The biggest practical note is that the mix thickness depends a lot on your liquid ratio. The directions can make it come out too thick for my liking, especially if you’re trying to use it as a meal-style shake. I use a Ninja with the smoothie attachment and I end up closer to 16–20 oz of water/almond milk per serving to keep it smooth. In a shaker cup it’s doable, but it’s noticeably easier to get consistent (and less gritty) with a blender.

Overall I’m pleased with it for what it actually is: a higher-fiber plant protein that can double as a temporary meal stand-in when you need it, and an easy post-workout option when you don’t want whey.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Carlyle Plant-Based Protein: Power your body with natureâs best source of fuel, Carlyle Plant-Based Protein. This vegan protein powder is made with a superb blend of 30 plus superfoods featuring a medley of seeds, sprouts, fruits, and greens. For more information go to www.P65Warnings.ca.gov/food.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: I have to say that I was fairly impressed with the quality of this protein powder. I find the ingredients to be very impressive. I do like protein powders to be nicely flavored, but I'm always very skeptical of ones that are too tasty. If they taste more like Hershey shake for example, versus a chocolate protein shake, then you know they're heavily laden with sugar, and other additives that bring down the quality of the protein powder itself. This vanilla flavor one I found very nicely flavored. It wasn't too sweet at all. I'm not too sure that I like the recommendations on how much powder to add to how much water however. 10 to 12 ounces of water with two scoops of this protein powder, is in my opinion not enough water. I use a ninja blender, that has a smoothie attachment, and so I just measured water by eye, more or less, but I could tell you for sure I put probably closer to 16 to 20 ounces of water. I just like to water it down a bit, as I found for my personal taste, it was more pleasant that way and went down smoother. The taste is subtle and nice, especially when diluted, like I did. I use it as a post workout drink, to help my muscles recover from resistance training that I do five to six times a week. I found it very adequate as far as a post workout recovery drink, and on par with other whey proteins that I would routinely use. I mean, you have to take that with a bit of a grain of salt, because it's hard to anecdotally quantify the results in comparison, but I can say that I hardly notice the difference between this and your name brand whey proteins. I didn't try it for cooking or anything like that, but I know that these powders can be used in many different recipes for baking protein rich snacks. All in all, I found that it was a nice quality protein, that certainly fit the bill, and did the job for me and my needs. I found it very easy to take down, and light on my stomach. For all these reasons, I would certainly recommend it.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been trying to work more plant-based protein into my routine, and I picked this up because the label is pretty aggressive on paper (30g protein + 15g fiber per serving) and I wanted to see what the “real unit” is in day to day use: does it mix easily enough that I’ll actually take it, and is the taste something I can repeat without dreading it.

The biggest make-or-break for me with powders is always how they drink. Mixed in a shaker, it does fine as long as you don’t follow the instinct to make it super thick. If I use more water than I normally would with whey, it becomes very manageable and doesn’t turn into that gritty sludge situation. The Natural Vanilla is not dessert-level sweet, which I personally consider a good sign with these “superfood blend” products (too sweet usually reads like it’s covering something up). I wouldn’t call it amazing tasting, but it’s easy enough to get down consistently, and that’s what matters to me.

Performance-wise, I’m not going to pretend I can quantify muscle recovery from a week of use, but I can say it feels on par with what I expect from a solid protein powder in my post-workout routine, and it’s filling enough that it can pinch-hit when I’m not getting a real meal in right away. The fiber is also something you’ll notice if you’re not used to that much in one shot, so for some people it may be smarter to start with a smaller amount and see how your stomach does.

One thing worth noting is the Proposition 65 warning about lead exposure. That’s not something I’m going to hand-wave, and it may be a hard no for some buyers. For me, it’s a reminder to keep this in the “supplement a diet” lane rather than thinking more is always better.

Overall, it’s a workable plant-based option if your priority is a repeatable shake that isn’t overly sweet and doesn’t require a full smoothie production every time.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Blackhead Remover Pore Vacuum,Upgrated Water Cycle Cleaning Facial Nose Black Head Removal,Electric USB Rechargeable Acne Comedone Whitehead Extractor Tools with 6 Suction Heads for Women & Men
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: This thing is powerful. I started with the lowest setting and it was strong enough to remove yucky things of my nose. I did use a face steamer to open up my pores. I thought this would be more effective. I always do this when I want to remove blackheads. Even if I use strips. And this was my first time trying something like this. And I was amazed by how powerful this thing is. It removed all of my blackheads easily. I did my nose and my chin area. I’ve never experienced something like this. I’m glad I gave it a chance. Now this is what I’ll be using from now on. And the price is just right. It’s easy to clean. If you want some heavy duty work this is the product for you I did have some redness after using it so if you have sensitive skin extra careful. My redness went away by the end of the day.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I was pretty skeptical of pore vacuums because I’ve seen people leave bruises on their nose/cheeks, but I wanted something less aggressive than squeezing. This one has legit suction, so the technique matters. I did a quick steam/warm shower first, used the smallest head around the sides of my nose, kept it moving, and stayed on the lowest setting. Used that way, it actually pulled out some gunk/whiteheads and left my nose looking cleaner right after.

If you crank the power or park it in one spot, you’re basically asking for redness (I tested one small spot for a second too long and it was pink for an hour). The “water cycle cleaning” feature is helpful for keeping the suction path from feeling gross, but I still rinsed the heads well after.

Not a miracle, but it does work if you respect the suction and treat it like a tool, not a shortcut.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: Braini, Capsules and 30 Day Challenge, Clinically Tested Brain Supplement, Stimulant & Caffeine Free Energy, Supports Mental Focus, Improves Brain Age Performance, 30 Servings
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: I had reviewed Braini capsules before. My experience with their products was very positive. When it came to the capsules, I wanted my 80 year old father to try them. He unfortunately, has been going through several health issues, but was also experiencing a little bit of fogginess in the head when it comes to memory, and issues of repeating himself. My father is still very much lucid, but struggles from time to time with remembering details, as well as short term memory issues. I actually had him try Braini for 30 days, and he had experienced some very promising improvements. His memory, as well as his focus had certainly improved. For him, that was something that also translated into an improved mood. That's because his frustrations from forgetting things, could often lead to aggravation. When I saw that there was another opportunity to order more capsules from Braini, I jumped at the opportunity, because I wanted him to continue taking it for longer than just 30 days. He continued to take Braini, and saw an even greater improvement in his abilities for recall, as well as communicating his thoughts without having to search for ideas. Basically, he did notice a positive impact to his cognitive ability, more so after continuing then even during his first 30 day trial. I personally, I'm very pleased with these results, and I know for him, who is going through health issues, something that can improve his cognitive abilities has increased his quality of life. For this, I am thrilled about it. I know he's happy with it too, because he says it to me directly. Based on his and my own experience with brainy products, I do highly recommend them.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve taken a few different “brain” supplements over the years, and I always go into anything claiming “brain age” improvement with some skepticism. I did the full 30 day run of Braini as directed (2 capsules a day, I took them in the morning with breakfast), and the main thing I was watching for was whether it actually helped focus/mental stamina without leaning on caffeine or giving me that wired feeling.

For me, it did what it said on the stimulant side — no jitters, no racing heart, and it didn’t mess with my sleep even on days I took it a little later than normal. No stomach issues either, which is a big pass/fail point for me because a lot of supplements start to disagree with you if you take them daily. Effect-wise, it wasn’t a dramatic “limitless” moment, but I did notice I was more consistent with attention in the afternoons and a bit less mentally foggy on days where I’m bouncing between tasks. That’s subjective (I didn’t do any formal testing), but it was noticeable enough over the month that I’d call it a real benefit.

If you’re expecting an instant hit like coffee, this isn’t that, but if your goal is support without stimulants, this one fit well into a normal routine and I felt better cognitively by the end of the bottle. I’d keep using it if the price makes sense for your budget.

AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.product_description: BrainiLex | Clinically Tested Brain Supplement | Memory, Focus, Learn, Perform | 90 Count
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.review_text: I have tried several products from Braini already myself personally, and have found them to be very good as far as the results that they provide. I have been able to improve my cognitive function, focus and attention, as well as my memory over the timeframe that I took it. With this particular supplement, I was actually interested in having my father test it. My father is now 80 years old, and oftentimes repeats himself, and has trouble with his memory. This of course is a factor of aging, so I wanted to see if Braini would be able to help him out with this problem that frustrates him. My father is certainly no stranger to taking pills, as he does so for his heart condition, diabetes, and as of late, cancer. Certainly, he has been going through some difficult health issues, so I thought perhaps he might be pleased by improved cognitive function. After taking the entirety of the bottle, and speaking with him regularly throughout, I noticed as well as he did, that his memory had actually improved a fair bit. He was a lot less likely to repeat himself, though he still did repeat himself from time to time. I would say though, that the frequency of him forgetting things, had been lessened. I hesitate to say anything definitively, because this isn't some sort of controlled clinical test. My experience here, and my father's experience, are entirely anecdotal, but that being considered, it was a positive experience. I think I would consider reordering this, and having my father continue it, to see whether or not his brain function could improve even further, or at the very least maintain the improvement that he already achieved. I know that for him personally, with all the frustrations that he has been facing lately with his health issues, the fact that we can speak without him having to repeat himself as much, or forget what it was that he was going to say, has been a great relief for him. For this reason, I do recommend giving it a try.
AE5ESL52LWWBJTSFOAXSFZA3XCGQ.best_prediction_review:
I’m always a little skeptical with “brain” supplements because the marketing usually gets ahead of what you can actually prove day to day, but I wanted to give BrainiLex a fair shot and keep it as clean of a test as I could. I had my 80 year old father take it daily for about a month, because with him it’s easier to judge functional changes (less repeating himself, staying on track in a conversation) than it is with me trying to convince myself I’m “sharper” on a random Tuesday. This obviously isn’t a controlled study, but we kept his routine the same and didn’t change caffeine or sleep patterns on purpose.

What I noticed with him was a modest but real improvement in focus and short term recall, and it also seemed to help his mood a bit because he wasn’t getting as frustrated when he’d lose his train of thought. It wasn’t a night-and-day transformation, and I wouldn’t frame it as a cure for anything, but it did pass my basic “is this doing something repeatable?” gate enough that I’m comfortable continuing it for a longer run.

Just as important for me with something like this, he tolerated it well. No stomach issues, no weird jittery feeling, and no headaches that we could attribute to it, which is usually the first reason I stop a supplement even if it has potential. If you’re expecting an instant, unmistakable effect you might be disappointed, but if your goal is a gentle nudge in memory/focus without side effects, this one has been a solid option in our household so far.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.product_description: 1875 Watt Negative Ionic Hair Dryer, ENISECA Precise Wind Heat/Speed Setting Tech Professional Salon Blow Dryer Fast Drying Low Noise with Powerful AC Motor and Diffuser Comb for Women Men, Grey
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
I have fine, dry, flat hair that gets frizzy at the ends. I blow dry with a large round brush and need to switch back and forth between warm and cool settings often to style my hair. I wrap my hair around the brush barrel, blow it with heat and then use a cool setting to lock in shape. I’m constantly switching between and heat and cool settings. But I haven’t found a practical way to do my routine with this Eniseca hair dryer. This hair dryer makes a good initial impression based just on looks in that it’s lightweight, nice looking in black with rose gold, compact, digital and comes nicely boxed with accessories. But I’ve tinkered around with it for days and can’t find a workable way to toggle back and forth between my desired warm and cool settings. I haven’t been able to properly style my hair once with this dryer.

To explain, the heat setting comes on at 50 C, but I need it at about 76 C (max is 99 C). I have to press the plus button 26 times to get my desired heat level. After drying a section, I need a cool burst of air so I switch to cool air, but it defaults to 99 wind power. That’s way too strong, so I have to press the minus button 20 times or so to get a reduced usable air flow. It takes more time to adjust to the cool air flow than to actually use it. The worst part is that I then need to switch to heat again for the next hair section, but I can’t find a way to toggle back to 76 C as the heat button seems to default back to 50 C. So then I have to press the plus button 26 times again to get the right heat setting and that tedious cycle just continues. This is not a workable product for me. If there actually is a way to toggle between desired temperature and air flow settings and not be resetting constantly, then it’s far too complicated for me to figure out. I don’t like it. 2 stars.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.best_prediction_review:
I have fine hair that frizzes easily, and I also have a teen with thicker hair, so I tried this ENISECA 1875W dryer on both of us. On basic drying power it’s legitimately fast, especially if I towel blot first, and the airflow on high moves a lot of air without feeling like it’s overheating my scalp. It also doesn’t have that harsh “hot plastic” smell some cheaper dryers have out of the box.

Where this one falls short for me is the precision/control side for actual styling. I rely on being able to hit a true cool shot quickly to set a blowout and to stop heat on my fine hair, and the “cool” here isn’t cold enough to function as a real cool-down for me. That makes it hard to lock in lift and smoothness, and it also means I’m more cautious about how long I keep it on one section.

The switch layout is simple enough, but in real use I’m constantly toggling between airflow and temperature, and I found myself wishing the settings were more distinct and easier to repeat by feel without looking. It’s convenient that it comes with attachments (diffuser/comb) and the dryer is light enough that my arm isn’t tired, but since the cool setting is a gatekeeper feature for my routine, I can’t call this a “salon” replacement even though it dries quickly.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.product_description: Kinga Hair Dryer Brush Blow Dryer Brush in One, One Step Hair Dryer Styler & Volumizer
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
I have fine hair that lacks body, but still gets frizzy. I blow my hair out and use a round brush or use a blow dryer brush to add style and body and also smooth out the frizz. This Kinga Blow Dryer Brush isn’t the first blow dryer brush I’ve used and like others it’s a low wattage dryer (only 1000 watts) that dries and styles better after I let my hair partially air dry so it’s just damp rather than wet when I use the dryer. It dries and adds style from a combo of hot air and the hot air heating up the inner barrel.

This dryer has a large oval shaped brush with stiff plastic bristles that grab my fine hair well enough that I can get the tension I need to style my hair. But I still don’t get smooth hair and can’t lock in style and body as the unit lacks a cool air setting. The lowest setting before “off” blows warm air, so I’m not getting the quick cool down I need and my hair continues to get heat from the hot barrel and warm air. In addition to that creating styling issues, I’m concerned my fragile hair may get heat damage as the barrel ends up staying hot too long.

The sliding control does turn the unit off and works as described with low, medium and high settings. But I feel it misses the mark as the low setting should be a cool not warm, and the medium setting feels more like a low setting with weak air flow. I need the high setting as my hair is so resistant to holding shape and body, but I’d normally toggle between high and a cool setting for hold and to prevents damage but this lacks that option. That feature is a deal breaker for me, so I can’t fully recommend this dryer. The dryer may be fine for those who don’t use a cool setting. I can’t gauge durability at this time. 3 stars.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.best_prediction_review:
I have fine, dry hair that frizzes easily and I usually blow dry with a round brush, so I was hoping this Kinga dryer brush would simplify my routine. It does give me a quick, smooth finish on the top layer and around my face, and it definitely adds some volume at the roots compared to air drying. For a fast “presentable” blowout it can be ok, but it’s not quite as controlled as a separate dryer + brush.

The main issue for me is the workflow. My normal routine depends on being able to go heat → cool repeatedly to set the shape, and I can’t get a practical, repeatable warm/cool toggle here. The settings take too much fiddling, and it tends to fall back into the default behavior so I’m not confident I’m actually in the mode I think I’m in while I’m working around my face.

Power-wise, it’s also not a true wet-to-dry tool for me. If my hair is more than lightly damp, it turns into a long exposure to heat without the payoff, so I end up pre-air-drying anyway—which defeats the “one step” promise.

Because my hair is fragile, the lack of an easy cool-down/finish step feels like a real damage risk if you try to muscle through a full dry with it. If you’re only smoothing and lifting at the roots on already-damp hair it’s usable, but I wouldn’t rely on this as my primary dryer/styler the way the listing implies.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
These PackQueen 6”x6”x4” square champagne gold boxes are attractive all occasion boxes. These are practical as they ship and store flat and are quick and easy to assemble. These aren’t made of heavy duty cardboard, but the materials are more than adequate for lighter weight gifts and they make a good impression in the gold color with a crisp white interior. They hold their form best when I take a moment to bend the creases before assembly.

The bottoms are formed by interlocking flaps that fit together well and lay fairly flat, but these aren’t heavy duty, strong boxes. While not necessarily needed, I do like to add a little tape inside to reinforce the bottoms. It doesn’t show, and the bottoms feels more sturdy. But I have used a couple without tape, and they haven’t collapsed as they feel more secure once I add tissue or filling with a gift and tie a ribbon around the box or wrap it.

These boxes are perfect for lightweight items like cosmetics, candles, a special ornament, small clothing items such as slipper socks, lingerie or a tee, and small accessories like gloves or a scarf. These are practical and pretty when finished off with a nice ribbon or bow. I find it convenient to already have these on hand as the need for a box arises, and the champagne gold color looks elegant. These make a good impression and exceed my expectations at the current price of $18.99 for 30 boxes ($.64 a box). Recommended. 5 stars.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.best_prediction_review:
These gift boxes are a solid, practical option if you want something that looks nicer than a bag but you don’t want to fight with flimsy cardboard. They arrived flat and I was able to fold them into shape quickly. I always pre-bend the creases before I fully assemble, and with that simple step the corners lined up cleanly and the lids sat on straight without popping back open.

The board is thick enough that the boxes don’t feel like they’re going to collapse the second you pick them up, but I’d still keep expectations realistic: I used them for lightweight self-care items for my teen (sheet masks, lip balm, a small candle) and they held their shape well. If you’re planning to load something dense/heavy, I’d reinforce the bottom with a bit of tape so the box stays rigid when it’s carried.

For the price, the set feels worth it because they’re reusable if you open them carefully, and they save time versus wrapping odd-shaped items. Overall, these do the job as long as you treat them as “nice presentation for light-to-medium gifts,” not a heavy-duty shipping box.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
These Packqueen gift bags are attractive and good quality bags that are super versatile given the design and generous sizing that can fit bigger and odd shaped items. The bags are crisp white with shiny gold foil stars and have long white ribbon handles. The shiny star pattern makes these perfect for an array of occasions such as graduations, birthdays, anniversaries, Father’s or Mother’s Day, Christmas, New Year’s, Valentine’s Day, housewarming and more.

These large bags have a horizontal orientation (more width than height) that makes them great for gifting items such as shoes, clothing items like robes or sweat outfits, home goods and gourmet food, a kitchen tool set with mitts, small appliances, toys, books, blow dryers and all kinds of things. These measure about 10“ high, 5“ deep and 13“ wide. The paper is thick and sturdy, and the bottoms are reinforced with a thin piece of white cardboard glued inside.

The bags have clean, fresh look. They aren’t super premium gift bags like some I’ve seen adorned with faux gems, plastic handles and so on. But I’d classify these as high quality, premium bags that are plenty nice for special occasions. No tissue is included, and I personally think these look fabulous with patterned tissue with shiny dots or stripes, but they also look great with plain tissue in a range of colors such as navy, red, pink and so much more. I’m super pleased with the quality and design. Recommended. 5 stars.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.best_prediction_review:
These are attractive, practical gift bags to keep on hand for last-minute gifts (including for my teen’s friends). They open up easily and hold their shape while you’re trying to get an item in, which is the main “workflow” issue I run into with cheaper bags that collapse and fight you.

The paper feels like a decent weight and the handles feel securely attached. I used one for a medium candle (in a glass jar) and another for a small boxed skincare set, and both carried fine without the bottom getting soggy/warped or the sides bowing out. They’re more useful than the tiny favor-bag size, but they’re also not giant shopping bags—think small-to-medium gifts.

One thing to note: these are just bags. Mine didn’t come with tissue paper or separate gift tags, so plan on having those on hand if you need the “ready to give” setup.

For the per-bag cost, the sturdiness and ease of loading make them worth keeping in a drawer so you’re not making an emergency store run.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.product_description: Spirit Gift Bags 4 Pack Black, White and Gold. Perfect for Any Celebration by Paper Riot
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
These four wine and spirit gift bags are attractive and look festive for the holidays and especially New Years. The black, gold and/or white dot and stripe themed patterns also suit a variety of other occasions, such as a housewarming, birthday, anniversary or other special occasion. The black, gold and white color combinations look sophisticated and modern.

The paper is good quality and each bag feels sturdy enough to support a glass bottle. The bags have strong black or white ribbon handles. Each bag has an attached matching gold tag that looks nice and is an added convenience, but tissue isn’t included. These are upscale looking, good quality gift bags that exceed my expectations for $7.99 for a pack of four bags. 5 stars.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.best_prediction_review:
These Paper Riot spirit gift bags are a nice, neutral set for celebrations. The black/white/gold design works for teen gifts and more “adult” occasions, and having 4 in a pack is convenient so I’m not scrambling last minute.

The paper is a decent weight and the handles feel securely attached. They did fine with a medium sized gift and also a taller item (a glass bottle) with tissue, and the bottom insert kept the base from bowing. I still wouldn’t overload them with anything very heavy, but for typical gifting they feel sturdier than a lot of cheap party-store bags.

For the price per bag, the quality is good and the included tags make them actually ready to use.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.product_description: zelaxy Mask Strap Extenders, Soft Silicone Mask Holders for Relieve Ear Pain Caused by Mask (Thumb)
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
I’ve tried so many brands of masks that loop around my ears trying to find a good fit, but most masks just pop right off my ears and fall off. I don’t know if it’s the shape of my ears, head size or what the issue Is, but a mask extender seemed like a potentially good solution to keep a mask secured in place. Unfortunately, these silicone extenders seem like a poor design that doesn’t help secure a mask in place.

These extenders are only 5.5” long, and each extender has three notches on each side. The idea is to seat the ear loops into the notches to keep the mask on. But the extenders are short and flimsy, and the notches are shallow. The loops don’t securely fit down into the notches in a way where they don’t just separate from the extender. I’ve tried many times and find no way to hook the loops to the notches on the extender so the mask would stay in place. The extender just falls off, and so does the mask.

I’ve tried using these extenders with various brand of masks with stretchy loops, but they don’t work with any of my masks. I don’t know if the extenders could work for a kid or teen or someone with a small head, but I still don’t see how the design of the notches can actually keep the mask loops securely in place. The extenders seem like they need a clip or a way to tie a loop to it or something to secure the loops besides the notches. The variety of colors is nice and I like the idea, but these extenders don’t work for me. 2 stars.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.best_prediction_review:
I bought these “thumb” style silicone mask strap extenders for my teen and me to get the ear loops off our ears during longer wears. The silicone itself seems fine (soft, flexible, no weird odor), and the idea is straightforward: hook the mask loops onto the little nubs and rest it behind your head.

In practice, I can’t get consistent holding with the masks we actually use. On a couple of standard disposable masks with thinner, rounder ear loops, the loops either pop off the nubs when we move/talk, or they slowly walk off as tension shifts. On cloth masks with wider/flat elastic, the elastic doesn’t seat cleanly into the notch and seems to ride up and slip off even faster. The nub geometry just doesn’t have much of a “capture” lip, so any twist in the strap tends to lever it off.

Because it won’t reliably stay hooked, it also ends up being fiddly to put on and adjust, and I don’t trust it for anything where I need the mask tension to stay stable (quick errands are fine, but not a long wear). We tried a few different hook positions and tension levels and got the same basic failure across masks.

If your mask loops are very thin and super stretchy, maybe it works, but for the common disposable and cloth styles we have, this feels like a good concept with a retention design that isn’t robust enough.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.product_description: Bare Performance Nutrition, Field Bar, High Protein Bar, Nutrition Bar, Made with Whole Ingredients, Naturally Sweetened, Peanut Butter (12 Bars, Variety Pack)
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
﻿The variety box has Chocolate Cereal Crunch and Chocolate Peanut Butter Blondie bars. The taste of the Crunch bar is pretty good. It’s a darker brown peanut butter bar with a texture that’s kind of dry with a little crunch from crisp rice. It has a sweetened peanut butter taste with a hint of a chocolate from cocoa. I prefer it to the Blondie bar.

The Blondie bar is a lighter color peanut butter bar with chocolate chips and shredded coconut. The texture is a lot like the Crunch bar but it’s altered by so much shredded coconut (instead of the crisp rice). I like the chocolate chips and peanut taste, but I don’t enjoy the taste or texture of the shredded coconut (just my personal preference). The bars are satiating and filling.

But I’m unimpressed with some ingredients, such as the hydrogenated oils, mono and diglycerides, soy lecithin and caramel color in the Crunch bar and propylene glycol, cellulose gel, soy lecithin and imitation vanilla extract in the Blondie. And I can’t tell from the ingredient list what type (e.g., soy, whey, etc.) of “protein isolate” is in these bars.

I really don’t like that the Blondie bar has sodium metabisulfite. It can cause gastro issues, coughing, wheezing and even shock and life threatening reactions in those with sulfite sensitivity. I just don’t like the additives and these taste processed and have a strange aftertaste. There are protein bars I like better that seem to have cleaner ingredients and are organic. These are just okay. 3 stars. Made in the USA.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.best_prediction_review:
I bought this Bare Performance Nutrition Field Bar variety pack as a higher-protein snack to keep on hand for me and my teen (busy mornings + after school). These are definitely more “meal bar” than “granola bar” in both texture and how long they keep you full.

Taste/texture is very real-food leaning: dense, a little chewy/sticky, and noticeably nut-butter forward. That’s not automatically bad, but a couple of the bars in our box had a slightly dry, chalky finish that lingered and made it hard to want to finish the whole thing without water. The peanut butter flavor is the easiest for us to eat because it tastes straightforward and not overly sweet, but if you’re expecting a soft, candy-bar style protein bar, this isn’t that.

From a “will this agree with me” standpoint, these have been fine for both of us so far (no stomach upset, no weird aftertaste burps), but I still consider these something to read closely if you’re sensitive to certain sweeteners or protein sources. I also wish the label/description were a little clearer about exactly what type of protein is used, because “protein blend/isolate” style wording matters if you’re avoiding specific dairy components.

Overall, these work as a filling, portable snack, but the dense texture and occasional chalkiness make them a mixed bag for me rather than an automatic reorder.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.product_description: Herb Essential Pure Haritaki Powder - 100 g
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
This is my first time trying Haritaki. I can’t evaluate the purity of this or any other supplement.
