Notably, the label on my bag doesn’t indicate this is organic and neither does the Herb Essential India site referenced on the bag. The Amazon product page says it is organic and maybe the labeling has changed, but I can only go by the label and must assume (at least for now) this is not organic. I have no way of knowing if this Haritaki is free of contaminants.

Haritaki is supposed to have many potential benefits. The package only refers to it as a “gentle laxative”. It says 3g to 5g can be taken twice daily but doesn’t say if that’s a general dose or for a laxative effect, or how long it should take to work as a laxative. I took two doses the first day and then went to one dose in the morning (each dose was about 5 to 6 grams) for 6 days total. I didn’t have any digestive problems, but this Haritaki didn’t have any laxative effect for me.

Haritaki is extremely bitter (as expected) and is also so astringent it makes my mouth chalky and dry. I can get it down, but I have to mix it with a few ounces of sweet juice even though I don’t normally drink fruit juice. The package didn’t say what to mix the powder with, but I now see on the product page a picture showing to mix it with water. I don’t know if not mixing it in water prevented a laxative effect, but I doubt it as I always drank a full glass of water after taking a dose.

I noticed a few things while taking Haritaki that might be related, or they could just be pure coincidence. The first day, I took it morning and night as directed. That night, I had the most lucid, bizarre, disturbing “dream” (a nightmare actually) that I can recall ever having, and I still remember it as it was so vivid. After that, I didn’t take an evening dose again and started only taking one dose in the mornings. While I understand it may be coincidence, I haven’t had a bad dream again.

I came down with a bad sore throat that felt like the type that would last a few days and drank Haritaki mixed with juice, and my sore throat only lasted the one day. I get really fatigued most afternoons, and I felt more energetic when I took the Haritaki. I’ve since read Haritaki may treat a sore throat and increase energy, but my experience may be coincidence. I can’t fully recommend this product as it’s labeled a laxative but didn’t have that effect on me, and it’s not labeled organic. But it’s possible that it may have some beneficial properties. 3 stars.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.best_prediction_review:
I ordered this Herb Essential Pure Haritaki Powder (100 g) because I’ve been trying to keep a small rotation of simple herbal powders on hand, but I’m picky about what I’ll actually ingest and use consistently.

The powder I received is very fine and an earthy brown. Smell is strong and dry-herbal, which is expected, but the taste is the real gate for me: it’s intensely bitter/astringent and it hangs around. I tried small amounts in warm water and also mixed into applesauce, and it still comes through pretty aggressively. If you’re sensitive to bitter tastes or you’re trying to get a teen to take it, I don’t see this being a “hide it in anything” kind of powder unless the dose is very low.

Effect-wise, I bought this specifically because it’s commonly used as a gentle bowel support, so I did a bounded test over several nights at the same small dose. For me it was basically a non-response at that level (no clear change I could attribute to this), and I’m not comfortable just escalating the amount blindly because the listing/packaging doesn’t give a very clear, specific protocol (how much to take for what goal, expected onset window, etc.). With something like this, that lack of operational detail matters, because the line between “nothing happens” and “too much” can be thin.

No immediate adverse reaction for me (no burning mouth/throat, no nausea), but because it’s an ingestible herb powder with a strong sensory profile and unclear dose guidance, I’m not treating it as a casual daily add-in. If you already know haritaki and you can tolerate bitter/astringent powders, this may be fine, but for anyone relying on it as a predictable, gentle option, I didn’t get a repeatable result at a cautious dose and I don’t think the product supports safe trial-and-error very well.

AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.product_description: PACIFIC PPE 4 Pack Household Glove Mixed Package Reusable Cleaning Dishwashing Gloves-Latex Free Waterproof PVC Gloves for Kitchen,Gardening Gloves Unlined and Flocked with Cotton Liner(Pink&Green,L)
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.review_text:
These household gloves are made of PVC instead of latex and come in a 4-pack that includes 2 green flocklined pairs and 2 white/pink unlined pairs. Each pair is separately sealed. The colors are quite pretty compared to my standard yellow household gloves, and these gloves have cute details like scalloped rims at the opening with dots and little flowers.

These gloves feel thicker and a little bulkier than my usual yellow latex gloves. I do like the feel of the cotton coating inside the two pairs of green gloves as it prevents and/or absorbs sweat and keeps them from feeling clammy or sticky inside. The white/pink unlined gloves don’t feel as comfortable as the PVC does feel sticky to my skin, but they’re ok for short time periods. I’d prefer all the gloves were coated with cotton inside.

These gloves come up higher on my forearms than my similar household gloves, so they offer good protection of my arms and hands from hot or dirty water and grime when I’m washing dishes, cleaning the toilets and so on. The hands have a grippy exterior, so my dishes, wine glasses and other breakables don’t slip through my hands as easily.

These gloves seem to run big. I normally wear a size L in household gloves, but the size L are roomy everywhere. I can and do still use them, but my dexterity isn’t quite as good, they slip down almost off my fingers and the arms gape open a bit much. I’ve reused both styles of gloves and haven’t had any issues of tearing, and I find these easy to get on and off. While not perfect, I like these household gloves for chores pretty well (especially the coated pairs). 3.5 stars.
AE3PLZHW6NXWBMZ76TDVFQG2MJFA.best_prediction_review:
I ordered this PACIFIC PPE mixed 4-pack in size L for general kitchen cleaning and dishwashing, and I’ve also used a pair for some light gardening. First impression is good: they’re truly latex free (important in our house), the PVC feels thick enough to handle hot-ish wash water and cleaners, and the flocked cotton lining makes them more comfortable than the totally unlined cheap gloves.

Fit is where this gets a little mixed for me. The L is roomy in the fingers, which is nice for pulling them on/off quickly, but it also means I lose some dexterity when I’m trying to grip slick glassware or scrub in tight spots and the fingertips can feel a bit “floaty.” On the flip side, the cuffs are long enough to actually keep splashback from running down into the glove, and the gloves stay put on my hands without constantly sliding.

The 4-pack is useful because I can dedicate one pair to dishes, one to bathroom cleaning, and one to yard/garage stuff without cross-contaminating. So far they’ve held up well to repeated use without getting tacky or developing pinholes, which is the main reason I wanted something reusable instead of disposable.

Not perfect if you need a very snug, high-precision fit, but for routine household cleaning these are a solid, durable set and I’m likely to repurchase in a different size next time to see if I can get better control.

AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.product_description: Sharper Image Spahaven Foot Bath, Heated Spa with Massage Rollers & LCD Display, 108 Degree Heat with Insulated Walls & Pumice Stone, Soothe & Rejuvinate Tired Feet, Aromatherapy Holiday Gift
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.review_text: This Sharper Image Acupressure Foot Massage Ottoman is a luxurious relaxing experience for tired feet. Whether you are just looking to put your feet up or add some heat or have a massage, it is versatile and compact. The convertible feature is convenient and I appreciate the ability to adjust the settings such as compression level and speed. I've used some other foot massages that do not have the top piece to keep your feet snug and it prevents it from allowing a deep massage. Luckily, this device helps to hold your feet in place and allow for a more powerful massage. Overall, it is a great device and I highly recommend it for anyone with tired feet after a long day or also it would make a wonderful gift.
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.best_prediction_review:
I got this Sharper Image foot bath to help with sore feet after long walks, and it’s really a heated soak first, with some nice extras. The main thing I cared about was whether it actually gets up to a genuinely hot temp and then holds it, and it does. Once it’s warmed up, I’m not constantly topping off with more hot water, and it feels consistent rather than spiking too hot.

The massage rollers are manual (you move your feet over them), so don’t expect a “deep massage” machine, but they’re a nice add-on during a longer soak. The unit is straightforward to fill and use, the display is easy to read, and overall it’s a relaxing, low-effort way to get repeatable relief for tired feet.

AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.product_description: Trace Minerals Liquid Gut Health (32 oz) | 10mg Humic and Fulvic Acid | Protects Gut Lining, Digestion and Immunity Support, Detoxification | Cognitive Function and Mood, Metabolism | Unflavored
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.review_text: I have read about the potential health benefits of Humic and Fulvic Acids and was interested in finding a supplement that contained them. Trace Minerals appears to be a trusted and well recognized brand that makes high quality supplements so I was interested in this Liquid Gut Health supplement. Fulvic acid has a list of potential benefits ranging from inflammation to immunity to brain function to metabolic health and more. But there is not a lot of great research comparing dosages and offering proper recommendations. This liquid contains only 10mg per serving of 1 tsp. The rest of it is just water. It tastes and looks just like water. And after taking it for over a week, I can't notice any difference. But if you're looking for a pure and tasteless means of supplementing with fulvic and humic acids, this is certainly a worthy option.
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been adding this Trace Minerals Liquid Gut Health to a big glass of water most mornings. It’s genuinely easy to take: basically unflavored with just a faint earthy note, and I haven’t had any stomach cramping, urgency, or weird “detox” side effects, which is my main gate for anything in this category.

Where I’m more on the fence is the actual payload. The label highlights 10mg humic/fulvic acids, and in a 32 oz bottle that reads like you’re mostly buying water unless your dose is fairly large. I’m not comfortable claiming big gut/immune/mood changes from this yet because the effect has been subtle for me, but as a clean, tolerable way to get these minerals in, it’s been fine. I’ll update after more time if I notice a clearer difference.

AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.product_description: Spira Kind Spirulina Nibs Algae Superfood, Organic Spirulina Algae, Plant Based Protein Spirulina Crunchies, Non-GMO, Vegan, Raw, Nutrient Dense Booster for Snacks, Smoothies, Salads, Toast - 3.2oz
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.review_text: Spirulina is well known as a superfood with many health benefits. I have used spirulina powder in my morning blended protein smoothie for years. The issue with the powder is that it is a very fine dust and can make a mess. The color is a deep blue-green and even just trying to get a scoop of the powder out of the bag can make your hands stained. Luckily, there is a nice alternative thanks to Spira Kind Spirulina Nibs. These nibs make it much easier to scoop out spirulina to add into my smoothie without any mess. Furthermore, you can use the nibs more versatilely such as adding it as a topper on a salad. I like that this product is USDA certified to be organic. I wish the bag was a bit bigger for the price because it doesn't last as long as a typical spirulina powder.
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.best_prediction_review:
I like adding spirulina to smoothies, but powders are messy and tend to hijack the flavor, so I tried these “nibs” as a cleaner way to use it. The format is the win here: they’re truly crunchy and easy to sprinkle on toast, salads, or into a bowl without the green dust cloud (and without staining my counter like some powders do).

They don’t magically taste neutral—there’s still a very real algae/seaweed flavor—so I wouldn’t snack on them plain. But mixed into something savory, I can get a small daily amount in without fighting a gritty drink.

The ingredient list is simple and the bag I received was sealed and fresh. The only hesitation is value: 3.2oz disappears fast if you use it generously, so for heavy spirulina users a powder may still be cheaper per serving.

AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.product_description: FREY Laundry Stain Remover Spray -Stain Remover for Clothes, 2 Ounce Travel Size Bottle (325 Sprays) (Sandalwood Cedarwood Fragrance)
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.review_text: These FREY Laundry Stain Remover Spray bottles are small, but they get the job done well and are convenient to pack in a car or purse so that you have a stain remover for when you are on the go. I've used it on several small stains so far and found it to work rather well. There is still a minor outline of one of the stains, but I'm sure it'll disappear in the next wash. Although the bottle seems small, it does contain 325 sprays each so it will last you.
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.best_prediction_review:
I like having a true travel-size stain remover in my bag, and this FREY bottle is small but still gives a lot of uses (325 sprays). The mist is easy to aim and it’s been great for quick, on-the-spot treatment before a stain sets in.

In my testing it handled fresh food drips and light collar/cuff marks well after a short soak and a normal wash. It’s not magic on older, set-in stains, but for the everyday stuff it’s been reliably effective. The sandalwood/cedarwood scent is pleasant and doesn’t feel “chemical,” though it is definitely fragranced if you’re scent-sensitive.

AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.product_description: Natural Shoe Deodorizer Spray for Foot Odor & Sport Gear - INSTANT Shoe Odor Eliminator w 12 Essential Oils & Enzymes - Odor Eaters for Shoes, Foot Spray, Gym Bag Deodorizer (4 oz.)
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.review_text: I have a collection of sneakers and they can build up odors over time and pass on that odor into my gym bag or closet. I have used various scent sprays in the past, but have been trying to avoid artificial chemicals and potential exposure to VOCs. I was happy to discover this Natural Shoe Deodorizer Spray from Sprayzee. What really appealed to me is the natural ingredients and essential oils. Rather than being fearful of using a spray, I can rest assured knowing it is safe. Best of all, it really works! My shoes now smell great and fresh.
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.best_prediction_review:
I bought this for post-workout shoe funk and to freshen a gym bag, and it’s basically a natural deodorizer spray with an herbal essential-oil scent (not that harsh “chemical cover-up” smell). I like that for something you’re spraying into enclosed shoes/gear you’re going to be breathing near.

For effectiveness, it does a solid job knocking odor down fast, but on my worst running shoes it isn’t a one-spray miracle—more like a few sprays per shoe plus letting them air out so it can actually work. The scent itself is pleasant and doesn’t feel overpowering once it dries, and I haven’t had any irritation using it as a quick foot/shoe spray.

AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.review_text: These bags are nice for any birthday gift because they are colorful and durable and can be given to both men or women. The bag size is rather small so it is best for a few small gifts. The ribbon handles are cute, but you need to install them yourself; the bags do not come with the handles attached.
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.best_prediction_review: These gift bags are a good, no-fuss option for small gifts, but the “real” product is a bag + DIY handles. The ribbon handles aren’t attached, so you have to thread and tie them before they’re usable, which adds a step if you’re wrapping in a hurry. Once assembled, the paper feels nicely thick and the seams and handles held up fine with normal-weight items without tearing.

AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.product_description: VivaStix BCAA, Glutamine & Beetroot Powder - Supports Muscle Recovery and Growth |Cherry Limeade Flavor with No Artificial Sweetener & Sugar, Non-GMO & Gluten Free, Keto & Vegan Friendly - 30 Packets
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.review_text: I pack my gym bag and take it with me to work and it can sometimes be a pain to pack pre workout. You can either take the entire container or put a few scoops in a container or bag. Also, some preworkout mixes have caffeine which I try to avoid if I am training in the evening. This VivaStix solves both those problems. It packs 5g of BCAAs, 5g glutamine, and some beet root powder along with being naturally sweetened. It tastes great and is very easy to mix into a water bottle. Quick, convenient, and tasty.
AEKMZRFI24IXTBXMZOKENNL3VFBQ.best_prediction_review:
I like the single-serve packets on this VivaStix BCAA/Glutamine/Beetroot because it’s genuinely easier than a tub and scoop—easy to keep a few in a gym bag or at work and mix when needed. The Cherry Limeade is pleasantly tart and not cloying, and I didn’t get that fake-sweet aftertaste (a dealbreaker for me). It mixed cleanly in cold water in a shaker with no weird foam or grit.

Effect-wise, I’m not going to claim miracles, but it’s been a solid post-workout option and I’ve felt reasonably good the next day after lifting. For me the main win is the repeatable, low-friction routine plus a flavor I’ll actually drink consistently.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: OTC Hearing Aids, Volume and Modes Adjustable, USA Made Digital Core, Rechargeable and Active Noise Cancelling, Pocket Size Hearing Amplifier for Seniors
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: These hearing aids arrived well packaged with an egg-shaped container and charging bay, two manuals, a micro-USB cable and wall plug as well as replacement earpieces and cleaning brushes. The hearing aids took less than one hour to fully charge which is indicated by the orange and green LED lights on the charging/storage container. The hearing aids are small enough to keep their use unnoticeable while still being large enough to handle easily. Being rechargeable, you don't have to mess with small replacement batteries that are difficult to install. The hearing aids work well and are easy to operate which is assisted by the quick instruction booklet provided. These are a little pricy but compared to others that I've tried I would say that these are worth the extra cost. I would recommend these hearing aids.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.best_prediction_review:
I use kinesiology tape for occasional knee and shoulder support and wanted to see how these precut strips compared to the bigger brands I’ve used. The 3-pack (black/blue/beige) arrived as expected and the strips have a good amount of stretch without feeling stiff or plasticky. The precut size is convenient and the rounded corners definitely help with edge lifting, especially around the knee.

Adhesion is more of a medium-to-strong hold than “rock tape” level, but it’s consistent. On clean, dry skin with a good rub to activate the adhesive, it made it through a workout and a shower without peeling off, and I was able to get about 2 days before the edges started rolling where clothing rubs. It was comfortable and didn’t irritate my skin, and it removed without feeling like it was taking skin with it.

For the price you get a lot of usable pieces, and for moderate support this is a good value. If you need maximum multi-day hold for heavy sweating, you may still want a premium brand.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: M.A.S.S. Nutraceuticals Dominate - Stim Free Pre-Workout Supplement – Nitric Oxide Supplement, Nitric Oxide Booster - Caffeine Free, Creatine Free – Performance Booster, Vasodilator, Vascularity, Pump
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: I've tried many pre-workout supplements previously. Most of them contain caffeine or large amounts of B12 but this one does not. The container arrived double sealed and has 240 capsules in the container which are gold in color. This provides only 30 servings per container at the recommended serving size which is 8 capsules! The directions state to use 30 minutes prior to your workout and that new users may want to start with only 4 capsules. I'm not a fan of taking pills and having to take 8 capsules prior to my workout isn't appealing by any means. The capsules are average in size but I'd prefer this to be in a powder format instead. You could open up each of the capsules and put them into a shake instead but I have no idea if that would cause any issues. There are a good mix of ingredients in the supplement which does contain some salt and black pepper. The supplement does work for its intended purpose but I had to lower the rating because of the 8 capsule serving size.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.best_prediction_review:
This pre-workout arrived sealed and the label is clear that it’s stim free (no caffeine) and also creatine free, which is exactly what I wanted for late day workouts. The serving directions are straightforward and it mixes easily with water with no odd smell or aftertaste, and I had no stomach upset from it.

For a pump product the only thing I care about is whether it actually changes the workout, and I did notice better pumps and vascularity compared to training with just water (I’m not claiming it’s a miracle, but it’s a real, repeatable difference). It doesn’t feel like a “wired” pre-workout, which is the whole point for me.

Value-wise it’s fine for what it is, but you do need to look at the servings per container and your actual dose, because if you end up taking more than one scoop to get the effect you want the cost per workout goes up fast. Overall I’d recommend it if you specifically need a caffeine-free pump formula.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: Alpha Evolve, Natural Testosterone Support - Hormone Support- Powerful Estrogen Blocker - 180 Veggie Capsules , 1 Month Supply
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: This supplement arrived in a double sealed container. There are 180 capsules in the container and the suggested serving size is 3 capsules per day which is a 2 month supply at the suggested serving size. The capsules are of average size and there was no strange odor from the container upon opening. This supplement combines several ingredients that aren't typically found together in a single supplement. Those ingredients are DIM and DHEA. The other ingredients are comparable to most competitive products and what you would expect to find in a testosterone supplement. I found the supplement to be well tolerated with no noticeable side effects. I like the combination of ingredients which allows me to take fewer supplements overall but the requirement to take 3 capsules sort of makes up the difference. The price is comparable to other competitive products but you get 2 months instead of 1 month which most of the competitive products provide. Overall I like the ingredient list and I think that this supplement is worth taking.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.best_prediction_review:
The bottle arrived sealed and contained 180 veggie capsules. The serving size on mine is 6 capsules per day, so the “1 month supply” claim is accurate, but it’s also a lot of capsules to commit to if you already take other supplements, and it makes the cost-per-day higher than it first looks.

I’ve taken this daily with food and I’ve had no unwanted side effects (no stomach issues, no headaches or jitters), which is my main gate with anything that messes with hormones. As far as results, I did notice a small bump in workout drive/energy around the end of the first week, but I wouldn’t call it a dramatic change and I can’t claim anything specific beyond that yet.

Overall it seems tolerable and the capsule quality is fine, but for me the 6-capsule protocol is the biggest downside and it hurts the value unless you’re specifically looking for this type of formula and don’t mind the daily pill burden.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: Testosterone Booster for Men | Made in USA | Libido Booster | Tribulus Terrestris for Men | Horny Goat Weed, Natural Stamina, Energy and Strength Booster | Male Enhancer Pills - 180 Capsules | VitPro
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: I looked at a lot of supplements prior to this one but the Vitpro supplement seemed to have the best ingredient list and the most reasonable cost. The bottle arrived double sealed and with 180 capsules that are average in size. The suggested serving is 3 capsules which provides 60 servings per container at that level. I had no unwanted side effects from taking this supplement. I would suggest starting with a single capsule and working your way up to the suggested serving size to ensure that it doesn't cause any issues for you. I had a great workout and I believe that the quality ingredients will help with stamina, endurance and energy. I can't say that it affected my libido but I wasn't having any issues in that department previously so I probably wouldn't notice any difference. There are many of these types of supplements that include "proprietary blends" or other unnecessary ingredients and this supplement was one of the few that didn't which I liked. I would definitely recommend this supplement.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been testing this VitPro testosterone/libido booster for a few weeks. The bottle arrived sealed and it’s a good quantity at 180 capsules. What matters more here is the serving math: if you follow the higher end of the suggested dose you can burn through it fast, so it’s not automatically a “6 month supply” type product.

The capsules are average size and I didn’t notice any strong smell. Ingredient-wise I like that it’s a straightforward formula (no proprietary blend hiding the amounts), so at least you can sanity-check what you’re actually taking.

As far as results, I did notice a mild bump in day-to-day energy, but I can’t honestly claim a big “testosterone boost” from a few weeks without labs. I also don’t have a baseline libido problem, so I’m not going to oversell that side of it.

Tolerability was good for me with no noticeable side effects. I’d suggest starting below the label dose for the first several days and ramping up only if you’re fine with it. Probably best for someone looking for a mild, well-tolerated herbal option rather than expecting a dramatic change.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: Weekly Pill Organizer 2 Times A Day, BUG HULL 7 Day AM PM Pill Case Push Button Pill Container Box for Vitamins, Fish Oils, Supplements
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: Now that I'm older I find that I need to take more supplements and medication than before. I also find that I sometimes forget if I've taken my meds and supplements already, especially when I'm tired. This organizer fixes that problem because it has separate day and night containers for each day of the week. The organizer is easy to use and well made. There was no strange odor upon removing from the packaging and the containers are all clearly marked. The containers are large enough to store a decent amount of pills and since there are separate containers for day and night it should have plenty of room even if you take a large number of pills each day. I would definitely recommend this pill organizer.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.best_prediction_review:
I use a weekly organizer for supplements and a couple prescriptions, so the main job for me is reducing the “did I take my morning dose already?” doubt. This 7-day AM/PM layout makes that simple, and the day labels are easy to read at a glance.

The push-button lids are a lot easier than the super tight snap lids I’ve had in the past, but they still click shut and stay closed in a bag. The compartments hold a decent amount (including larger fish oil softgels) without feeling cramped, and the doors line up well so you’re not fighting them every time. Overall it feels well made and actually supports a repeatable routine without extra hassle. I would recommend it if you need a straightforward twice-a-day pill box.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: D-Shaped Hand Exerciser Set,3 Levels Grip Strength,Hand Grip Strengthener, Silicone Grip Strength Trainer for Senior & Arthritis Relieve Wrist Pain (3PCS)
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: I already have the metal and far more challenging hand grip strength exerciser but I wanted something that didn't cause as much discomfort for my hands. These D-shaped exercisers seemed like a good alternative. For what they are they work very well. The D-shape fits nicely in the hand and you can comfortably use them for extended periods. They come in three different levels of difficulty (30, 40 and 50 lbs). This isn't nearly as much as the metal ones that I have which start at 100 lbs. but you can use these for a longer period without hurting your hands. These would be good for someone with limited grip strength, rehabilitation or for seniors. They seem to be well made and should last for a long time. I would recommend these for the above mentioned purposes.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.best_prediction_review:
This set comes with three silicone D-shaped grip trainers with different resistance levels. I like the D shape because it keeps my wrist in a more neutral position compared to the cheap spring grippers I’ve used before, which matters when my hands feel stiff.

I tested them for about a week: started with the light one for a few days (2 sets of 15 squeezes per hand), then moved to the medium. The light is very doable for seniors/rehab or arthritis days, the medium feels like general maintenance, and the heavy is still nowhere near a 100+ lb metal gripper, so strength athletes will outgrow it.

No weird odor out of the package and the silicone hasn’t torn or gone sticky. It’s not a miracle cure, but it did make daily tasks like opening jars and twisting caps a little easier without aggravating my wrist.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: 8 Pcs Pimple Popper Tool Kit - Blackhead Remover Comedone Extractor Acne Removal Set with Metal Case for Quick and Easy Removal of Pimples, Blackheads, Zit Removing, Forehead, Facial and Nose
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: So this is a gross subject but there are times that you just have to do what you have to do and this kit helps to minimize the damage. The kit arrived well packaged in an included storage tin. The tools were wrapped in a sealed bag and are all well made. They look like a small dental tool kit but they actually work well and are easy to use and clean. For those that suffer from persistent acne these tools help to avoid excess swelling from trying to use you hands. There are a number of different tools included so that you can use the one that is most effective for your condition. I would definitely recommend this kit.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.best_prediction_review:
This kit arrived in a small metal case and the tools were clean and individually slotted so they don’t rattle around. The stainless tools feel solid and the tips are precise enough to target a clogged pore without having to use fingernails. I tried a couple loop sizes on my nose and chin and it did remove a few blackheads with better control than squeezing.

A couple notes: only 2-3 of the 8 tools are realistically going to be in my routine (the rest feel like bundle padding), and you need to be conservative with pressure and prep the skin first or you can irritate the area. For the price it’s a decent little set, but I wouldn’t treat it as “quick and easy” if you’re new to extractors.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: Nutro Organic Apple Cider Vinegar Gummies - Immunity & Detox | Vegan-Friendly, Non-GMO, for Digestion and Weight Loss | Apple Flavored | May Boost Immunity & Energy
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: I wanted the benefits of apple cider vinegar without the bad taste or smell and these gummies seemed to be a good solution. I'm also not a fan of swallowing pills and having to measure out liquid for each dose also wasn't appealing. These gummies arrived sealed and you get a decent amount in the bottle. There was only a slight odor to the gummies but it wasn't overpowering. The gummies actually taste good with only a very slight bitter taste. They contain a good amount of apple cider vinegar with only a small amount of sugar. I had no digestive issues with these gummies or other side effects. I would definitely recommend this apple cider vinegar supplement.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.best_prediction_review: These apple cider vinegar gummies arrived well sealed and the bottle is easy to open/close. The gummies have a mild apple taste and aren’t overly sour like liquid ACV, which makes them easy to take daily without that harsh aftertaste. I’ve been using them for about 2 weeks (2 gummies per day) and I do notice less post-meal bloating, but I wouldn’t expect dramatic “detox” or weight loss results from gummies alone. Ingredient-wise I like that they’re vegan and non-GMO, but I still wish the label was a little more specific on exact amounts per gummy so it’s easier to compare potency to other brands. Price is fair for the quantity, and I haven’t had any stomach irritation so far.

AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.product_description: Aggressive Labz Behemoth Creatine Power Plexx - Seven Elite Forms of Creatine - Promotes Power, Strength, Size and Explosiveness - 60 Day Supply - Lions Blood Flavor
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.review_text: This creatine supplement arrived in a double sealed container. I was easily able to find the scoop at the top of the container. With a flavor named "Lion's Blood" I was expecting the taste to either be really bad or be somewhat close to blood orange flavor. I used 24oz of water instead of the 8oz suggested and the taste is actually pretty good. It has a light orange taste (because it is watered down from adding more water). There are no extra unnecessary ingredients and no "proprietary blends" in the powder. There is a higher than average amount of creatine in this powder which is reflected in the additional warnings on the label regarding its use. This supplement is targeted toward the serious athlete or body builder and not the casual gym user. I would recommend this supplement for those that need the additional creatine for their sport but it's probably an excessive amount of creatine for the average person. I would recommend this supplement for its intended audience.
AHAS7NULKULTXUJBAFC4HKPRQPJQ.best_prediction_review: This creatine arrived well sealed and the Lions Blood flavor is sweet but not unbearable (kind of a fruit punch vibe). No strange odor when I opened it. It mixes fine in a shaker bottle with 12-14 oz of water, but if you just stir it in a glass it does leave a little grit at the bottom. I’ve used plain monohydrate for years, so I was curious if the “seven forms” would feel any different. After about 2 weeks of daily use I can’t honestly say I can prove a big difference in strength or size versus monohydrate yet, but it also didn’t cause any GI issues for me. I started with about half a scoop for the first few days and then worked up to a full serving to make sure it was well tolerated. The ingredient list is straightforward without a bunch of extra filler, which I appreciate in a creatine. If you want a flavored creatine that’s easy to get down and you’re okay with the “multi-form” claim being hard to verify short term, this seems like a solid option.

AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.product_description: Esmartlife Foldable Squat Free Sitz Bath,Sitz Bath for Toilet Hemorrhoids Postpartum Care to Treat Postpartum Wounds, Hemorrhoids, Perineal Care, Episiotomy Recovery,Reusable Anal and Vaginal Care
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.review_text: The seat of the tub is amply sized. I have a big rear end and I fit on this just fine. I think the water stream is positioned more to the front, so you just need to position your body to get it into the right place where you need the stream to flow. I think the water stream pumps up high enough with the hand pump used in a free hand. It fits on my elongated toilet eat, and I assume on a standard as well. It will also sit directly on the toilet bowl. I like that it can fold up and become more compact. The ad/order page actually shows 3 different styles of this sitz tub (you can see 3 different hole patterns in the back of the seat). I received the one with 5 circle holes... I am sure they all function the same, but I don't know why the would depict them differently in this one order page. I removed a star based on this product depiction "sloppiness."  I do not understand what they mean by "no need to squat down."
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.best_prediction_review:
I got this sitz bath for hemorrhoid flare ups. It does fit my standard round toilet, and the foldable design is why I picked it (tiny bathroom, I don’t want a big basin sitting out).

The main “system” thing to know is the geometry: when you sit, the spray/soak area is centered enough for anal/perineal relief, but if you’re expecting it to reach very far forward without you adjusting how you sit, it’s more limited. It’s stable for me once it’s seated on the rim, but you do have to make sure the fold is fully opened so it’s not lopsided.

My only real gripe is the way it’s marketed/listed. The photos made it look like there were different versions (different hole patterns/shapes shown), but what showed up is one specific style. It works fine, but that mismatch is a trust issue with body-care items.

Cleanup is basically rinse and let it dry. I’d still recommend it if you specifically need compact storage and you have a standard toilet.

AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.product_description: Enyur Floor Cleaner - Multi-Surface Cleaner, 60 loads (1 pack, fresh scent)
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.review_text: I find this cleaner to be fairly average, which does not mean bad or deficient. It gets a star for being more environmentally friendly than others. I found for it to work better with grease type dirt, just like any other cleaners, you need to let it soak into the stain for a little while first, then mop it up. Give it a little time to soak in and then scrubbing won't be so hard. (The packaging does not tell you this, but just basically mop and go.) In the package it smells fresh and clean, but one sheet of it in a gallon doesn't really smell like much. This pack does yield quite a bit so it should last a while. I also tried it on my stove for grease spots and that worked too, but again, I had to let it sit for bit to make wiping easier.
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.best_prediction_review:
I got this Enyur floor cleaner because I’m trying to simplify what we use around the house and still keep things actually clean. I’ve been using it diluted in a mop bucket on vinyl and sealed tile. For me the “works/doesn’t work” test is the kitchen traffic lanes (sticky spots around the stove and the dog’s water bowl). If I let it sit on the worst spots for a minute before mopping, it does lift the everyday grime without me having to scrub every inch.

If I just slosh it on and immediately wipe, it’s more “average” and I’ll still see a couple dull/sticky patches until I go back over them. The fresh scent is there but pretty mild at the dilution I’m using.

So far it’s been easy to mix and use, and it hasn’t left my floor feeling weird or tacky when I use the little dwell time.

AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.product_description: Formula Trim Probiotic for her 25 Billion CFU Guaranteed, 10 Strains, Shelf Stable, Gluten Dairy and Soy Free, Promotes Gut Health and Eases Digestion 30 Day Supply
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.review_text: I rate it 5 stars becasue I try to take a probiotic everyday and these seem to be a good enough substitute for what I was already taking. I just started with these, and so far not bad side effects. 25 Billion is a lot! I read that at some point the higher (way high) numbers might be overkill, but I also read that this high number can help the gut even more... and I need all the help I can get in that dept! I'll try anything to get my gut to behave! I do find that they are a bit pricey for a 30 day supply. I will report back if these present any difficulties.
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been taking this about 2 weeks, 1 capsule in the morning with breakfast, because my digestion is touchy and a lot of “for her” formulas don’t sit well with me. Good news: no nausea, no cramping, and no “run to the bathroom” urgency. I did notice less bloating after a few days, but it’s not a miracle and I’m not calling it a full fix yet.

I like that it’s shelf stable and labeled gluten/dairy/soy free. That said, I wish the listing/packaging were a little clearer about what exactly “25 Billion CFU guaranteed” means at the end of shelf life vs at manufacture. For now it’s a tolerable swap in my routine and I’ll keep watching over the full month.

AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.product_description: Magnesium Citrate Liquid Drops, 30 Pre-Filled Single Servings for Freshness, Support Bone Density, Muscle Health, Immune Defense, Mood, and Sleep, Made in The USA
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.review_text: I take magnesium regularly for leg cramp prevention. I try to take it at night before bed to try and head cramps off at the pass, but unfortunately this is not always possible to do. These looked like a good product to try. Twist the top off, and just drink the premeasured strawberry flavored magnesium in one shot. Well, unfortunately I do not find that the liquid easily comes out of the tube when opened, and I have to squeeze and roll it several times to get every drop out. Also it is only about 1/2 of the dose as my other under the tongue formula, so I do not think it packs enough of a punch for my needs. We are all different, so your requirements may be different. I still get leg cramps the nights I use this... but that is me. Sometimes nothing helps me, no matter what or how much. You need to be the judge on things like this based on your experience taking it.
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.best_prediction_review:
I got these magnesium citrate liquid drops mainly for nighttime muscle tightness and sleep (I deal with a lot of on-and-off aches, so I’m careful adding anything new). The single pre-filled servings are the best part for me because there’s no measuring, no sticky bottle rim, and it’s easy to throw a couple in a bag and know I took the full dose.

Taste is not great, but it’s doable if you just take it quick and chase with water. The bigger “health” question with magnesium for me is always GI upset, and I did not get cramping or an urgent laxative effect from these at the serving size provided, which is a win. As far as results, I can’t say it’s a miracle, but I did notice my legs feel a bit less twitchy on the nights I use it, and I fall asleep a little easier. I wouldn’t rely on it as a fix for serious sleep issues, but as a simple, low-drama magnesium option, it’s been working well enough so far.

AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.product_description: Improved Food Allergy Test by CERASCREEN – Analysis for Food Allergies to 44 Foods | Health Screening | Determine Food Intolerances and Allergies | Professional CLIA-Certified Lab Analysis
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.review_text: As long as you are drawing blood and sending it in, why not test for as many allergies as possible? The instructions are easy enough. They do ask that you have eaten from all the foods that they test for, within a 3 week period, to get better results. Since these are common foods, I did eat from most of them in this timeframe. I only poked myself once, and I was able to fill the number of circles that they asked for, I think 8 samples was what was needed... but it took a long time becasue my initial poke was relatively small and wimpy. I sent the results in on a Monday, and I had results back online in about a week. Mine luckily came back that I am not allergic to any of the foods tested for, so I was happy about that. I am fairly confident that this is accurate becasue I really didn't feel that I was allergic to any food substance... I just like to eat, so darn, it didn't tell me anything I should avoid on this list. THe test was easy enough for me to do myself, and I do not like poking myself to draw blood! It was a matter of 1-2-3...go!
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.best_prediction_review:
I ordered this because I have ongoing gut/skin issues and I’m trying to be more methodical about possible food triggers without doing endless random eliminations.

The kit was pretty straightforward. You register the code online, do the finger prick, and collect the sample the way they show. Mine arrived sealed/clean, and everything I needed was in the box. The lancet was a little more “ouch” than I expected, but it was quick and I was able to get enough blood without multiple tries.

Results came back in the expected window and the report was easy to read (clear list of the foods tested and where you fall). For me the main “does it work” question with these is: can I complete the process at home without guesswork, and does the report actually answer something actionable. In that sense it did. I’m not treating it like a magic diagnosis, but it gave me a clearer starting point for what to discuss with my doctor / what to trial first, instead of throwing darts at 20 foods.

AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.product_description: 12 Pieces Eyeglass Ear Saver for Mask, Anti-Slip Ear Mask Holder Relieve Pressure & Pain for Ear, Mini Mask Hook Strap Extender for Long-time Mask Wearer(White)
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.review_text: I tried these on my plastic frame glasses, and I was able to slide them into place. I think how well they work depends on the style of mask you are attaching, and how tight the ear loops are. A brand new maks out of the package seemed to work pretty well. It fits more snugly. An accordian style mask left a bit of a gap on the bottom. You have to play with the elastic to find the right place for it to sit on the arms of the glasses. I found my old looser cloth masks do not do as well. They don't hold in place and the ear loops may be too thick for this silicone button to grab. With the right mask it does prove to be comfortable. It comes with 6 pairs, so you should have enough here for most of your glasses, with some spare... unless you are sharing!
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.best_prediction_review:
These are very basic little plastic hooks, but they solve the main problem for me: soreness behind the ears from mask loops (and with glasses on, it gets worse fast). With these on, the loops are no longer rubbing my ears, and the relief was immediate, especially on longer errands.

It does take a minute to figure out which notch to use because it depends on your mask’s ear loops and how tight you normally wear it. On thinner/looser loops I had to use a tighter notch or the mask felt a little too relaxed, and on one style it did create a slight bottom gap if I went too loose. Once I found the right notch, it was easy to repeat and I can get the mask on/off faster without fighting my glasses.

For me these are worth having around, but just know it’s mask-dependent and you may need to adjust the notch to keep a good fit while still getting the ear relief.

AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.product_description: Bruizex Lymphatic Drainage Massager: Fibro Roller for Fibrosis Massage and Treatment I for Post Surgery Recovery After 360 Lipo, Tummy Tuck, BBL I Works with lipofoam, Foam Pads, Faja I
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.review_text:
What I like is that the ease of the roller ball, and the hardness of it, allows you to roll right over your clothing... so if you do not feel like getting undressed (other than after a shower, or any other reason why you are not clothed), it seems like it is still effective even thru "average" wight clothing. I am sitting here with sweatpants and a thermal shirt on, and the rolling motion is still felt thru my garbs. The instructions on the box do not say anything about "skin," and just mentions "affected areas."

Now I will go the opposite direction with this... let's now assume I am now in only my birthday suit, and I want to rub in a lymphatic massage oil, or any other herbal oil or body butter... can I use this roller to roll over or to apply something to my skin that also helps my lymphatic fluids to move? The directions on the box that this ships in do not mention anything about using this product with oils or creams. THEN i did just look at the sales/info order page here on Amazon and it does mention using it with these products. I only checked here to double check it's usage. Good to know, but I with this was printed on the box or came as an inserted instruction. But, now I know and will proceed to try that!

The only other negative that I noticed, and this is probably just me, but I felt my soft tissue on my upper outer arm kind of hurt, like light bruising, after rolling this over that area a few times. That sore feeling did go away after a couple minutes. I got this becasue I have lymphedema swelling and liquid retention mostly in my left leg, becasue I had uterine cancer and 50+ lymph nodes removed 16 years ago. My arms are fine, but I do have fibromyalgia sensitivities, so that is probably why this would have felt like it hurt. (Kinda dumb observation on my part I admit, being that I am the one who controls the pressure! Not the fault to the product! But I guess it doen't take much pressure to use this ball, and that can be a good thing.) THis said, compared to other lymph massagers, like wooden U shaped sticks and wands, they tend to massage up a larger and flatter swath, involving more skin area with each stroke. This one is more like a rolling acupressure point. I an not tell which method moves the lymph better... I am going to guess this is personal preference.
AFI6TBNX2KK7QFVUIMLDG2PC37PA.best_prediction_review:
I bought this because I get swelling and those “lumpy/tight” spots sometimes (NOT from surgery), and I wanted something I could use that wouldn’t wreck my hands/forearms the way doing it manually does. This is a firm roller, and it definitely gives you that deep pressure feel right away.

A couple things I learned quickly: you really do have to go slow and treat it like a controlled massage, not a back-and-forth scrubbing motion. If I used too much pressure or moved too fast, it was just uncomfortable and felt like I was going to bruise myself (which kind of defeats the point). With moderate pressure and shorter passes, it seems to help with that “stuck” feeling, but I’m careful about what I claim here because it’s hard to know what’s true lymph drainage vs just general tissue massage.

Size/shape-wise, it’s easy enough to hold and get around curves, but it works best on flatter areas where you can keep even contact. On bony areas or anywhere already tender, this is a no for me. I also found it works better over a thin layer of lotion/oil so it glides, otherwise it drags and that’s when it starts to feel too aggressive. If you are super sensitive or bruise easily, I would start very lightly or skip it.

Overall, it’s a simple tool and it does what I expected in terms of firm pressure and helping me work on tight spots without my hands getting tired, but it’s not a “magic fix” and you need to use some common sense with pressure so you don’t make yourself sore.

AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.product_description: Get relief from cold and flu symptoms and bring wellness to your entire family in style with a Crane warm mist humidifier. Crane’s 0.5 Gal. warm mist humidifier provides up to 250 sq. ft. of coverage and 13-hours of soothing moisture to help relieve the effects of dryness and congestion. This unit is an all-natural, non-medicated relief from dryness, cough, cold and flu symptoms. Use with Crane’s vapor pads (HS-1950 & HS-1950L) for an elevated experience to help you and your family breathe easy and sleep through the night peacefully.
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.review_text:
I got this because I have never come across a warm humidifier with the option of adding essential oil (which is what I always wanted) I usually use a diffuser/humidifier when someone in our house is sick, and I like to add eucalyptus or something along those lines for (stuffy noses because it helps them sleep better, and moisture helps with dry air.) I feel like the warm humidifier is more effective with opening up nasal passages. The size is pretty convenient would fit nicely on a nightstand and hold enough water (half gallon) to run for 13 hours. Mine came looking used because there were several dirty looking spots on it (pictures). I filled it up today because my daughter is currently sick with what sounds like croup. You simple unscrew the bottom of the tank and fill it up (I noticed there is no line that says the max) so i filled it up to the brand name. It took a couple minutes to warm up and then it started to shoot out the warm (kinda hot) vapor. There is only one button so it's very easy to operate- the button gives you 3 different options: low, high, or off. I love the tray feature for the essential oil vapor that and the warm air are the main reasons why I decided to get it! It's also pretty quiet, just kind of sounds like a very low heater fan. It's easy to clean and doesnt require filters either.
Overall happy and working out great so far.
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.best_prediction_review:
We got this Crane warm mist humidifier mainly for those nights when someone is congested (especially kids), and for us the warm mist feels more soothing than cool mist. In a medium bedroom it starts putting out a steady mist pretty quickly and the air doesn’t feel as dry by morning. I also really like that it’s just a simple dial (no modes to click through when you’re half asleep).

Refilling has been easy and the tank size gets us through the night without needing to top it off. Cleaning/maintenance is pretty straightforward too, but like any humidifier you do have to stay on top of it so it doesn’t get gross—warm mist doesn’t magically make that go away. One thing to be aware of with warm mist: the unit/base can get hot, so I keep it up out of reach and wouldn’t put it on a low nightstand next to little kids.

Overall it’s been a convenient, low-fuss humidifier that’s comfortable to sleep with (no weird smell, and the moisture feels “gentle” vs blasting cool air).

It mixes easily into smoothies and tea, but it is definitely very “green” tasting, so I’d start with about 1 tsp and adjust. No stomach issues for me, but I wouldn’t take this on an empty stomach if you’re sensitive to greens. My only issue is the “30 servings” claim isn’t super clear unless you already know what they count as a serving size.

AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.product_description: Grenera Organic Turmeric Tablet, 240 Tablets - Uncoated, Chemical Free Tablets | Organic, Vegan, Kosher
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.review_text:
What I like about this product:
- organic /non-gmo
- you could break the tablets in half
- easy to digest
- no flavor burps

Cons:
- tablets are large if you don't break them in half, not ideal for those who have a hard time swallowing pills
- unsure if these contain curcumin. The bottle says curcuma, I don't know if they are the same thing or different but I specifically take Turmeric Curcumin with black pepper.
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.best_prediction_review: I like that these are organic/vegan/kosher and the tablets are uncoated (no weird coating taste). The main thing to know is they are real turmeric tablets, not a “curcumin + black pepper” type formula, so don’t buy expecting that. The tablets are on the larger side, so if you struggle swallowing pills you may want to split them in half. No stomach upset for me so far even on an empty stomach. Bottle was sealed and arrived fine.

AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.product_description: Premium Cotton Balls (200 Count) Absorbent Natural Cotton Balls for Skincare and Makeup Removal, Hypoallergenic Soft and Multi-Purpose for Applying Oil, Lotion, Powder, and for Baby Care
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.review_text: We use cotton ball for different things around the house- we like our crafts and beauty supplies so it's good to have some on hand. These are pretty much what you would expect of cotton balls only they are a bit more smaller (but slightly more dense) and a bit more expensive (currently $4.99)
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.best_prediction_review: These cotton balls are fine for basics (toner, removing eye makeup, baby care). They’re absorbent and mostly hold together, but I do get a little lint if I saturate them with oil. Mine arrived clean/no weird odor. The bag isn’t resealable, so I had to move them into a jar to keep bathroom moisture out. Count seems accurate; price feels a bit high for how small they are.

AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.product_description: Freshfield Chlorella, Vegan Friendly, 500mg Capsules, Naturally-Occurring Chlorophyll & Beta-Carotene from Freshwater Green Algae, High in Antioxidants, Plastic Negative
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.review_text: My go to Chlorella brand is no longer available, but i found this one which seems basically the same (from the size of the capsule down to the MGs). I've been taking it for years for overall health benefits (b12, detoxifying properties, etc).  I like that this one is veggie capsules, non-gmo, and free of soy, dairy, and fillers. These are easy to swallow and digest and are ok to take on an empty stomach. They are also tasteless and don't cause any after taste or burps. Not sure where the chlorella is sourced, but It would be a huge plus if it was organic.
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been taking chlorella on/off for a while and this one is comparable to other 500mg capsules I’ve used, so it’s easy to keep my dose consistent. Tolerance is my main gate with greens and these passed: no nausea, no “green” burps, and I can take 1–2 capsules even on an empty stomach.

I do wish the brand would be a little clearer on the listing/bottle about what form/spec you’re getting (ex: broken cell wall vs not), because that affects what you’re actually relying on. Packaging was fine and arrived sealed, and the capsules are easy to swallow.

Overall good quality so far and I’d consider repurchasing if they tighten up the labeling info.

AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.product_description: Dreft Stage 1: Newborn Hypoallergenic Baby Laundry Detergent Liquid Soap, 64 Total Loads, 46 Ounce (Pack of 2), & Dreft Blissfuls Laundry Scent Booster Beads for Washer, Baby Fresh Scent, 14.8 oz
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.review_text: This smells different than their other dreft I've got in the past and I love the update. It smells so good and not too intense. The box reduces plastic waste and is recyclable, easier and more convenient to use than a pour plastic bottle. Just a warning to turn and open the spout slow because it will come out fast if not. I've been using this on our daughters clothes and cloth diapers and it cleans very well and makes everything smell great!
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.best_prediction_review: Used this Dreft bundle on newborn clothes/blankets and it cleans well and rinses out without that stiff “detergent” feel. The scent booster beads give that classic baby-fresh smell, but it’s strong, so I used about 1/2 of what I normally would and that was plenty. No skin issues for us so far (my baby is pretty sensitive). Packaging arrived clean/no leaks. Only annoyance: the bead container opens fast, so pour slowly or you’ll overdo it.

AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.product_description: Tattle Plant Protein Powder - Vegan & Non-GMO, Natural Flavors & Sweeteners, Turmeric & Tart Cherry for Anti-Inflammation, Lactose & Gluten Free, Soy Free, No Sugar Added (Vanilla Cold Brew, 25 Serve)
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.review_text:
Unique tasting protein powder and its pretty tastey. The flavor reminds me of something I cant quite put my finger on at the moment.

The ingredients are very good.. organic, non gmo and plant based are always what I look for in a product.

The bag is resealable, and it comes with a large scooper in the bag. The powder is pretty dense.

I put 1 scoop into about 12oz of unsweetened non-dairy milk. Unfortunately it doesnt mix well just shaking it alone (will just clump up). You either need a blender or shaken with one of those coil springs, then it becomes smooth and looks like a dijon mustard color. You could adjust the thickness to your liking by adding more liquid, but I found that about 12-14 oz makes for a good thickness and sweetness. I'd consider getting it again, I'm interested in seeing what the chocolate tastes like.
AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been trying different plant proteins and this one checks a lot of boxes for me (vegan, non-gmo, lactose/gluten/soy free, no sugar added). The Vanilla Cold Brew flavor is actually good and not overly sweet, and I don’t get that weird diet-y aftertaste. It also didn’t bother my stomach.

Mixing is fine if you use the right method. For me, 1 scoop in about 10-12 oz of cold water in a shaker bottle with a metal coil ball, shake hard for ~20 seconds, let it sit 1 minute, then shake again. That got it pretty smooth (still a tiny bit of plant “graininess,” which is normal). I wouldn’t try to just stir it with a spoon.

I like the added turmeric/tart cherry idea, but I’m mainly buying it as a clean protein that I can actually drink consistently. Overall good experience and I would get again.

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: Padgene Mens 11 in 1 Hair Clipper Electric Rechargable Quiet Detail Trimmer Professional Cordless Haircut Oil Head Head Shaver Grooming Kit for Heads, Beards & all Body Grooming (Super Grooming Kit)
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text:
We came to terms very quickly that we were going to use ONE attachment on this device. That attachment is the head shaver. No, I didn't use it on my head, but I did get good use of it as a body trimmer. The blades dull out really quickly and there is no cleaning solution included with this (unless I was just missing it from my box). When it is fully charged, it works VERY well and has a nice close shave without any issue. A few uses in though showed a bit of dullness that didn't hurt the skin, but instead "skipped" hair that should have otherwise shaved off.

This could be a great set if there was more quality control and a much, much easier way to change the attachments. We tried the nose/ear hair trimmer one during our initial use and found that it worked great, but we ended up breaking the attachment when trying to remove it.

There are better products on the market for all of these needs, and I'd recommend you check those out first.
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.best_prediction_review:
I’ve used a lot of pre-workouts over the years, and I went through enough of this Pre Workout Demon (berry) to feel comfortable calling it a nonresponse for me.

You definitely get the beta-alanine tingles, and the flavor is actually fine (not overly sweet, which I prefer). The problem is that those sensory cues didn’t translate into a meaningful training bump. Compared to my usual “no pre” baseline and a couple other pre’s I keep around, I’d say this felt ~20% less effective on drive/strength endurance. I even did a couple A/B checks on similar sessions and one day tried a full two-serving dose just to rule out “maybe I’m underdosing,” and it still didn’t move the needle in a way I could reliably notice.

So for me it ends up being mostly taste/tingles without the payoff. Maybe it’s the specific form/source quality of the stimulants or the overall blend balance, but I can’t recommend it based on performance.

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: BrainiLex | Clinically Tested Brain Supplement | Memory, Focus, Learn, Perform | 90 Count
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text:
I thought after using this for a week after finishing the regular Braini formula that I was on to something really great. I really thought this would be a better solution for me.

Compared to the regular formula, this is three capsules instead of two capsules per serving. If you look quickly you may not notice this major difference and how it contributes to an obviously higher amount of each ingredient in the formula (925 mg vs 700 mg)

Further, this adds Omega-3 DHA and Zinc to the formula. I am not sure which of these factors led to it working better for me, but I would probably be safe to assume it was the overall serving size versus what I was seeing with the original formula.

All said, this had a decent impact on my focus the first couple of weeks of use. After a month, however, I wasn't able to notice anything compared to if I missed a day. I tried this by using it five days a week and taking two off. Sure, there are always traces of supplements that remain for "off days", but I got to a point where I didn't feel too much different. That has been magnified in the time since I emptied the bottle. I really want to think that these ingredients, unique that they are, could be more beneficial than they are.

Is it worth a shot? Absolutely. Their brain test and money back guarantee is pretty interesting. If you are so inclined, I think it's worth trying this out. Otherwise, there are many other proven ingredients that you could either take individually or combine into your own stack for goals trying to be reached with BrainiLex.
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.best_prediction_review:
I went through almost a full bottle of BrainiLex (90 count) and tried to keep this as controlled as I could on workdays so I wasn’t just guessing. I started with 1 capsule in the morning with coffee for the first week, then did the full serving for weeks 2-3.

Week 1 I thought I felt a mild “cleaner” focus for the first couple of hours (less bouncing between tabs), but it wasn’t dramatic and it wasn’t consistent enough that I could call it a real win. What mattered more for me is that by week 2 that early signal basically leveled off and I was back to my normal baseline. I even had a couple days where I felt a little more scattered than usual, so I can’t confidently attribute anything positive here.

This might just be a case of ingredient/type variability or it not matching my needs, because I’ve had other cognitive supplements where the difference is obvious within the first 10-14 days. With this one, I don’t have a clear “before/after,” and that’s the dealbreaker at this price point. If it was cheaper I’d say it’s worth a personal trial, but as-is I can’t recommend it based on my results.

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: Braini, Capsules and 30 Day Challenge, Clinically Tested Brain Supplement, Stimulant & Caffeine Free Energy, Supports Mental Focus, Improves Brain Age Performance, 30 Servings
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text:
There is a lot to be said for this supplement. I thought it did a pretty decent job within the first two weeks but then leveled off for the rest of the 30 day period. I found that I could measure performance being marginally better when using this versus when I took a few days off after. It was on the third day after that I knew that something about this supplement did in fact work for me.

That said, I find that there are other options that provide better value and better results. In fact, their other product (which is a little stronger in three capsule form) is significantly stronger than this one. However, I still find other options are available with other ingredients that work as well for me at a lower cost.

My suggestion would be to try this product first, take the 30 day challenge (I did not take it) and if it works great for you, awesome! If it works pretty well and you're fine continuing something, try the Braini LEX formula they also have.
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.best_prediction_review:
I ran this Braini “30 Day Challenge” for a full month and kept it pretty controlled (same sleep schedule, same morning coffee, no other new supplements).

For me this was basically a non-response. The first 10-14 days I thought my focus felt a little “cleaner,” but when I tried a simple on/off check (skipped a couple days, then restarted) I couldn’t reliably tell a difference either way. If I have to squint to notice it, it’s not doing the job I bought it for.

I also compared it against my experience with Braini’s regular formula, and that one gave me a more obvious early impact. This 30 Day version might fit someone who just wants something very mild, but if you’re expecting a noticeable change in focus/mental energy, I don’t think this is it.

Because it didn’t produce repeatable results, I can’t justify the cost and won’t repurchase.

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: Vitamin C Facial Skincare Set with Toner and Moisturizing Serum - Organic, Holistic, Healthy
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text:
This is a nice overall skincare set with the serum being the better half of it for many reasons.

I'll start with the scent. I really, really like the scent of the serum. It's a lemon type of smell that works well for me. It absorbs pretty well and is drawn in for the most part by the accompanying toner.

The toner isn't my favorite. It doesn't absorb very well on my skin and I find that my face is shiny about 2-3 hours after applying. I thought at first maybe the serum was the contributing factor, but found that when I used just the toner there was no difference. However, with just using the serum, I did not have the same issue.

All said though, there are some great ingredients in both of these products. I think that other than the greasy feeling, I like what it did for my skin tone and consistency. I would recommend it with reservation.
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been using this Vitamin C toner + serum set for a little over 3 weeks, once nightly (cleanse, toner, then 2 pumps serum). I eased in every other night the first week.

Effect-wise it’s been modest but real: around days 10–14 I saw a little brightening and slightly more even tone, mostly on dullness in my cheek area. It’s not a “wow” transform, but it’s a noticeable small upgrade if you’re consistent.

The deciding issue for me is the feel/finish. I assumed the serum was the shiny/greasy culprit, so I isolated it for a few nights and it was actually the toner that left that tacky, slightly glossy layer on my skin. The serum alone sinks in better and is more comfortable, but together I don’t like how my face feels when I’m trying to go to bed.

If you’re fine with a dewy finish, you’ll probably be happier than I was. I’ll keep using the serum, but I’m on the fence about repurchasing the set.

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: Aggressive Labz Nootropic Focus Support - Nootropadren Neuro Matrix - Enhance Productivity, Mental Focus, Prolonged Energy and Cognition - with ALCAR, Alpha-GPC and More - 30 Servings
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text:
I have gone through the entire bottle of this supplement and can report that nothing changed for me. I didn't feel increased energy and my focus was no different. In fact, during this time (and there were some other factors I am sure) I felt LESS focused.

I am not sure what it could be other than the fact that some of the ingredients can be obtained from a number of different sources. There is a lot of diversity in the "types" and quality of these ingredients. That's not to say that all of these ingredients are of poor quality. It's more likely that it wasn't the "type" that worked well for me. Unfortunately I can't recommend the product since it didn't work.
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.best_prediction_review:
I ran this for the full 30 servings (1 scoop in about 10-12 oz water, mornings) to see if it would give me a clean, repeatable “locked in” focus or productivity bump. For me it was basically a nonresponse. No meaningful change in attention, no noticeable mental drive, and whatever “energy” I got was no different than a normal coffee day.

I even stopped for a few days and restarted to see if I could catch an on/off contrast, and still couldn’t. I’m not saying the formula is junk, but in my case it didn’t deliver a measurable effect, which makes the cost per serving hard to justify when other nootropics show up within a few uses. Unless you already know this ingredient profile works for you, I’d spend elsewhere.

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: Black Seed Oil Organic Cumin-Capsules Black Cumin Seed Oil, Cold Pressed, Nigella Sativa Extract, Kalonji Oil, Black Seed, Hair&Skin Omegas - 60CT Black Seed Oil Capsules, Herbal Goodness, USA Made
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text:
I am a huge black seed oil (Nigella Sativa) fan and have used it for YEARS. I don't use it for skin care, although it definitely helps in that regard too. For me, it's all about building my immune system up as well as I can. This is one of the core ingredients in doing so.

The quality of this seems to be good. I do like the fact that they were able to use an extract, which can result in a better "use" of this supplement. Sure, drinking the liquid straight out of a bottle does have advantages, but you really negate that when you're able to extract the right "stuff" that matters to the goals you have.

I would recommend this brand and product based on the fact that it seems to have worked as well for me as my other favorites. With that said, it must be pretty good. :)
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.best_prediction_review:
I ran through the full bottle of these black seed oil capsules at 2/day with food, mainly for the inflammation/immune support angle and to see if it helped my skin/hair dryness at all. Tolerability was fine for me (no nausea, no “oil burps”), and the capsules were easy enough to swallow, but I can’t honestly say I got any noticeable benefit.

No change in skin dryness, no clear difference in how I felt day to day, and nothing that stood out enough that I could tell if I missed a day. That doesn’t mean black seed oil “doesn’t work,” but with herbs like this the source/type/actual potency of the oil/extract can vary a lot, and I’m not convinced this particular bottle delivered anything I could measure.

Since I finished it with a clean nonresponse, I wouldn’t repurchase based on results. If you’re sensitive, at least it was easy on my stomach.

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: Dose & Co Collagen Protein Powder (Chocolate Fudge) 14.8oz (420g) – Non-GMO, Gluten Free, Sugar Free Collagen Peptides Supplement – 20g of Whey Protein
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text:
There are so many collagen powders available to put in the coffee. I measure my success by a couple of areas: flavor and how well it settles into my coffee. Many of the collagen powders I have tried clump at the bottom and don't stir in effectively. Then some that do blend in well don't have the best flavor. Yes, I have had some vanilla and other flavors that I really like, but this one takes the cake...or is it the fudge?

This collagen has a really good chocolate flavor. It doesn't dissolve perfectly, but it doesn't clump in the bottom of my coffee as so many other collagens have in the past. I get a little bit of the powder in my last sip, but it's acceptable given the great flavor. I think that the addition of protein (whey and milk) is great and at 20g per serving, you're getting quite a boost there. So that is a definite bonus for me.

Overall, this is one of my favorite collagens to date. I find that I like a lot of them I have tried more recently, so I will keep this review updated if anything changes.
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.best_prediction_review:
I’ve tried a lot of collagen/protein powders and ran this Dose & Co Chocolate Fudge through my normal test: hot coffee first (because that’s where most “mixes fine” powders fail), then cold almond milk.

In hot coffee it’s mostly fine if you whisk it in fast, but it does thicken a bit and I still get some sediment in the last few sips. Not the worst I’ve had, but it’s not the “disappears completely” experience either.

In cold almond milk it does better. The chocolate fudge flavor is actually pleasant and not overly sweet, and it’s easy to drink daily. For me, that usability matters more than chasing a perfect mix.

The bigger win here is the 20g protein per serving. If you’re buying this as a “collagen plus real protein” scoop, it pulls its weight. If you’re expecting a pure collagen peptide that vanishes into any drink, this isn’t that.

I’d recommend it with reservation: good taste and solid protein payload, but expect a little settling in coffee.

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: Gym Molly After Party with Caffeine 7g BCAA Powder - Workout Drink Supplement for Fitness Recovery | Contains 750 MG Coconut Electrolytes and Zero Carbs, Sugar, or Calories | Blue Raspberry Lemonade
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text:
This is one of the better initial tasting post-workouts I have tried. Yes, there is that BCAA aftertaste that happens with nearly every supplement. However, in this one it is not as pronounced. I otherwise really enjoy the flavor of this After Party.

Without this it is more like a hangover, so it's a great play on words with the product name. This does keep things going and has kept me from crashing or otherwise being sore. Unlike many other post-workouts, this one has caffeine in it as well. That is a nice thing to have to keep things flowing for another half hour or so after finishing a workout.

I would definitely recommend this based on the ingredients alone, and would further recommend it on the fact that it works. I'll give a five star recommendation to anyone who can figure out the after taste plus give me the ingredients and impact of a great intra or post workout supplement.
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.best_prediction_review:
I used this as a post-workout drink for about 2 weeks (4 lifting days/week) and kept everything else pretty consistent. Mixed in a shaker with ~16 oz cold water it dissolved better than I expected for a BCAA drink and I didn’t end up with gritty sediment.

The flavor is very close to blue raspberry lemonade, but it is SWEET for something with zero sugar/calories. If you like that “candy” sweetness you’ll probably love it, but it’s intense and I wouldn’t want it any stronger than their suggested mix.

As far as the actual “after party” effect, it’s more of a light reset than a noticeable recovery game-changer for me.
