I didn’t feel anything dramatic in soreness or next-day performance, but I also didn’t get any weird stomach issues or a rough caffeine hit. Overall it’s a decent tasting, easy-to-drink option, just don’t expect it to be some magical recovery switch.

AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.product_description: Vegan Organic Apple Cider Vinegar Gummies Vitamins by Clever Life Rx, Dietary Supplement - Raw, Unfiltered Healthy Detox & Weight, Support Immunity Gummy (60 Count with The Mother) B9, B12 Cranberry
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.review_text: These gummies were very disappointing to us. They appeared to be broken apart, the taste was inconsistent across the board, and I just simply don't trust them to be effective. Sadly we ended up discarding the bottle because of what we saw as potentially poor quality control. Perhaps they had melted in transit? I have no idea, but I can't recommend this.
AHKDB7ZTU7PAKM3DPDCJWHQPUP3Q.best_prediction_review: I tried these ACV gummies for about 3 weeks (2/day) for digestion and any “detox/weight” claim. They taste more apple/cranberry than vinegar, so don’t expect that ACV bite. I did a quick stop/restart and the only repeatable change was slightly less bloat after heavier meals. No appetite shift and no scale change. No stomach irritation for me, but the results were mild enough that I wouldn’t repurchase for anything beyond light digestion support.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: Hair Dryer Brush, 5 in 1 Hair Deyer Styler, Black + Uv & Rubber Oil, Electric 3 Modes Blow Dryer Styler, One Step Hair Dryer Hair Straightener Brush and Blow Dryer Brush with Alci Plug for Women, Wig
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text:
I had a similar product which was recalled (rightly so as it heated up tremendously), this does not do that.
First it’s really meant to style not dry your hair. I towel dry, use a conventional blow dryer for a couple of minutes till damp then switch to the hot air brush and the decrease in frizz is awesome.
Really easy to use, especially for the back (yeah). I love it! It literally take no longer than five minutes even if I use my heated brush all told I’m in the 10 minute range, quite remarkable for curly, dense frizzy hair. Highly recommended.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review:
This is really a “style/finish” tool, not a true from-soaking-wet dryer. I towel dry or rough dry first, then use this to smooth and cut frizz quickly and it does a nice job of getting hair presentable in a few minutes. The 3 heat/speed modes are easy to toggle and it’s simple to use section by section.

Safety was my main gate with hot brush dryers (scalp burns are real). This one hasn’t gotten scary-hot at the bristles, and I can keep it moving near the roots without that “too hot” feeling, but I still wouldn’t park it in one spot. If you expect a full blowout on very wet/thick hair, this isn’t that—use it as the final step and it’s great.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: Replacement Toothbrush Heads for Phillips Sonicare : Brush Heads Compatible with Sonicare ProtectiveClean Plaque Control Electric Toothbrush 4100 5100 6100 C2 C3 HX9023,8 Pack
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text: These fit both my sonicare & generic brand power toothbrush and I’ve been using various generic brands for years and it has not affected the longevity of my power toothbrushes. I like the average to smaller head and soft bristles as this combination is best for oral health. The blue fades over time as it should but these are generously priced so I just change monthly….yes I do! Highly recommend.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review: These fit my Sonicare ProtectiveClean 6100 perfectly—click on solid, no wobble, and no weird grinding that makes me worry about wearing out the handle over time (that’s my big concern with generics). Head size is comparable to the OEM C2/C3 and the bristles are soft enough for the gumline but still remove plaque well. About 3 weeks in at 2x/day and they’re holding shape with minimal splaying/shedding. For an 8-pack the price per head is excellent, assuming they keep this up to my usual change-out schedule.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: QUACOWW 400 Pieces Dental Floss Picks, Flossers for Adults, Dental Care, Unwaxed Dental Floss, Dental Floss Picks, Access Flosser for Tooth Cleaning at Home
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text:
Can’t beat the price or quantity (400) excellent.
They are thin and the pointed end is quite quick to bend. The floss is thin and malleable, happy to report little fraying or breakage. I love floss picks. My patients are more likely to use this than any other flossing tool. Men in particular will use will driving. These are better quality than some store brands (CVS & Rite Aid). Highly recommended.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review:
These are my “car/bathroom” floss picks because I’ll actually use them when I’m rushed (vs string floss I skip). The unwaxed floss is thin enough to get through tight contacts and it doesn’t just slide around, it actually cleans. The one downside is the pointed end/handle tip bends pretty quickly on back molars, but the floss strand itself has held up fine.

400 picks for the price is excellent value, and the bag arrived sealed/clean. If you want something convenient you’ll realistically keep up with, these do the job.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: Probiotics 50 Billion CFU | 60 Capsules | with Prebiotics for Men & Women | Non-GMO & Gluten Free Supplement | by Zurich Research
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text: Mixed strains of good bacteria, excellent price and high count =equals everything you want in a probiotic. No gas, no diarrhea but helpful in maintaining or achieving regularity. Normal size capsules, easy to swallow. No refrigeration. Recommended.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review: I’m picky with probiotics—if they cause gas/diarrhea it’s a no. This one has been easy on my stomach and I did notice more consistent regularity. I like that it’s a higher CFU with prebiotics and a full 60 capsules; feels like everything you want without buying 3 bottles. Capsule size is normal and simple to stick to. Price is excellent for the count/potency.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: Sonic Facial Cleansing Brush-Waterproof Silicone Face Scrubber for Women, Facial Deep Cleansing and Massage, Exfoliating
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text:
Small enough to travel with and keep in the shower or on the counter for daily use. Compares well with Popsonic facial cleaner except Popsonic has a larger facial footprint and it’s charge literally lasted a year (crazy huh).
Very soft silicone and non abrasive on my skin (clarasonic truly did a number on my skin). It cleans beautifully, just wonderful for makeup removal and the brush itself is quite easy to clean. Really love it in the shower. Very good value, gift able also. Recommended.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review:
I bought this silicone sonic face brush because I wanted something gentler than a bristle brush but still effective. Used with my usual cleanser for about 60 seconds, it does a better job loosening sunscreen/makeup around the nose and chin without leaving my skin feeling scrubbed-raw. The vibration is strong enough to matter but not obnoxious.

It’s simple (one button) and the silicone feels more like a massage than a “scrub.” Rinses clean fast and I’m comfortable using it in the shower. For the price it’s a solid little tool, but I do wish the button/indicator was easier to see when it’s wet and soapy.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: Ball of Foot Cushions 6 Pairs - Metatarsal Pads Forefoot Pad - Metatarsal Cushion Morton’s Neuroma - Metatarsal Foot Pads - DAILISHA Soft Silicone Forefoot Cushion Pads for Women
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text: These a thin but functional and a little tricky to put on because of the thinness. They could benefit from some heft but again are functional just the same. I use during workouts since that’s when my feet are most problematic... I see a podiatrist in my future...and use socks over to keep securely in place. They wash extremely well in the dishwasher and they certainly need to be washed after each use so huge benefit to have multiple pairs.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review:
These are soft silicone and actually comfortable against skin (no strong gel smell) and I can wear them for a treadmill workout without irritation. They do take the edge off that ball-of-foot pressure, but don’t expect a dramatic “off-load” unless your shoes have a little room—these are fairly low profile and I do wish they were a touch thicker.

They’re simple to use, but the real operating trick is wearing a snug sock over them; otherwise they can migrate in tighter sneakers. Cleaning is easy: I just wash with soap/water after use and they dry quickly, so they feel sanitary enough to re-wear.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: Skyfoot’s Silicone Gel Non-Slip Heel Cups Protector,Heel Cushion Insoles,for Plantar Fasciitis,Bone Spurs,Heel Pain Relief ,Shock Absorption, for Kids & Women & Men (1 Pair) (M)
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text: I only use when my daily routine includes a treadmill workout as my feet take a bit of a beating. These offer a good shock absorption, fit into my sneakers without adding bulk, stay in place and add noticeable comfort. I wouldn’t mind a bit more thickness for better performance. The silicone is flexible and soft. They wash extremely well in the dishwasher.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review:
I use these silicone heel cups only for treadmill workouts when my heels act up. The gel is very soft and takes the edge off impact without feeling bulky in sneakers, but I don’t love them barefoot (they can shift); with socks they stay put.

They rinse clean easily, and I run them on the top rack of the dishwasher after each use for a real sanitize—no odor and they’ve held up fine so far. Simple, effective for that narrow use case.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: Homesprit Strap Extender 12 PCS Reusable 3D Face Nose Mouth Bracket to Increase Space and 50 PCS Adjustable Strap Extender Non-Slip Ear Saver Strap Extension Ear Hook
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text:
The lanyard is sort of a throw away but if you remember it (and remember to wash it!) it can be useful. (Try hanging it from your rear view mirror with a mask attached.
The items are being shared and reviewed by a family of healthcare workers (RDH, MD, EMT, RN) for professional and personal use.
None seem to be fans of nose/mouth brackets in the professional arena, it’s just another layer of PPE but for teachers and retail it surely is a matter of preference and this package offers a wide variety of products to try at a fantastic price.
The ear savers (mask extender) are stiff and thin and we liked these as they stayed in place much better than those made of silicon. (The general consensus is I’ll use most earsavers!)
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review:
Bought this for long clinic days when ear loops start tugging. The strap extenders are the only part I keep using: they hook on fast, the notches actually hold, and the plastic is stiff enough that it doesn’t flop around or pop off mid-shift. They work with most standard disposable ear-loop masks I tried, but they’re not universal—some thicker/shorter-loop styles don’t sit right, so don’t assume “fits all.”

The 3D brackets (the nose/mouth cages) were a miss for me. They add bulk, they’re another thing to remember, and anything sitting inside the mask becomes a cleaning issue. I’m strict about sanitizing (UV/dishwasher-safe routines), and these aren’t a set I want to fuss with daily. For the price/quantity it’s okay if you mainly want the ear savers; I wouldn’t buy it for the brackets.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: Pill Case - Portable Travel Medicine Organizer and Holder for Purse or Bag. Daily Pill Container. Suitable for All Size Medications, Vitamins or Supplements. 4 Compartments (Sage Green)
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text: If you’re using this in your purse it’s a decent footprint for a pillbox. However, great lock and separation areas. If I have to grab this while I’m driving (ya, ya, ya, I know), no way can I open it, take that when you’re migraine prone and can’t grab an aspirin. Regardless, it’s chunky big. It’s also a little (more than a little) overpriced for what it is, a well made, bulky piece of functional of plastic  with a snap.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review:
I keep this in my work bag for “just in case” meds and a couple supplements and it’s a big upgrade from baggies. The 4 compartments are actually usable (a few larger capsules + smaller tablets) and the lid snaps shut with a positive click—no accidental openings so far.

The hinge/closure feels sturdy, and I can open it without feeling like I’m going to crack it, but it’s not truly effortless one-handed when you’re distracted. Also, it’s a little bulkier than I expected for a purse pocket, so it’s another item you have to remember and make space for. Price is fair for the build, and I’d buy it again.

AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.product_description: New Silicone 3D Bracket for Comfortable Wearing by Creating More Space for Breathing Ideal Makeup Saver (3)
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.review_text:
Pros
Easy to clean with in the dishwasher or UV sanitizer
Thin
Affordable
Comfortable (subjective)

Con
Yet another item to remember to take with you
Requires some maneuvering to keep in place while placing mask over
Does not work/fit with all types of masks, Tommy Copper and level 1masks -yes.....average size flu mask—nay.

I honestly wouldn’t bother. I found it to be not worth the hassle and awkward.
AE4YB4AE5WZMIZHVUVRP3TXCK5QA.best_prediction_review: These silicone mask brackets do help keep fabric off your lips (less damp feeling, easier to talk) and they’re easy to wash/sanitize. But they’re not universal: they sit better in structured/surgical masks; in looser cloth masks they shift and I end up fiddling with it. Also it’s another thing to remember to carry/clean, which matters on long shifts. Soft silicone, no odor, no irritation for me. Fine, but not a must-have.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: MHSY Electric Razor for Women, Shaver for Women Pubic Hair with 3 in 1 Wet & Dry Painless Rechargeable Hair Remover for Legs Underarms and Bikini Trimmer Line
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: This rechargeable electric razor does look and feel cheap as others have mentioned. I do like the shape of it as it is easy to hold. I wish there was some texture on the body to provide a better grip. I used this to shave the bikini area and I thought it did a great job. The size of the shaver head was perfect for me for that area (much easier to maneuver compared to my old battery standard sized shaver head which I'd been using for years). I didn't experience any snagging of the hair as others have mentioned. The hair was pretty long but I just went over it with a light hand until it got short and then I got a close shave. I like that one side of the blade is curved for the under arms and bikini area (although I did those areas just fine with the straight side too). I think the biggest thing that I don't like is the way the blade/ foil is removed in order to clean. Mine was a mess inside because there was some kind of lubricant/vaseline type substance in there and the hairs were all stuck. I had to wipe all that gunk out. It wasn't the easiest the clean compared to my old shaver. I feel like this new one is not really meant to be cleaned or it would've had a better design to open up the shaver head. Another issue is that this is not to be used in or around water which is kind of inconvenient. I think the only thing I would put under water is the blade/foil once it's removed from the body. I wouldn't trust rinsing it under water while attached to the body like they show in the product video. On my unit when the blade/ foil is removed, I see a little piece of sponge/ foam in the center and underneath that it looks like it's opened into the handle. Definitely don't want to get water in there. I wish there was an included protective cover like my other shaver. I like that it is rechargeable by using the included micro USB cord. The handle glows red when it's charging and turns off when it's done. There is more to be desired but it gets the job done so it's not a total waste. I also don't know why the box says 'epilator' because it is not.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review:
This is definitely a shaver/trimmer set-up, not an epilator, so don’t buy it expecting “pulls from the root” results. What I received was a small rechargeable handle with 3 heads (foil shaver + trimmer style heads) and a little brush. It feels lightweight but not flimsy, and the attachments click on/off without feeling like they’ll snap if you’re careful.

I tested it dry first on lower legs and underarms, then very cautiously on the bikini line. It takes hair down close enough to feel smooth to the touch, but it’s not the same as a fresh blade razor for that truly baby-smooth finish. Where it does better (for me) is safety: I didn’t get any nicks, and it’s much less intimidating around looser skin. I still found that performance is very geometry-dependent — it works best when you keep the skin taut and go in shorter passes; if the skin is looser it can miss a bit and you have to go back over it.

Cleaning is the part I’m pickier about. Hair gets trapped inside the head and you do have to pop the head apart and brush it out, otherwise it starts to feel like it’s dragging. Because of that (and because I don’t want wet hair/gunk working its way into the handle), I personally wouldn’t treat this as a true “rinse under the faucet and forget it” wet-use tool even if it’s marketed that way. I’m keeping it as a dry-use shaver/trimmer and doing quick cleanouts after each use, which makes it feel more hygienic and keeps it cutting consistently.

Overall it’s a decent option if your priority is a low-risk shave for underarms/bikini line and you’re okay with “close” rather than razor-level smooth. If you want perfectly smooth legs in one pass, I’d still reach for a regular razor.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: USUIE Red Light Therapy Belt, Infrared Light
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: This was packaged nicely with removable Velcro elastic straps.  It automatically shuts off after 20 mins I believe. There are 3 different light settings. I layed the pad over my feet & ankles after coming back from a 5 mile walk. I was sore so I figured this was the perfect opportunity to try it. I did a few sessions for the next few days and my feet & ankles felt better. Maybe they would've felt better on it own anyway but it was soothing since it gives off a warmth feeling. I just wish the size was slightly larger but this size is good if you need to wrap it around your arm or leg. This is lightweight and portable and easy to travel with. It came with a long USB-C cord and a short one. I use a portable battery pack and it works just fine.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review:
I’ve only had this red light/infrared belt a short time, so I’m not calling it “proved pain relief” yet. What I can say is it behaves like a plug-in heat/light wrap: you plug it in, select a setting/time, and it shuts off on its own, which makes it easy to use without babysitting.

My quick trial was after a long walk when my feet/ankles were sore. I wrapped it around each ankle for a session and got a noticeable gentle warmth/soothing effect right away. It didn’t feel like an instant fix, but it was the kind of calming heat that made me more comfortable.

Fit/contact is the main gate. It wraps and stays put better on straight areas than on joints, and if the edges lift you’re not getting consistent coverage. I also wouldn’t use it over broken/irritated skin. Overall: straightforward workflow and pleasant warmth; longer-term results still unknown.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: Healing Crystal Set Polished Palm Stones Pocket Crystals Worry Stones Collectible Figurines for Meditation, Collection, Chakra Healing & Home Decor (Heart)
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: This is such a nice set of quality polished healing crystals in a heart shape. I like collecting different crystals & stones and this is a great addition. The colors are vibrant, there are no visible cracks in any of the pieces. They were packaged in a velour drawstring pouch which was wrapped in bubble wrap and packaged in a cardboard box. This could be gifted as is, just wrap it up nice and you're good to go since there's no thank you card from the seller or printing on the box like you often find nowadays.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review:
This is a small heart-shaped polished palm stone. Mine arrived smooth and evenly rounded with no sharp edges, and the polish is nice (cool in the hand at first, then warms up). It’s truly pocket-size and has held up fine being tossed in my bag for a few days without obvious scratching.

Packaging was simple (plastic + small box) but presentable enough to gift if you rewrap it. I’m not using it for any “healing” claims—just as a worry stone/desk fidget—and for that it does the job.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: Blissful Being Pink Himalayan Salt Massage Stone Set - Himalayan Salt Stones for Massage- Saltabilty (Heart Shape)
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: These are a set of 2 beautiful pink Himalayan salt heart-shaped stones. They are very large and heavy. It came packaged in an organza drawstring pouch and sealed in a cardboard box. I don't plan on using these for massage purposes. I will be displaying them. I think they would be fine for massage since they are smooth and fit nicely in the palm.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review: These heart shaped Himalayan salt stones are really pretty and feel smooth in the hand with a little natural texture. I mainly bought them as a small gift/display item and they look nice in the pouch/box without weird seller inserts. I did a quick “bench test” glide on my shoulder with a light oil and it moved fine once warmed, but I wouldn’t buy this expecting real massage therapy results.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: Exfoliating Mitt & Back Scrubber Set for Shower, Body Scrubber Gloves for Bath, Deep Cleaning Acne and Dead Skin Removal
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: This is a nice exfoliating scrubbing set. The universal mitt (not a fitted glove) has a bit of cushioning compared to the back scrubber. Both provide just enough scrub without feeling too rough and uncomfortable. I do wish the back scrubber was just a bit longer. It is not stretchy so it might take some time to get used to it especially if you don't have much flexibility in your arms (if you had a injury and have less mobility & range of motion). Both rinses clean easily.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review: This exfoliating set is pretty straightforward and works well. The mitt has enough texture to lift dead skin (especially after a few minutes in a warm shower) and it helped smooth out some rough bumps on my arms without feeling like sandpaper. The back scrubber is long enough for mid-back, but if you’re trying to reach your entire back or you have limited shoulder mobility, it may still come up a little short. Handles didn’t dig into my hands. I rinsed everything and hung it to dry; it dried overnight and didn’t stay funky.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: HOUSTASK Painless Bug Bite Suction Tool,Poison Remover - Bug Bites and Bee/Wasp Stings, Natural Insect Bite Relief
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: I was intrigued by this product. I get very bad reactions to mosquito bites (I swell so badly & get cellulitis) so I wondered if this would work. Of course I'd rather not get bit by any kind of insect so I hope I never have to use this. I did try it out on random areas to test out the suction. I guess it's decent. The suction was stronger on areas of looser and not taut skin. The pieces can come apart easily if you're not careful but it's easy to put back together. I will update if I ever get bit by anything so see if this product helps.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review:
I got this mainly for mosquito bites so I can avoid slathering creams every time. What arrived was a small plastic suction tool with a couple tip sizes; mine came clean and intact in simple packaging.

My quick test so far is strictly the mechanism: the suction is strong enough to feel “pull,” but it wasn’t painful. Where it seems to matter is skin geometry—on flatter/taut areas (forearm) it holds a seal and you get consistent suction. On softer/curvier spots it tends to lose the seal and the pull is uneven.

I haven’t had a fresh, truly itchy bite yet to judge real relief, so I’m keeping the verdict open until I can use it immediately after a bite/sting.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: Oopee Toilet Room Spray - Odor Eliminator For Strong Odor Room Fresheners - Toilet Spray For Poop Odor Eliminator - Air Fresheners For Home Room Fresheners - Travel Size Bathroom Spray - Pear Scent
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: I could smell this right out of the package. In fact my hand smelled like it for a while. I think the bottle leaked slightly as the pump looked wet but the box was dry. I shook the bottle and sprayed it twice into the toilet. I couldn't really smell the scent at that point. I've tried this with formed stools which don't really smell in first place. Then I tried it with soft stool/ diarrhea and it masked the smell but not completely. Maybe if I sprayed more into the toilet it would've completely masked the smell but I didn't want to go through the whole bottle so quickly. Overall I think it works for the most part.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review:
This is basically a “spray the bowl water first” poop-odor blocker, not just an air freshener. In my small bathroom, 2 sprays into the toilet before use noticeably reduced the odor, so it’s doing something. On a stronger worst-case (looser stool), it didn’t fully contain everything unless I used more sprays, and I wouldn’t rely on it as a guaranteed seal.

The pear scent is pleasant and not super chemical, but it is still fragrance, so if you’re sensitive you’ll want to start with 1 spray. The bottle is small/travel-friendly and came sealed with no leaks, and the packaging was simple (no extra junk), which I appreciate.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: 42" Foldable Grabber Reacher Tool with Light, Trash Picker Grabber with Magnet Tips, 90°Rotating Anti-Slip Jaw, Senior Living Aids Tool, Long Arm Extension
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: This is a long reach grabber that's 10 inches longer than a regular reach grabber. I was excited to stumble across this because I have 30-32 inch grabbers and always said "only if it was just a little bit longer". The handle is easy to grip and squeeze. The grabber folds in half for easy storage. The grabber has a light source which is a nice feature but it's not something that I need. It came with a small screw driver and 3 button cell batteries  that you would have to install yourself. There is an on & off switch located near the battery door located where the grabber arms are. It also has a magnetic pickup built into one of the arms. It did take some effort to extend the grabber fully for the first time. There is an attached velcro strap to keep the grabber folded in half. The grabber arms can be rotated if needed.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review:
This 42” grabber is noticeably longer than the basic reacher I’ve had for years, and that extra length is the whole reason I wanted it. It arrives as a foldable unit with the light already attached, and it folds down small enough to stash in a car or closet without being awkward.

The rotating head is actually useful for lining the jaws up without having to twist your wrist, and the anti-slip jaw has been precise enough for small stuff like wrappers/paper but also grippy on slightly heavier items. The magnet tips are a nice bonus for grabbing keys/screws, but I wouldn’t buy it just for that.

Build-wise it feels decent for the price. The folding/locking joints were stiff out of the box, but after a handful of open/close cycles it started feeling more predictable. I’m still a little cautious about forcing the rotation/joints too hard long-term, but so far it’s been a convenient upgrade over my shorter reacher.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: Cloth Face Mask 4ply Washable Reusable,Adjustable Earloop Unisex Adult Dust-Proof Face Mask,Flower-F,2PCS
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: This is a thicker mask with adjustable ear loops and filter pocket. It is also a larger mask. On my smaller face, the fabric practically reaches ear to ear on me and I have to make the ear loops very small. With that being said, this mask should fit a wide range of face sizes. I do wish it had an adjustable nose piece which is important if you wear glasses because it helps lessens the fogging by creating a tighter seal. This set comes with 2 identical masks and 4 disposable filters.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review:
These are cute masks and the flower print looks like the photos. The fabric is noticeably thicker than my thinner cloth masks (it says 4‑ply) and on my face it covers well over the nose and under the chin without sliding around. The tradeoff is breathability: fine for quick errands, but when I’m walking fast I do feel a little more restricted.

The adjustable ear loops are easy to set and didn’t tug my ears, which is a big deal for me. Fit-wise I have a smaller/narrower face and this runs a bit on the roomy side, so if you like a snug fit you may need to tighten the loops quite a bit. Overall they’re comfortable enough to actually wear and feel like decent everyday dust/coverage masks.

AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.product_description: EIVOTOR Sonic Facial Cleansing Brush with 4 Modes, Waterproof Sonic Vibrating Face Brush for Deep Cleansing, Gentle Exfoliating and Massaging, USB Rechargeable(Pink)
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.review_text: This is a massaging silicone facial brush that not only vibrates but there's also 4 magnetic beads that rotate in the center which makes it different than most silicone vibrating brushes. It is waterproof to an extent and it recharges with its proprietary USB charging cord. I do wish it was a standard micro USB or a USB-C cord instead. The device is easy to hold and because of the cute flower design, a "petal" can easily reach the sides of nose, above my upper lip area, temple areas and chin. The device will automatically turn off in 90 seconds and when you turn it back on, it goes back to the mode where you left off. There are 4 modes and I like to use the first 2 modes (gentle or deep cleaning both with magnetic bead massage). I noticed that my face felt soft & smooth after use. I do find the power button which doubles as the mode button is really small and could be difficult to press. To plug the charging cord in, there is a tiny slit in the silicone that covers the opening and I did find that I had to gently wiggle the plug around to find the correct spot internally to actually plug into the device (instead of jamming it in and risk damaging it). Device also comes with a drawstring storage / travel bag which was a nice surprise.
AGROJMVDIUS3PBURBTV5MX5CVKSA.best_prediction_review:
This is a small silicone sonic face brush (pink) and it came with the USB charging cord. It’s basically the common “silicone vibration cleanser” class (not a spinning brush), with a single button to cycle through the 4 intensity/mode levels. The charging port is on the handle with a little flap; it seals fine but you do have to make sure it’s fully seated closed before using it wet.

In actual use it’s been easy to add into my routine. I use a gentle cleanser and mostly stick to the lowest/second mode. The vibrations are noticeably different as you go up, but I don’t feel like I need the highest setting on my face. It feels more like a light exfoliating/massaging assist than anything aggressive, which for me is a plus.

Comfort-wise the silicone nubs are soft and it hasn’t irritated my skin so far (I wouldn’t use this over active breakouts or if you’re already sensitized). Battery-wise, after the initial charge it’s been holding up over multiple washes without me thinking about it, which is what I want from a small rechargeable tool.

Overall I think it’s a decent, straightforward sonic cleanser if you want something compact and gentle. If you’re expecting a heavy-duty “deep scrub” like a bristled or spinning brush, this isn’t that type of device, but as an everyday option it’s worked well for me.

AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.product_description: Sleep Eye Mask for Men Women, 3D Contoured Cup Sleeping Mask, Concave Molded Night Perfect Blindfold, Protect Eyelashs, Soft Comfort Silk Eye Shade Cover for Travel/Nap/Shift Work
AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.review_text: This is a great sleep mask! I've been using a mask for years and I'm pretty picky. This is comfortable on the face and softens up over time. The contour is awesome, I can't imagine going back to just regular flat masks. It gives you room for rapid eye movements while you sleep. Overall very pleased.
AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.best_prediction_review: I bought this mainly for naps/travel and the 3D cups are the real win—my eyelids/lashes don’t get smashed and I can blink without feeling pressure. It blocks light well without having to crank the strap down, and the fabric feels smooth (no scratchy seams). It’s also quick to adjust and stays put, and I didn’t get that tight “mask headache” I sometimes do with flat masks.

AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.product_description: HEAVY DUTY Stainless Steel Cleaner and Polish for Pots, Pans, Clad 16oz – Removes Stains, Burnt Food, Tarnish – Pot Cleaning Kit – Includes Scouring Cloth for Scrubbing
AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.review_text: This worked really well on a pan I had some burns in as well as my stainless steel dishwasher. It does say "not recommended on stainless steel exterior". I couldn't find the exact reason on the label. I assume its so you don't dull a super shiny exterior but my dishwasher is a satin finish. I used a gentle hand and just a little product and it came out great. It even got rid of a few spots I thought might be unremovable. I had to use some elbow grease for the pan but it came out clean. I would say it's a lot like bar keepers friend powder but in liquid form as far as the texture and use. The scouring clothes stood out. Didn't scratch anything but made cleaning easier.
AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.best_prediction_review:
I bought this to rescue a couple stainless pots with a brown heat ring and some stuck-on burnt rice. A little goes a long way (about a nickel-sized amount), and it spreads easily. What I liked most is the included scouring cloth — it has enough bite to actually lift the discoloration and cooked-on spots, but it didn’t leave scratch marks on my pans the way some scrubbers can.

I did need a second pass on the worst area, but it still took less effort than my usual Bar Keepers Friend + baking soda routine, and cleanup was simple (rinse well and dry). If you want something that makes stainless look presentable again without feeling like you’re sanding it down, this worked well for me.

AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.product_description: Wellmo Elderberry Immunity Gummies for Men, Women, and Children - All-Natural Elderberry Extract Gummies with Vitamin C and Zinc for Immune System Support - 75mg of Elderberry Extract - 60-Day Supply
AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.review_text: I like that these are a combo of all the things I need, elderberry, zinc and vitamin C. They have a good texture and flavor plus they're lower in sugar (1.5 g) than others I've tried. The only con is that none of the ingredients are organic, but if that's not important to you, they're great!
AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.best_prediction_review:
These taste good (fruity, not “medicine”), not overly candy-sweet, and the chew is soft without a weird aftertaste. No stomach upset for us so far.

What I mostly wanted to verify is the formula/label: 75mg elderberry extract plus vitamin C and zinc makes sense as a simple immune-support combo, and the serving math is straightforward. I’m not expecting a dramatic “I felt it” effect from gummies, but as a daily add-on this is easy to stick with. Only caveat: if you’re looking for organic ingredients, this isn’t that.

AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.product_description: Xobo Beet Root Powder Gummies Supplement, 100 Gummy Soft Chews with Organic Ingridients, Natural Nitric Oxide Booster Cleanse Promotes Circulation and Digestive Health, Vegan
AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.review_text: Beet root powder has a lot of healthy benefits and I love beets so I thought these would be a good supplement for me. They're good for heart and brain health. These taste more like a sweetened mild beet, not super beet flavor. I love the taste of beets but I know not everyone does. These are pretty sticky, have a good texture and flavor. I really like them!
AESIZ3ATHVPMZM5NI6ZJOZBH547A.best_prediction_review: I got these because I like beets but hate dealing with beet powder everywhere. These are pleasantly sweet with a light earthy beet taste, and the chew is truly soft (not gritty or sticky), so it’s easy to take daily. I’m not going to oversell the “nitric oxide booster” part, but as a low-key beet option I do feel like it supports circulation/overall wellness. If you want a stronger beet hit, you’ll probably still want straight powder.

AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.product_description: Shoe Sole Protector for Air Jordan 1, Strong Self-Adhesive Heel Pad Non Slip Heels Repair Plates for Men and Women Sneakers and Other Flat Sole Shoes
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.review_text:
[[VIDEOID:fceaf07df1742d003d474a09e5bd82af]] final update: almost 5 1/2 months after first application.

I must say that while I was disappointed that these heel protectors didn't 'glue on' the way I wanted to, I am more than pleased with the longevity of these protectors once they were applied. While I did have to apply some 'touch-up' glue to the protectors a couple of times over the last few months, I did find that these protectors did the main job that were designed to do; that being protecting the heels of my shoes from wearing down as quickly as they would do otherwise. What just happened here recently was that the upper front part of the shoe (where they bend around the toe-line) gave out before the heel protectors worn down to the point where the original heel of the shoe would starting impacting the ground. Without these heel protectors in place, I believe (based on the miles I put on my shoes) that the heels on the shoes would have worn down to replacement level at least one or two months ago. So, I do think these heel protectors work (though they do require some maintenance 'glueing') and am therefore giving the product a couple of extra stars.
---------------------------------------------

update: 2 weeks after first review (see below) ... i completely removed the pads from the tennis/walking/running shoes and then reattached the pads to shoes using a more-than-generous amount of rubber cement (the type used to attach patches to a bike inner-tube). Since that time i've put in excess of 30 miles on the shoes/pads and have had no issues. to me, this indicates that this product --might-- be useful on non-rubber soles, leather for example, but isn't useful on rubber or rubber-like soles like the ones i have on all my tennis/walking/running shoes.

Review of: Miiooan Invisible Shoe Heel Protectors

I’ve found in my experience that the soles of my shoes, especially the heel portion of the bottom of the shoe, normally gives out far before the upper part of my shoes give out; and there have been many times I’ve had to buy a new pair of shoes even though the tops of the shoes were still in good condition.

So, I was really hopeful that by adding these heel protectors I could get some ‘extra time/extra miles’ out of my walking shoes.

What I found, however, was that the very first time I wore shoes with these heel protectors on was that the pads failed right around the 2-mile mark of my planned 6-mile walk on city streets/sidewalks.

To prepare for the first walk, I carefully followed the instructions that came with the heel pads. I used a brand-new pair of tennis shoes/sneakers/walking shoes that had never been worn before, I cleaned the bottom of the shoe (including using rubbing alcohol) and I firmly applied the heels to the bottom of each shoe and I waited 3 days before going on the walk.

After only 2-miles, however, my right foot scrapped across a bit of sidewalk that was slightly raised on one side (by a tree next to the sidewalk) and the front part of the heel pad peeled back and away from the shoe. And, since the glue was ‘hardened’ by that time, the flap of heel pad wouldn’t reattach to the shoe.

What did happen, though, was that the loose flap of heel protector continued to grow to the point where, at right around 2 ½ miles, I had to rip the protector off the shoe just to be able to continue to walk safely down the sidewalk.

And, when I got home and checked the heel protector on the other shoe, I saw that the heel protector on that shoe had also started to pull away from the shoe.

The bottom-line for me is that, in what I consider to be a real-world test of the heel protectors just walking down a city street/city sidewalk, is that one protector failed completely and the second protector was on the verge of failing.

This product, for me, doesn’t come close to getting a passing grade.

If this review was helpful, please click the Helpful button below
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.best_prediction_review:
I picked these up mainly to keep a couple pairs of flat-sole sneakers from getting that “heel drag” wear spot, and to add a little traction on slick floors. I’m not precious about my shoes, but I do try to extend the life of what I own, so I figured it was worth a try.

Application is easy, but it’s not “stick it on and forget it.” The soles need to be truly clean and dry and you only really get one good chance at placement. I wiped the area with rubbing alcohol, let it fully air-dry, and did a dry-fit first so I knew exactly where I wanted the protector to land. Once it touches down it grabs pretty quickly, so if you’re the type who wants to slide it around to line it up, you’ll be frustrated.

Where these worked (for me) was on flatter, harder rubber where the contact patch is fairly even. On that kind of sole, they sat down without obvious bubbles and the edges stayed put. The traction improvement is real but modest—enough that I notice less “skate” on smooth indoor floors, but I wouldn’t buy these thinking they’ll turn a worn sneaker into a true non-slip shoe.

The main limitation I ran into is compatibility with certain outsole textures. If the heel area has deep grooves, soft spongy foam, or a heavily pebbled texture, the adhesive doesn’t have a continuous surface to bond to, and the edges are more likely to lift once you start putting miles on them. In that case you’re basically being asked to add extra glue or accept that it’s a temporary patch, and that changes the whole value of the product.

After a few weeks of regular walking (not just one test day), the pair on my smoother soles is still holding up and the wear is happening on the protector instead of the shoe, which is the whole point. For my other pair with a more textured/softer heel area, I wouldn’t rely on these long-term unless you’re willing to do more prep and possibly add adhesive. If your soles match the flatter/harder style, these are a convenient way to take the abuse instead of your sneakers.

AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.product_description: Spotlight Oral Care Toothpaste for Sensitive Teeth
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.review_text:
Review of: Spotlight Oral Care Toothpaste for Sensitive Teeth

I’ve been using the Spotlight Oral Care Toothpaste for the last week or so and, so far at least, I’ve been pleased with the product.

I found that the taste of the toothpaste is actually pretty good, especially in comparison to other ‘fresh mint’ flavored toothpastes I’ve tried before that seem to have an off-taste or bitter after-taste them. The mint taste of the Spotlight is present but not overwhelming; which I like.

The foaming action of the toothpaste I consider to be about average; certainly, enough in my estimation to feel as if the toothpaste was getting to every corner of my mouth but not so much as to be annoying.

The active ingredients I also consider to be about average for a decent quality ‘sensitive’ toothpaste; Potassium Nitrate and Sodium Fluoride. And, after using the product a few times now, I consider the effectiveness (based on the potassium nitrate level of 5.0%) to be about the same as many other ‘sensitive’ toothpastes I’ve tried in the past.

After brushing, the Spotlight toothpaste does leave my teeth feeling clean and does leave my mouth with a nice, somewhat minty taste.

For me, while this toothpaste is noticeably better than some bargain-basement type sensitive toothpastes, it is roughly equal to many quality, name-brand or store-brand toothpastes.

The cost, however, for this 3.5 ounce tube of sensitive toothpaste is a concern for me as I consider it to be on the higher side; especially for what I rate as an average, middle-of-the-road toothpaste.
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.best_prediction_review:
I bought this because I get occasional cold sensitivity and I wanted to see if this would do any better than the other “sensitive” toothpastes I’ve rotated through over the years.  I’ve been using it twice a day for a little over 2 weeks.

For me, the honest result is that it’s about the same as many other sensitive-tooth options—good enough that I don’t dread the first cold rinse of the day, but not some instant “zap is gone” change.  If I miss a brushing or switch back and forth with a regular toothpaste, I don’t see this holding the line any better than the big-name sensitive formulas.

The good part is the taste.  It doesn’t have that super medicinal, overly strong aftertaste that makes you want to rush through brushing, and that matters because if I’m going to stick with a product, it has to be pleasant enough to use every day.

My issue is the price.  Since the sensitivity results for me are middle-of-the-road (not clearly better than my baseline), I don’t see the value unless it’s on sale or you strongly prefer the flavor/feel over the mainstream brands.  If you’re looking for a premium-priced toothpaste to meaningfully outperform the category, I didn’t get that from this one.

AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.product_description: When used inside a filter-replaceable cotton mask, the efficiency of preventing splashes increases. It is thin as a single layer but has a water-resistant effect.
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.review_text:
I have both “3D” and regular masks that need filters. These are designed specifically for the 3D masks, so they’re larger than the PM2.5 filters (see my photo for comparison) and shaped differently. I did not have any problem simply cutting the top and bottom “points” off of these so that they’d fit into the regular masks. I did not both to clip the sides as they didn’t hang out that far. So the size was not an issue for me.

My issue was the thinness of the material. At first, I couldn’t even figure out if I was supposed to peel them each apart…or use 2-3 at a time. Compared to my PM2.5 filters these are a lot thinner. If I have to use 2-3 to get the same thickness, then the price isn’t worth it.
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been using these inserts inside a filter-replaceable cotton mask, and what I wanted was something that would sit flat and not make the mask feel bulky. In that respect they do fine: they’re very thin, easy to slide into place, and easy to swap out without fighting the mask shape.

Where I think people need to set expectations is the “filter” part. Compared to the thicker PM2.5-style filters I already had, one of these feels more like a splash/moisture barrier layer than a true substitute. For me the question becomes “how many layers would I need to equal my usual filter,” and once you start doubling/tripling them, the per-use value isn’t as good and it starts to defeat the thinness advantage.

Build-wise they seem consistent (no tearing just from handling), but I’d only buy these if your goal is adding a light water-resistant layer, not replacing a real particulate filter.

AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.product_description: Natural Shoe Deodorizer Spray for Foot Odor & Sport Gear - INSTANT Shoe Odor Eliminator w 12 Essential Oils & Enzymes - Odor Eaters for Shoes, Foot Spray, Gym Bag Deodorizer (4 oz.)
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.review_text:
Review of: Sprayzee 100% Natural Shoe Deodorizer & Foot Spray

I’ve tried the Sprayzee Shoe Deodorizer on 3 different pairs of tennis shoes; one pair for general walking around in, another pair for use at the gym and a third pair of shoes I wear out in the garden. And, so far, I’ve come to a couple of conclusions about the Deodorizer.

The first item, and the most obvious, was the aroma of the spray. While the spray promotes itself as some version of mint, to my sense of smell at least, the aroma was closer to being medicinal; something similar to a pine-scented all-purpose, floor cleaner. While I did not find the smell to be even vaguely pleasant another family member mentioned that the smell wasn’t too bad, almost a peppermint-type aroma.

The second thing ‘discovery item’ for me was to realize that I should always use the spray outside. I first tried using the spray in the garage and discovered that the ‘aroma’ of the spray covered the interior of the garage and lingered for at least a day. This, to me, is an indication of the power of the spray which is a plus.

The next item I noticed was that the bottle itself was somewhat difficult to use. The spray nozzle worked fine when positioned upright but didn’t work well, or at all, when positioned sideways or upside down. This meant that I found it hard to spray up inside the body of the shoe without having to manually hold and maneuver the shoe with one hand while spraying with another hand. And, of course, I found that this led to the spray getting on my hands and the ‘aroma’ getting on my hands. And, this in turn, led me to understand that the spray, with it’s unpleasant smell, was not easy to wash off my hands.

Finally, after letting the sprayed shoes sit for a day or so, outside, I determined that the spray had indeed cut down on some of the original foot-odor the shoes had a day earlier; which is what the product is intended to accomplish.

Unfortunately, I found that original odor had been replaced with a odor that was half foot-odor and half pine-scented floor cleaner. To me, that aroma was perhaps a little better than the original smell, but not by much.

I will most likely continue to use the remainder of the product but I don’t feel that it is likely that I will try the Sprayzee Shoe Deodorizer a second time.
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been using this natural shoe deodorizer spray for a little over a week, mainly on a couple pairs of running shoes that tend to get that “locker room” smell if I don’t let them air out perfectly. I also tried it once in my gym bag (on the inside fabric, lightly) just to see if it would help there too.

First off, it does work pretty quickly for day-to-day odor. I did a simple test where I sprayed 3-4 pumps per shoe at night (toe box + heel area), let them sit open in the laundry room, and checked them the next morning. The “stale sweat” smell was noticeably reduced and, on the lighter-smelling pair, basically gone. On the worst pair, it didn’t make them smell brand new, but it brought them down from “I don’t want these in the hallway” to “I can live with this,” especially if I repeat it a couple nights in a row.

The scent is definitely essential-oil forward. To my nose it’s a strong herbal/citrus type smell right after spraying, but it does calm down after it dries and doesn’t stay overpowering all day. I didn’t notice any residue inside the shoes once dry, and it didn’t make the insoles feel tacky.

Health-wise, I appreciate that it’s positioned as a more natural option (enzymes + essential oils), but I still recommend spraying it in a ventilated area and not going crazy with it if you’re sensitive to fragrances.

The bottle size (4 oz) is on the small side, so value will depend on how often you need it. For me, using a few sprays per shoe a few times a week seems reasonable, but if you’re treating multiple pairs daily you’ll go through it fast. Overall, it’s been a useful tool in the rotation, just not a miracle reset for truly entrenched odor.

AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.product_description: ImmunoSwift - NEXT LEVEL IMMUNE SUPPORT! If you have a compromised immune system or if you suffer from occasional runny nose, sore throat, cough, and dry mouth try our premium product. ImmunoSwift Mint Zinc Lozenges are an optimized way to support your immune health. ImmunoSwift contains top quality Japanese active ingredient that supports your immune system so that you can get back to living your best. Each package comes with 15 quick-dissolving Zinc tablets. Multipurpose All-Natural Immune Booster Our Immune Boosting supplement uses one of the most studied Zinc delivery systems. It works effectively for occasional Sore Throat, Dry Mouth, and overall healthy Immune function. ImmunoSwift promotes immune boosting functions by providing your body with the zinc essential mineral. When symptoms sych as occasional sniffling, sneezing, and coughing begin, take one lozenge at a time up to two times a day. Why leave yourself at risk with a weak immune system or suffer through miserable cold-like symptoms? Try ImmunoSwift today and enjoy its effective immune boosting benefits and relief!
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.review_text:
I like using zinc to help ward off colds and flus. I find that the regular lozenges that are like hard candy are difficult for me…I always end up chewing on them, which is not the way to get the full benefit from them. These lozenges are the quick dissolving powdery variety (kind of like an antacid). It’s much quicker and easier to dissolve them on my tongue without swallowing them whole. The taste is a very pleasant minty taste, which also helps.

The ImmunoSwift tablets only have 8 mg of zinc per table vs the 11 mg in my usual variety, and are priced higher. The L-Carnosine in the ingredient list is supposed to also enhance the effect of the zinc, so that may be reflected in the higher price. If you’d like to try a zinc supplement and want something that dissolves quickly and easily, this is definitely worth a try.
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.best_prediction_review:
I picked up these ImmunoSwift mint zinc lozenges to keep in the cabinet for those “scratchy throat / dry mouth” days. The tablets are small and do dissolve pretty quickly, which I prefer versus hard candies that you’re stuck with forever. Flavor-wise it’s a mild mint and not overly sweet, but there is still that typical zinc/metallic note on the back end.

What I’m less happy about is the lack of clarity in the listing on the full ingredient panel and how much elemental zinc you’re actually getting per lozenge. The description leans hard on “Japanese active ingredient” and broad immune claims, but without transparent amounts it’s hard for me to judge value or whether I want to take it twice a day.

So far I haven’t had any stomach upset using it after a meal, but I’m calling this a “maybe” until the labeling is clearer. Price per 15 lozenges also feels a bit high for what you get.

AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.product_description: Aloe-Cran On the Go Sugar Free Cranberry Drink Mix Powder - Aloe Drink Zero Sugar
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.review_text:
Review of: Aloe-Cran On the Go Sugar Free Cranberry Drink Mix Powder - Aloe Drink Zero Sugar

I was pleased that, especially compared to many Cranberry drinks, this powder version didn’t have any sharp taste to it. Instead, the taste was a smooth, almost-strawberry flavor with just the right amount of sweetness. This made the Aloe-Cran not only easy to drink but also made it enjoyable to drink.

The aloe in the product (200 mg of aloe vera inner leaf gel) added a nice mouth-feel to the drink so the liquid felt more substantial than just drinking flavored water.

The calorie count (10 per container) was good and, thanks to the stevia leaf and luo han guo fruit there is zero sugar listed on the supplement facts label; all nice to have.

I was also pleased to see that each serving has a full 5 grams of dietary fiber; perhaps this is due to the soluble corn fiber in the product. This, for me, is a big plus.

Each packet makes about 8 ounces of drink; this is a relatively small amount compared to many standard ‘drink mixes’ that make about 16 ounces of drink but I found that the 8 ounces of Aloe-Cran were enough to satisfy my taste.

The Aleo-Cran is a tasty, easy to drink, healthy aloe& cranberry drink mix.
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been using the Aloe-Cran “On the Go” sugar free cranberry drink mix for about a week now, usually 1 packet in a 16.9 oz bottle of cold water. It dissolves pretty easily with a quick shake (no gritty stuff left at the bottom), and it’s convenient enough that I’ve been able to keep a few packets in the car without them getting crushed.

Taste-wise, it’s better than a lot of zero sugar mixes I’ve tried. The cranberry flavor is present and the aloe note is mild; it doesn’t have that heavy “diet” aftertaste, although there is still a little sweetness lingering at the end if you’re sensitive to that sort of thing. I didn’t notice any stomach upset, but I do wish the listing was more specific/transparent about the full ingredient panel and what sweetener is used, since that matters to me.

As far as results, I’m not using it as a miracle product—mostly just as a way to drink more water without adding sugar—and it does that job well. Price seems reasonable for the number of packets, assuming the ingredients check out for you.

AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.product_description: USA Merchant | All-in-One PPE Kits |Travel Pack by The Premium Shoppe - | Disposable Travel Kit | 1 Pair of Gloves | 1 Disposable Ear Loop Face Mask |2 Hand Wipes | One Size Fits All (3 Pack)
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.review_text:
[[VIDEOID:a87ad8307c2097351858440688a0e620]] Review of: All-in-One Personal Protection Kits |Travel Pack by The Premium Shoppe - (3 Pack)

I try my best to remember, every time I get in the car, to make sure I have a face mask, some gloves and some alcohol wipes with me. That way, if I have to make an unexpected stop, I at least have the minimum ‘equipment’ I need with me.

I could, for a lesser price, throw some separate items in the back of the car, of course, but, for me, I wanted a pre-made kit where each item (glove, mask, wipe) in the kit is individually wrapped and the aluminum-foil wrapped kit is fully sealed. Then, when I find a need to use the items, perhaps months down the line, they will still be useable.

For this review, I opened one of the 3 kits and I found that my ‘want list’ was met by this product. The glove, which to me visually looked to be at least a medium sized glove and appeared to be large enough to be comfortably worn, was individually wrapped. The mask, an adult sized paper mask with elastic loops was also individually wrapped. Included also was two foil wrapped packages with the alcohol hand wipes.

Each item, at least visually, looked like they would serve their intended purpose at least on a short-term basis; which is exactly what I wanted and needed. I may not ever need to use them but, if I do, they will be in the car ready and waiting.

If this review was helpful, please click the Helpful button below as that helps me know that I'm providing useful information in these reviews.
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.best_prediction_review:
I picked up this 3-pack of “all-in-one” travel PPE kits to stash one in the glove box, one in my work bag, and one by the front door, and it’s basically what the description says: each pack has 1 disposable ear-loop mask, 1 pair of gloves, and 2 hand wipes.

The main value for me is readiness and convenience. Everything is together, flat, and individually wrapped, so I’m not digging around for a loose mask or a dried-out wipe when I’m already in a hurry (quick stop at a gas station/public restroom, etc.). Mine arrived sealed and clean, which matters with anything you’re putting on your face/hands.

I tested the gloves by putting them on and taking them off a couple times to see if they’d be usable in the real world. They’re powder-free, but I didn’t have trouble getting them on, and they have enough stretch that I think a range of hand sizes could make them work (I’d still call it “emergency/short task” gloves, not heavy-duty). The wipes did the job for a quick clean-up, and the mask is the basic disposable ear-loop type—fine for what it is, but I wouldn’t buy this expecting a premium mask.

Price-wise, you’re paying for the pre-packed setup more than top-tier components, but for a grab-and-go kit that can sit around until you need it, I think it’s worth having a few on hand.

AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.product_description: gracosy Disposable Gloves, Food Grade Cleaning Gloves PVC Powder Free Textured Foodstuff Chemical Domestic Industry White 100 pcs White Large
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.review_text:
Review of: gracosy Disposable Gloves, 100 pcs White Large

I’m pleased with the results I’ve had with the Gracosy disposable gloves. These gloves aren’t anything fancy of course, but I’ve found that for general around-the-house use these gloves will get the job done; and that is exactly what I wanted.

The quality of the gloves is about what I expected for disposable gloves; not super thick but not so thin so they tear super easily. Of course, long nails or rings (etc.), anything with an edge could poke a hole in the gloves but, in my experience, the same could be said for other similar gloves.

The gloves are ‘powder free’, which I prefer, but I found that the gloves were still easy to slip on.

The gloves I tested are listed, on the box, as being a size ‘large’. I normally wear a size small in gloves so, for me, I found the gloves fit very comfortably without having to count on any stretchiness on the gloves. As I prefer gloves that are looser, this fit was fine for me.

As a quick test, for this review, I tested the stretch of the latex gloves by trying to put two hands into a single glove and I found that test worked okay as the glove stretched without tearing. After testing the stretch of the glove, I found that a medium sized hand should fit without too many issues. My test also demonstrated to me that a large size hand, even if the glove doesn’t tear, may find the fit too tight to be comfortable.

For me, the Gracosy gloves are just what I need for working around the house.

If this review was helpful, please click the Helpful button below as that helps me know that I'm providing useful information in these reviews.
AGQIAEHZCIQ4F5O6TKP6YJA6V63Q.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been using these gracosy disposable PVC gloves for about a week for kitchen prep/cleanup (raw meat, greasy pans) and general household cleaning, and they’ve been working fine as long as you set expectations for what they are.

The main thing I wanted was powder-free gloves that still go on without a fight, and these do. Even with slightly damp hands after washing, I can usually get them on without tearing, and I don’t end up with that powdery residue on my hands or on whatever I’m handling. The texture is enough that I can hold onto a sponge or a slippery container without feeling like I’m going to drop it, but they’re not so “grabby” that they’re annoying.

Fit-wise, the Large is roomy on me (which I prefer for quick on/off), and they have a decent amount of stretch. I’ve pulled a few on pretty aggressively to see if I could split the fingers or the cuff and they held up better than some of the thinner food-service style gloves I’ve tried. That said, these still feel like a lighter disposable glove, not an “industrial/chemical” glove, so I wouldn’t buy them expecting puncture resistance or heavy-duty solvent work.

So far the box has been easy to grab from and I haven’t had a bunch come out stuck together. For basic food handling and routine cleaning where convenience matters, these have been a good, comfortable, no-fuss option.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: Beauty Biotin Supplement for Hair, Skin, and Nail Support Multivitamin 30 Capsules by BeautyLux
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: Age-related hair loss is REAL!  About six months ago, I added BIOTIN to my daily supplement regimen.  My long-time cosmetologist has really noticed a difference.  I have also noticed improvement in my skin and nails.  This is a great product!
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review: I’ve taken biotin for a while, and this BeautyLux bottle was an easy one-a-day add-on with no stomach upset when taken with food. After finishing the 30 capsules, my nails did seem a bit stronger and I’m seeing less shedding in the shower, which is the only “proof” I can really go by. I do wish the label were clearer on exact biotin amount and quality testing, since that matters for safety and consistency.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: 4 Pack Mop Head Replacement Microfiber Spin Mop Refill 360° Easy Cleaning Mop Replacement Heads Mop Refills (White)
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: These mop heads are almost indistinguishable from the original O-Cedar replacement heads!  They clean well, spin to a desired dampness, and come clean in the washing machine after use.  I appreciate this money-saving alternative!
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review: These microfiber replacement heads fit my 360 spin mop exactly and actually clean like my original heads did when they were new. They rinse out easily and came out of a warm wash still fluffy with no weird odor. For a 4-pack, the cost per refill makes sense and saves buying a whole new mop.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: Vitamin D3 + K2 - Max Strength Vitamin D3 5000IU Cholecalciferol Supplement with K2 mk-7 - Vitamin D Supplement with Calcium for Bone Strength, Mood and Immunity by Vitamorph - USA-made 60 Veggie Caps
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: There is nothing that frustrates me more than when a supplementanufacturer uses "Servings 1," but then somewhere else on the label recommends you take two-a-day. Let's just be upfront and let consumers know that this 60 capsule bottle will only provide a 30 day therapy. That makes this an incredibly expensive D3 supplement compared to other quality supplements. I don't like to be mislead. I will not recommend this product.
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review: I take 5000IU of Vitamin D3 daily to keep my levels stable, so this D3 + K2 (MK-7) one-a-day format fits my routine without adding more pills. The veggie caps are easy to swallow and I’ve had no aftertaste or stomach issues. I also like that it’s made in the USA. Sixty capsules is a straightforward two-month supply at my dose, and the price is reasonable for the strength and combo.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: Bath Bombs Easter Gift Set, Ribivaul XXXL Size 6.5oz ×3 Handmade Kids Bath Bombs with Natural Ingredients, Cloud Bath Bomb with Rich Bubbles and Rainbow Color, Great Gifts at Holiday and Birthday
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: These HUGE bath bombs completely THRILLED a pre-teen!  They are very large in size and take a few minutes to start releasing.  At first, we thought they were "duds;" but when they started to release, the result was "magical" to a pre-teen.  Her mother was worried about the "cleanup" since the bombs release very colorful trails.  The colors truly did not adhere to the tub.  There was a little residue left on our bather, and unfortunately it did transfer to her bath towel.  But. the color did come out in the wash.
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review: These bath bombs are HUGE and make a fun “cloud” bath with lots of bubbles and rainbow color. My kid loved watching it fizz, and one bomb was plenty for a full tub. It looked like a dud for the first few seconds, then it really kicked in and foamed up. The scent is pleasant and not overpowering, and nobody had any skin irritation. I also did a cleanup check and didn’t see staining on the tub, but I’d still use a darker towel the first time just in case of color transfer.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: Daily Bone Strength Supplements - Calcium Supplement with Vitamin D3 and K2 - BioPerine for Better Absorption - Bone Vitamins for Women and Men - 60 Vegetarian Capsules (2 Month Supply)
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: As I get older, I am more conscientious about supporting my physical health through supplements.  With Osteoporosis present in my family history, I pay particular attention to my Calcium intake.  However, it is important to understand that Calcium is difficult to absorb through supplements.  This formulation includes D3, K2, and BioPerine (black pepper fruit) which improve absorption.  Also, I specifically like the fact that this formulation is a one-a-day since I take various supplements to support my health.  It is also important to note that this supplement is GMO Free; Lab Tested; GMP Certified; and MADE IN THE USA!
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review:
As I get older, I’m much more intentional about bone support, and I like this formulation because it combines calcium with D3 + K2 (plus BioPerine) so I’m not juggling multiple bottles or trying to time different supplements. The capsules are vegetarian and easy to swallow, and I have had no stomach upset with one-a-day.

This is the kind of product where “results” are really confirmed on labs and long-term consistency, but the ingredient combo makes sense to me for absorption and compliance. I plan to keep it in my daily regimen and see how it supports my next checkup/bloodwork.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: Vitamin D, K2, K1, Zinc, Quercetin, Elderberry, Turmeric, Ginger & Echinacea Whole Food Supplement 8 in 1- D3 5000 IU Plant-Based from Lichen, Vitamin K (K2 MK7, MK4, K1), D3 K2 Vitamins - 60Capsules
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: I must take 5000IU of Vitamin D3 per day to stabilize a Vitamin D deficiency.  I recently learned that  Vitamin K aids in the absorption of Vitamin D.  I was thankful to find this XGoldHealth formulation that combines the 5000IU of Vitamin D with a 225mcg complex of Vitamin K1, K2 and K3.  I have had excellent results with the XGoldHealth formulation and look forward to the results of my next blood test which will determine the true effectiveness based on my Vitamin D levels.
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review:
I bought this primarily for the D3 5000 IU (lichen/plant-based) paired with Vitamin K support (K2 MK7/MK4 plus K1), because I don’t like taking D without K in my regimen. I like that it also includes zinc, quercetin, elderberry, turmeric, ginger, and echinacea, but I’m not expecting “feel it” results.

What matters to me is that the label is clear on what you’re actually getting per capsule, and that the formula makes sense for long-term use. I’ve tolerated it well with food and have kept it in my daily routine; I’ll consider it a true win if my next bloodwork supports the D level I’m targeting.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: Flexzion Brown Kraft Paper Bags with Handles, Gift Retail Shopping Bags for Small Business Bulk 12Pcs Medium Size (12 x 10 Inch) Heavy Duty Paper Bags for Craft, Book Store, Clothing, Grocery Food
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: This KRAFT bags are just the right size!  They are not too small, and not too big.  They are well-crafted of a heavyweight mil kraft paper.  The seams are well glued.  The handles are attached securely and seem to be quite durable for the size of the bag.  I will order these Kraft gift bags again!
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review: These kraft bags are sturdier than I expected. I used them for a small gift/book bundle and the handles held up without tearing, even with a little weight. The size (12 x 10) is just right for medium items and they fold flat for storage. Seams and bottom glue look clean and consistent, and I didn’t have any strong “paper” odor, which matters for gifting. I would buy these again.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: Root Vitality Liposomal Vitamin C Capsules - VIT C - High Dose - 1450mg - 120 Pills, Ascorbic Acid, Antioxidant, Vegan Collagen Supplement, Non GMO, 2 Month Supply
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: With an autoimmune condition in the midst of a pandemic, I am taking more interest in the effectiveness of the supplements I take.  After doing some research, I found that Liposomal Vitamin C has been shown to have the greatest absorbency rate.  I spoke with my own PCP who echoed this belief.  I have switched to Liposomal Vitamin C, and have managed to steer clear of ANY illness symptoms since beginning my regimine.  I plan to continue use of Liposomal Vitamin C on the advice of medical professionals.
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review: I wanted a higher-dose Vitamin C, but I’m careful about stomach tolerance. These 1450mg “liposomal” capsules have been easy for me to take daily, and I don’t get that harsh, acidic feeling I sometimes get with plain ascorbic acid. I also like that they’re vegan and non-GMO. I can’t “prove” immune results in a short window, but this has been a solid way to keep Vitamin C consistent in my routine without GI upset, which is the main reason I’ll keep using them.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: QIELIZI Pill Box and Pill Organizer with Lanyard,Large AM PM Medicine Pill Case 7 Day Travel Pills Box Daily Vitamin Container Fish Oil Holder (Colorful Flower Set)
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: I used to use rubber bands and a zippered storage bag to keep my supplements safe and secure when traveling.  When I saw this travel organizer, I knew it would be perfect!  I actually use the organizer every day [not just when traveling]; but when I need my pills "on the go," I just slip the organizer into the pretty pouch and securely slip it in my bag.  The pill organizer is well made with a lever latching system which makes each compartment simple to open.  The compartments conveniently stay open while you are filling them.  However, since I completely fill these containers, I have found that the little lever can actually crush the pills on top when closing.  Even though this organizer is large and each compartment is generous is width, the compartments are much more shallow than my previous organizer.  It is a challenge to fit all of my supplements into the compartments and close the lid.  If each compartment was just a little deeper, this would have been a five star review!  Although some reviewers have said the pouch looks "cheap," I think it is attractive, functional and durable.  The manufactured PU "leather" will keep this pouch looking good for a long time.  I did not think I would have a lot of use for the pretty little matching round three-compartment container.  However, I have decided to keep it filled with several doses of OTC anti-inflammatory pain reliever and keep it in my purse.  It is great to know I always have a little on-hand.
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review:
I bought this pill organizer for travel and for keeping my AM/PM vitamins straight, and it’s been really helpful. The main win for me is that the compartments are genuinely roomy when you’re packing a “real load” (multiple capsules/softgels), not just a couple tiny tablets, and the AM/PM layout reduces the chance of grabbing the wrong day.

The lanyard is a nice touch for tossing in a carry-on or keeping in a purse, and the lids have stayed closed without popping open in transit. The plastic feels sturdy enough for daily handling, and the hinges haven’t gotten loose after a couple of weeks.

My only caution is cleanliness/maintenance: the corners can hold powder residue if you split tablets, so I wouldn’t assume it’s “clean” without occasionally wiping it out and letting it dry fully before refilling. Overall, this is a practical travel organizer with good capacity and secure closures, and I would order it again.

AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.product_description: Eye Mask for Sleeping, Sytmhoe Sleep Mask for Men Women, Upgraded 3D Contoured Blindfold, Super Soft and Comfortable, Eye Shade Cover for Travel, Nap, Shift Work
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.review_text: This sleep mask truly blocks out the light.  However, as a side sleeper, I still have issues.  It is admittedly more comfortable than most other mask I have tried when I roll over onto my side, but it has not proven to be the "lifesaver" claimed in the description.  I will continue to wear it and rate it four stars while I continue to search for the "perfect" mask.
AHRJIHB7RXXQJJC7RKYYFCY754FA.best_prediction_review: I am picky about sleep masks because if they press on my eyes or let light leak in around the nose, I won’t use them. This 3D contoured mask actually sits off my eyelids and gives a true blackout for naps and overnight. The fabric feels very soft, and mine had no strong “chemical” smell out of the package. The strap adjusts easily and stayed put as a side sleeper without needing to crank it tight. The listing uses big superlatives, but for me it’s simply a solid, comfortable mask that blocks light as advertised.

AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.product_description: Ferrero Rocher offers a unique taste experience of contrasting layers: a whole crunchy hazelnut in the heart, a delicious creamy hazelnut filling, a crisp wafer shell covered with chocolate and gently roasted pieces. And thanks to its inimitable golden wrapper Ferrero Rocher is even more unique and special. The iconic original.
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.review_text: I've had Ferrero Rocher many times before and these are the same nutella-filled wafer balls coated in chocolate and chopped nuts that I would expect. I love the contrast between the smooth creamy filling and the cirspy wafers. Each is individually wrapped in foil and sits on a paper cup which keeps it fresh and the cup allows me to rest half of it while I pause between bites. It's a two-bite experience, but it is good to savor. The packaging is hard plastic, which is somewhat wasteful, but does a good job of protecting the delicate candies.
