AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.best_prediction_review:
Ferrero Rocher is one of those “treat” chocolates that still feels special even if you’ve had it before. The texture is the main win for me: crisp wafer, creamy hazelnut center, and the whole hazelnut in the middle gives it a satisfying crunch. The sweetness is on the higher side, but it’s not cloying, and the roasted nut pieces add a nice balance.

Portion-wise, one or two is usually enough, which helps if you’re trying not to overdo it. The individual wrapping also keeps them tasting fresh after the box has been opened and makes them easy to toss in a lunch or put out for guests without getting messy.

AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.product_description: 5-HTP Hemp Capsules for Body and Mind Balance - Hemp Oil Capsules with Vitamin B6 for Health, Immunity and Calming Support - Hemp Oil Soft Gels with Omega 3, 6, 9 - 2 Pack 120 Caps in Total (60 Capsules (Pack of 3))
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.review_text:
I've been taking 5-HTP for many years to help with the afternoon blues and general fatigue. If I don't take it, I tend to feel a bit sad around 3:00 pm and want to eat junk food. Taking 5-HTP has always helped me deal with this issue and this brand was as effective for me as others. I'll note that I don't take it for sleep specifically, though I sleep pretty well and it could be related to taking 5-HTP. I don't know if 5-HTP would impact others as it does me, but I've always found it effective.

I found these easy to swallow and tolerated them without issue. I had no stomach upset or changes in bathroom habits. They are quite economical as well. I liked the inclusion of hemp as an added calming agent as well. They didn't make me feel sluggish or tired either.
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.best_prediction_review:
I took these for about 2 weeks, 1 softgel in the evening with a small snack (I tend to get GI upset if I take supplements on an empty stomach). The softgels are easy to swallow and, importantly for me, I didn’t get reflux, “fishy” burps, or next-day stomach weirdness.

Effect-wise, I got a mild but repeatable calming effect within about 45–60 minutes—more like “easier to settle” than a sedating knock-out. I didn’t notice a daytime mood lift, but I also wasn’t using it for that.

The main limitation is that this is a multi-ingredient concept (5-HTP + hemp oil + B6), so I can’t attribute what did what. For me it’s usable as an evening add-on because it’s tolerable and predictable, but I wouldn’t rely on it as a strong sleep aid.

AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.product_description: TEMGCO Heated Eye Mask, Sleep Mask for Women Men, 100% Blockout Light 3D Contoured Blindfold for Dry Eyes with Temperature & Timer Control, Soft & Breathable for Travel/Sleeping/Yoga (Gray)
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.review_text:
This mask has several good points and one which didn't work so well for me. On the plus side, it warms up quickly and has multiple warmth settings. The settings are easy to select because they are color coded (blue is the coolest, red the warmest, and green is in the middle). This is USB powered and has to be connected while in use, which is a bit of an issue, especially if your power source is on the right or on the floor. The cable is quite long, but I still had to move my power source to my left side to give me enough play to comfortable wear the mask while sitting in a chair.

The mask has padding around the edges which raises it away from your eyes. If you don't like a mask that presses down on your eyelids, this is a plus. However, this same raised ridge caused an issue with the fit of the mask for me. It could be that the bridge of my nose is higher than average (though I don't think it is), but it didn't fit at the bottom very well for me and let a pretty decent gap that let in light. I think it worked well for warming my face, and I especially liked that it heated up higher onto my forehead which may have helped with some sinus pain, but I don't think it works for blocking out light/sleeping.

The mask is easy to adjust with velcro fittings that thread through a plastic piece on the back. The band is elastic as well so you don't need to set it precisely and can easily get it snug, but not too tight. The mask has an overall sturdier feel to it than thinner ones and the design is generally good except for the fit at the bottom.
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.best_prediction_review:
I bought this mainly for dry-eye comfort at night and because I need a true blackout mask (I’m sensitive to any light in the room). The 3D contoured shape is the best part: it keeps the mask off my eyelids/lashes so I don’t feel that “pressed eye” thing, and the fabric is soft enough that I can wear it for a full night without it feeling scratchy.

Where it fell short for me is the core requirement: blackout. In a dark room it’s fine, but with a small ambient source (my bedside charger light) I still get a thin leak around the nose/upper cheek area unless I crank the strap tighter than is comfortable. That also makes it more likely to shift when I roll onto my side, which is exactly when I need it to stay sealed.

The heating function does warm up reliably and the timer/temperature controls are straightforward, but the cord setup is a real “in situ” issue: if your outlet/power bank is on the wrong side of the bed, you’ll notice the cable and it can tug when you turn. I ended up using it more like a short pre-sleep warm-up while reading, then switching to a different mask for actual sleep.

If your main goal is gentle heat and you don’t require perfect blackout, it’s a nice, comfortable mask. If you’re buying it specifically for total light block, I wouldn’t assume it will seal well on every face shape.

AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.product_description: Black Seed Oil Organic Cumin-Capsules Black Cumin Seed Oil, Cold Pressed, Nigella Sativa Extract, Kalonji Oil, Black Seed, Hair&Skin Omegas - 60CT Black Seed Oil Capsules, Herbal Goodness, USA Made
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.review_text: I've been taking either black cumin seeds or black cumin oil for several months now. This is my first experience with an "extract." I'm not sure what makes this an extract as what is in the capsules looks like ground black cumin seed. However, it does say it is standardized to 5% thymoquinone so I'm guessing it is balanced in some way. In terms of usability, this is easy to swallow and I tolerated it well when taking it with food (no stomach distress). It smells vaguely herb-like, but it doesn't have any taste. I also did have any unpleasant burbs after taking it. I can't speak to the efficacy of it as a supplement because this isn't the sort of thing that you take and have immediate results with. It's a long-term dietary addition which is supposed to contribute to overall health (as does eating leafy greens or other nutritious food). I believe it is helpful, but I can't prove it. This is only a one-month supply and on the pricier side, but it's hard to rate value when it's impossible to compare this extract to the more common oil supplements.
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.best_prediction_review:
I picked these up because I wanted to try black seed oil without dealing with the strong taste of the liquid. The capsules are a normal size (not tiny, but not a horse pill) and I’ve had no trouble swallowing them with water. I’ve been taking 2 per day with food for about 2 weeks and I’ve had no stomach upset, reflux/“oil burps,” or changes in bathroom habits, which is my main go/no-go with anything oily.

Effect-wise, I’m not comfortable claiming a big benefit yet (black seed feels like a slow-burn supplement and two weeks isn’t much), but I can at least say it’s easy to take consistently and I’m tolerating it well. The listing language around “extract” vs “cold pressed oil” is a little fuzzy, so if you’re shopping for a standardized thymoquinone dose you may want to look closely at the label on arrival.

Value seems reasonable for 60 capsules at my current dosing (about a month). If I notice a clear change over a longer run I’ll update, but for now it’s a convenient way to take black seed oil without the taste and without side effects for me.

AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.product_description: Swonuk White Noise Machine,Sound Machine with Night Light for Sleeping Adults Baby Kids, 16 Soothing Sounds for Sleeping, Powered by USB, White Noise Maker for Bedroom Home
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.review_text:
I have a few white noise machines of various types including mechanical and recorded. This is one of the better ones in several ways. First, I didn't notice any looping in the sounds, though I can't say that I listened carefully after falling asleep. It also has the widest variety of noises among all of the machines that I have had experiences with. I liked that it has buttons for each type of sound so I didn't have to cycle through too many options to get to the one I wanted. The animal sounds are soothing in particular, but I was a big fan of the types of white noise and the fan noise for aiding in falling asleep.

The light on this is pretty versatile. The first white light is very faint and works well as a night light without being too bright. The second one is more of an actual light then the rest are just atmospheric for color effects. One of the most impressive points is the volume. One of my white noise machines which uses recorded sound is pretty good, but it can't be made especially loud. That means it's hard to drown out louder noises with it. This gets super loud and I was happy with the range of volume options.

The only minor quibble I have with this is that the power cord connects to USB. I don't have a USB connector in my bedroom so I had to use my own adapter as this did not come with one. This is a small thing, but worth keeping in mind if you don't have a lot of spare adapters lying around or a USB connector on your power strip or wall.
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.best_prediction_review:
I got this white noise machine to mask neighborhood/condo noise at night and to have something simple enough to use in a kid’s room. The “real unit” is a small USB-powered sound machine with a basic night light and a handful of standard sound options (white noise, fan-like sounds, and a few nature tracks).

Sound-wise, it does what I need: it smooths out the sharp spikes (car doors, hallway voices) so they don’t wake me up as easily. I also did a quick “artifact” check because looping drives me nuts, and on the few sounds I used most I didn’t notice an obvious loop point in normal listening. Volume gets loud enough for our bedroom without having to max it out.

Usability is mostly good because the controls are direct and predictable—I can get back to the sound I want without cycling through everything and without guessing what mode it’s in. That matters at 2am. The night light is fine as a soft glow; I didn’t buy it as a real lamp.

My main gripe is readiness: it’s USB powered, but mine did not include a wall adapter. That’s not a dealbreaker, but it’s an extra dependency you need to have on hand (or you’re stealing one from another device). If you already have spare USB bricks, it’s an easy setup; if you don’t, plan for that.

AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.product_description: When used inside a filter-replaceable cotton mask, the efficiency of preventing splashes increases. It is thin as a single layer but has a water-resistant effect.
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.review_text: I got these filters mainly to use with homemade cloth masks and ordered a "large" and it seems to be just about enough to cover my nose and mouth without concerns about slipping down. The filter is not very thick, but it is tightly woven. As an addition to a mask that is already 2-3 layers, I found it to be satisfactory without making it too hard to breath through. The only down side to these was that they have slits across the bottom and top to make them fit your face more accurate by angling. I would have preferred to cut them myself because they are cut a bit longer than I need and that increases the likelihood of gaps. However, with a good pocket, I think they should work very well.
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.best_prediction_review:
I use these as the replaceable filter layer inside a cotton mask with a pocket. The main advantage is that they’re very thin, so they don’t turn the mask into a hot, hard-to-breathe-through brick, and I don’t notice much added breathing resistance.

They also don’t feel scratchy against my face and they don’t make that loud “crinkle” sound some filter materials do. The pre-cut slits are a mixed bag: in some masks they help the sheet sit in place, but in others they create a little fit risk because you can end up with gaps where you’d rather have a continuous layer.

I can’t realistically “prove” filtration beyond basic splash/barrier logic, but for an extra water-resistant layer that’s easy to swap and doesn’t wreck comfort, they’ve worked well in my routine.

AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.product_description: Probiotic 40 Billion CFU with MAKtrek® Bi-Pass Technology - Probiotics for Women and Men - Digestive and Gut Health Supplement with Live Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium Lactis (60 Veggie Capsules)
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.review_text:
I've been trying a variety of probiotics to help with digestive issues and this one has the shortest range of shelf stability (about 4 months). Since one bottle is a one-month supply, this isn't a big problem. I think the short expiration date is probably a good thing since it means that the manufacturer may be more accurately estimated the viability of the cultures in it.

This is supposed to have a special formulation to help the probiotics survive stomach acids and reach the intestines to assist with gut flora there. I have no way of knowing if it is working. I can only say that I have had considerably less stomach discomfort and bloating since taking this. I tolerated it well, didn't have any issues digesting it or having it feel "stuck". The capsules aren't too big to take, though they are less slender than some. They have a slightly sour milk smell, but they have not taste.
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been taking this probiotic for a few weeks mainly to see if it changed anything obvious with day-to-day digestion. The veggie capsules are a reasonable size, easy to swallow, and I didn’t notice any taste or “repeat” burps, which is usually my first red flag with probiotics. More importantly, I didn’t get the cramping or urgent bathroom changes I’ve had with a couple other high-CFU products, so tolerability has been good for me.

In terms of results, I can’t honestly prove anything beyond “things stayed calm and predictable,” but that in itself is a win in my book because it means I can actually keep taking it consistently. I also like that the bottle I received has a not-too-distant expiration date; I’d rather see a realistic shelf life on something that’s supposed to be alive than a date years out. Overall it feels like a straightforward, low-drama option if you want a higher-CFU probiotic without making your stomach a test lab.

AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.product_description: gracosy Disposable Gloves, Food Grade Cleaning Gloves PVC Powder Free Textured Foodstuff Chemical Domestic Industry White 100 pcs White Large
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.review_text: When I ordered these gloves, I expected something akin to what you see people wearing in fast food places - baggy, thin, crinkly food handling gloves. These are much more like nitrile gloves or exam gloves. They have a good fit (my hands are generally between a medium and a large and these large ones fit nearly perfectly with just a little extra room above my fingers). They have good "touch" (aka, you can feel things through them). They're stretchy and are easy to put on and take off. They aren't powdered inside, but they glide on easily anyway. They are pretty strong for thing disposable gloves as well. The only small point is that they are very short at the cuffs and don't expend much past the top of the wrist. I'm also not sure that they are good value compared to similar gloves, but they are high quality.
AGXNNDGBBN7K2RJQQW7UBR347SZA.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been using these for kitchen prep and general cleaning and they’re a solid basic disposable, but they’re not the super-stretchy nitrile/exam style. The PVC feels a bit stiffer and a touch “plasticky,” yet the light texture helps with grip on wet dishes and spray bottles and they go on/off quickly without a fight.

Fit is the main gate. “Large” is roomy enough for my hands and I can still pinch and pick up small items without the fingertips collapsing, but the cuff is fairly short. If you want wrist coverage for messier jobs (or sleeves you can tuck), these don’t give you much overlap.

They’re on the thinner side so I wouldn’t rely on them for anything that’s rough or likely to snag, but for routine food handling and light cleaning they’ve held up fine. For 100 gloves the price feels reasonable, especially if you treat them as a convenience item rather than a heavy-duty glove.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: FeelGood Organic Superfoods Broccoli Powder, Vegan, Non-GMO, Gluten Free, Rich in Fiber, Flavonoids, and Vitamin C, Pure Organic Broccoli, 7 oz
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I enjoy fresh and steamed broccoli and have noticed that usually, the next day after eating a good amount of broccoli, I feel and look better.  I think there's something in broccoli that really tunes my engine.  So, when this package of broccoli powder came up on my list of items I could review I wanted to try it.  I like it.  I mix it with some wheatgrass powder or other super foods (like to include a sweet one for flavor) and water, mix thoroughly and drink it down.  It's not something to write home about, but I like it well enough and I feel I'm getting some good nutrition.  This powder seems to be fresh and will puff out of the envelope if you're not careful with it.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
This is a straightforward organic broccoli powder. The ingredient list is just broccoli, which I appreciate since I try to avoid “superfood blends” with a bunch of extras.

It’s a fine green powder and I get the best results in a smoothie or mixed into soup/stock. In plain water it tends to puff and clump and the broccoli taste is definitely there (not awful, but very vegetal), so I wouldn’t call it flavorless. If you’re sensitive to strong green flavors, plan on mixing it into something with other flavors.

It arrived sealed and looks/smells like what it’s supposed to be, and for me it’s an easy way to add more cruciferous veggies and fiber without having to cook broccoli every day.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Oil of Oregano Drops, 30 Pre-Filled Single Servings, Plant Based Liquid Tincture Supports Immune Defense and Gut Digestive Health, Non-GMO and Vegan, Eco-Friendly and Biodegradable
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: Oil of oregano is a very potent anti-viral and anti-bacterial.  I've got oregano oil in a liquid (bottle) and in softgels.  I can't tolerate the liquid unless it's diluted because it's so hot.  These little tubes of oregano oil are convenient and taste good, but you only get 50mg of oregano oil per tube.  One softgel of oregano oil usually delivers about 150mg.  If you don't want that much then these tubes are good alternative.  I found that though they taste good, getting all the oil out of the tube was impossible.  There's always an amount left in the bottom.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I like oil of oregano but most bottles are messy and it’s easy to overdo. These single-serve, pre-filled drops are much more controlled and easy to use, especially for travel or keeping a consistent “one serving” routine.

My only complaint is dose delivery: a few of the little tubes would not fully empty no matter how much I squeezed, so some product is left stuck inside. Since oregano oil is so strong, that trapped residue matters because it’s part of what you paid for. The taste is exactly what you’d expect—very sharp and herbal and it hits the throat—so I take it with water right away.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Vitamin D3 + K2 - Max Strength Vitamin D3 5000IU Cholecalciferol Supplement with K2 mk-7 - Vitamin D Supplement with Calcium for Bone Strength, Mood and Immunity by Vitamorph - USA-made 60 Veggie Caps
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I think this is a very good D3/K2 supplement. It seems to be very reasonably priced (as of this review a bottle of 60 veggie caps costs $16.19 giving you a 3 month supply). One capsule delivers 125 mcg of D3 and 100 mcg  of vitamin K2. 210 mg calcium is also included which is only 16% DV. I take at least 5000 IU of D3 each day and usually more since it is such a powerful anti-viral and is so good for brain health. And in combination with K2, needed calcium is stored in bones and being 63, I don't want to get soft or brittle bones as I age. I think this is a very good supplement.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I take D3 daily and prefer it paired with K2 (mk-7) since I also take calcium, so this was an easy one to try. These are 5000 IU D3 and the K2 is mk-7 (100 mcg), which is exactly the strength combo I look for so I don’t have to juggle multiple bottles.

I’m 63 and I’m not expecting a miracle in a couple weeks, but I like having this dose in my daily regimen, especially in winter. The bottle is 60 caps, so at 1/day it’s a straight 2-month supply and the price can be judged off that.

I do wish the listing spelled out all inactive ingredients more clearly, but the actives/dose are what matter most to me here.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Ear Plugs Noise Cancelling, Ultra Soft Foam Ear Plugs for Sleeping, 12 Pairs 35dB Sound Cancelling Ear Plugs for Shooting, Travel, Industry, Study
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: These foam earplugs are easy to use and they expand inside your ears.  But, I would never rely exclusively on these for really loud sounds like gun shots.  I think they might be fine for car races (probably not Nascar - really loud), or to wear to block out some sounds while trying to get to sleep.  I have one in each ear right now, fully expanded and I can easily hear sounds around me so I can't really tell if they would reduce loud sounds or not. They seem to be made out the regular foamy material a lot of ear plugs are made out of and they come very well packaged and you get 3 pairs in one plastic box for a total of 12 pairs.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
These are basic foam ear plugs and they do what I mainly needed them to do, which is take the edge off everyday noise. I tried them for sleeping and also for working at the kitchen table when the TV is on in the next room. Rolled down, they insert easily and expand fast enough that I’m not fighting with them.

They cut down early-morning traffic and random house sounds a lot, but don’t expect “silence.” I can still hear loud voices and sharper sounds, just muffled.

One note: I would not rely on these for shooting/very loud impulse noise. For that I’d stick with proper hearing protection.

For the amount you get (12 pairs), I think they’re a reasonable buy if your goal is general noise reduction, not total sound blocking.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: LipoFit Liposomal Vitamin C Liquid – Premium Vitamin C Liquid with Vitamin E – 300ml Liposomal Vitamin C for Maximum Absorption, Natural Collagen Production and Antioxidants – Orange Flavor
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: All of the liquid liposomal vitamin C products that I've reviewed have all had a strong flavor - not bad but some people might like to dilute in water or something. I take it straight out of the bottle but the sweetened citrus flavoring is strong. I most definitely prefer a liposomal form of vitamin C whether it's capsules or liquid. Liposomal vitamin C stays in your system much longer than the water soluble ascorbic acid which is excreted quickly by the body. Liposomal means the ascorbic acid is wrapped in a fat blanket (sunflower oil) and is therefore carried much further and longer in your system fighting cold and flu viruses. This bottle of liposomal vitamin C is nearly the same as all the others I've reviewed so there's a lot of them to choose from. I can find nothing negative to say about this liquid liposomal vitamin C.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been using this liposomal vitamin C liquid for a couple weeks now, about 1 Tbsp a day (I just pour it into a little juice). The orange flavor is pleasant and not overly “chemical” tasting, but it does have that thicker, slightly oily liposomal texture, so if you’re sensitive to mouthfeel you may want to mix it instead of taking it straight.

I can’t claim any miracle results in a short time, but it’s been easy on my stomach (regular vitamin C can bother me if I take too much) and I like that it includes vitamin E. I do wish the listing was more specific about the actual mg of vitamin C per serving because “maximum absorption” is a big claim. The 300 ml bottle should last a while, but it’s not the cheapest, so value depends on the actual potency.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: QISEOG 2 Pack Reading Glasses Blue Light Blocking with Spring Hinges,Lightweight Readers for Women/Men
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I really like these blue light blocking reading glasses.  They are very well constructed and not cheap looking like some other readers I've reviewed.  The colors on the frame are very pretty and these glasses fit my narrow face nicely without sliding or hurting my narrow nose bridge.  The lenses are very clear with no warping and magnify very well. These come with a cleaning cloth and hard shell case.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These reading glasses are lightweight and comfortable and the spring hinges are a real plus (no pinching behind my ears).  The main thing I look for is whether the optics have any distortion and whether the magnification feels true, and these are clear with no “swim” at the edges when I’m reading small print or scrolling on my phone.  They don’t look toy-like/cheap on the face and so far the hinges feel snug and not flimsy.  You get two pairs which is nice.  I can’t really verify the blue light claim, but as basic readers these work well.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: compostable cling wrap
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: The cling wrap works great - just like name brand.  The box it comes in is cr*p and wants to fall apart.  I haven't had it long enough to see how well it decomposes in my compost heap but if it remains in it for long I will update this review.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: I like the idea of compostable cling wrap, but for me it hasn’t been a reliable replacement for plastic wrap. It will seal a bowl if I really press it down, but after a night in the fridge the edges tend to lift and I’ve had it loosen. No strong odor and the roll quality seems fine. I can’t verify the composting claim yet.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Univera I'MMUNE Aloe Vera 3-in-1 Immune Boost Jelly Stick, Clinically Proven for Immune Support, Digestive, Skin Health, 200:1 Aloe Concentration Supplement, 1 PK, 14 Sticks, Green Grape Apple
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: It's always a pleasant surprise to be able to get a very healthful supplement in a form that is very tasty and super easy to consume.  These aloe jelly sticks are a treat to eat.  I love the texture and flavor and I like knowing I'm getting the health benefits of aloe.  I can't find anything negative to say about them.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These aloe jelly sticks taste like green grape/apple and are pleasantly sweet, not syrupy. The texture is a soft gel that squeezes out cleanly and is easy to swallow, with no odd aftertaste. I like the single-serve sticks for travel and for keeping a daily routine. I’m using these mainly because they’re easy to take, not because I expect a dramatic “immune boost” feeling.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Silicone Facial Cleansing Brush made with Ultra Hygienic Soft Silicone, Sonic vibration facial brush for Deep Cleansing, Gentle Exfoliating and Massaging
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I like these types of facial cleansing devices.  I have 3 of them now and really like using them to exfoliate dead skin cells off my face.  This one is gentle works well.  I don't know how long it will hold a charge but my other two that are very similar hold a charge for a long time.  I don't use any electronic facial devices except for this one and I think it does a good job at gently loosening up dead skin cells.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: I like this little silicone facial cleansing brush. What arrived is just the brush itself (no extra heads), and the silicone is very soft, not scratchy. The sonic vibration is strong enough to make my cleanser feel like it’s getting into pores around my nose/chin without being harsh. I use it about a minute and my skin feels smoother, but it’s not a dramatic “peel” type exfoliation. I haven’t used it long enough to say anything honest about battery life yet.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Ashwagandha Gummies 1500mg Contains Withanolides Extract - 100% Organic Pure Ashwagandha Supplement
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: These are tasty ashwagandha gummies but the texture is more like a soft candy than real gummy which is very chewy.  Ashwagandha helps to calm me down and I sleep better at night and I've read that it helps reduce panic attacks.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These ashwagandha gummies taste good and have more of a soft candy texture than a tough “chewy” gummy, which makes them easy to stick with. I’ve had no stomach upset or odd aftertaste. As for the calming/sleep effect, I can’t claim a dramatic change yet, but I do feel a mild evening unwind. I’ll update after a few weeks.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: PineTales® Magnesium Glycinate - Made in USA | Sleep Support | Nervous System Support | Muscle Support
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: This supplement is in the form of magnesium glycinate. It is used mostly for helping one get to sleep. It relaxes the muscles, lowers blood pressure and basically prepares the body for sleep. I've taken magnesium glycinate off and on for years to help me get to sleep and it usually does the trick.  The type of magnesium (magnesium glycinate) is highly absorbable and is one of the best forms to take.  However, I think this bottle of 60 capsules (a one month supply for $29.99 as of this review) is too expensive.  I've seen it for a lot less than $29.99.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I ordered this Magnesium Glycinate mainly for sleep and nighttime leg tightness. I’ve been taking 2 capsules about 45 minutes before bed for a little over a week. It does seem to help me relax and I’m falling asleep a bit easier, without the “wired” feeling I sometimes get from other supplements.

No stomach upset so far (magnesium can do that), and I like that it’s made in the USA. The capsules are easy enough to swallow.

As of this review I don’t know the cost, but magnesium glycinate is easy to overpay for. If the bottle is only a month supply at 2/day, the price needs to make sense or I’d buy a different one.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: QIN SHAN TANG Lions Mane Mushroom Capsules (120ct), Made with 30% Beta-Glucans, Black Pepper Extract - Nootropic Brain Supplement
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I find this lion's mane powder in capsules to be very good quality. It has no taste that I can detect. I don't like swallowing pills so I open the capsules and dump the lions mane powder into another container and then add it to my coffee in loose form. The recommended dosage is 2 capsules/day and that's about the scooped amount I put in my coffee. Lions mane has many healthy benefits and my husband and I have been taking a blend of various mushroom powders, including lion's mane everyday for the past couple of years and both of us have not been the least bit sick since starting that. I think this is an excellent supplement.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’m going to assume you already know why you’re shopping for Lion’s Mane. I’ve been taking these daily and the main pass/fail for me is tolerability, and I’ve had no stomach upset or weird side effects. The capsules are easy to swallow, and I also like that I can pull them apart and dump the powder into my morning coffee when I don’t feel like taking pills. I can’t really taste it that way.

As far as “brain boost,” I’m not going to claim anything dramatic. But this seems like a good, straightforward way to get Lion’s Mane into my routine, and the black pepper extract makes sense to me as part of the formula. I don’t know the price as of this review, so I can’t speak to value.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Shower Steamers, 18PCS Travel-Sized Shower Bombs Set, Aromatherapy Shower Steamer with Bow Gift Box, Natural Shower Tablets with Essential Oil, Gifts for Women /Men/Valentine's Day
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I don't really see the advantage of "travel size" since all the other shower steamers I've reviewed were individually wrapped in plastic.  But, nonetheless these are easy to toss into a suitcase or bag for trips.  I used two of them at the same time yesterday in the shower and still couldn't really detect much scent.  They're pretty to watch dissolving but as far as scent goes there's not very much.  These come in a very good quality box with a bow, ready for gifting.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These shower steamers are cute and the bow gift box makes them very giftable. I tested one on the shower floor out of the direct water stream and it fizzed right away, but the scent was pretty light in a normal shower (and I’m not someone who needs “perfume” strength). I tried a second one on another day and it still didn’t get very strong, so if you’re buying for serious aromatherapy impact you may be disappointed. Travel size is nice and they’re easy to toss in a bag.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Aspen Naturals Maca Root Powder - Organic, Raw, Gluten Free, Gelatinized Superfood, Keto and Vegan Friendly, Easily Add to Your Smoothies, Baking, Ice Cream, and Coffee. 30 Day Supply
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I'm going to assume that you already know why you're shopping for black maca powder as it seems to have several health benefits, one of which is supposedly increasing sexual desire in both men and women. That said, I think these capsules are good quality and they have not given me any adverse side effects. I have to be careful what I ingest lest I get a serious bout of gastric distress going. So far I have not had any problems with this supplement.  I think this is  very good loose, maca root powder.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve used maca on and off for years and for me the main issue is whether it’s going to bother my stomach. This Aspen Naturals maca seems fresh and being gelatinized it has been easy on me (no GI upset) even several days in a row.

I’m not going to claim any big energy or hormone effects from one bag, but as a basic maca powder it seems like good quality. I’ve been adding it to coffee and smoothies and it mixes in fine. As of this review I don’t know what it costs, so I can’t really judge value.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Nail Clippers Set, Ultra Sharp Fingernail and Toenail Clipper Cutters with Visibly Tin Case by HAWATOUR - Silver
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: These clippers clip nail very quickly and efficiently with leaving any jagged edges.  I still have to smooth the edges down some but I do that with all my nail clippers, not just these.  These come in a nice tin to store them in.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These clippers are nice and sharp and give a clean cut without tearing my nails. The smaller one is perfect for fingernails and the larger works well on my toenails (even thicker ones) without me having to squeeze super hard. I like the little tin case for keeping them clean in a drawer or travel bag. So far they feel sturdy and reasonably priced.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: FG Clean Wipes Non-Woven Polyester/Cellulose Cleanroom Wipes for General Cleaning - 9" x 9" - 300 Per Bag - Saturix WD109
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: As of this review I don't know how much these clean wipes costs.  I think they do a good job of cleaning and are about the same size as a full sized Kleenex but much stronger and can be used multiple times.  I think the idea is that they don't shed any fibers which is great for places where tiny fibers can interfere with sensitive equipment - electronics labs and the situations given on the product page.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These are dry cleanroom-style wipes (non-woven polyester/cellulose) and they behave like that class of wipe. I use them for wiping down small equipment and surfaces where I don’t want paper-towel lint, and they don’t shed, pill up, or fall apart when dampened with alcohol or a general cleaner. They have enough “grab” to pick up fine dust and residue, but rinse out and don’t leave streaky fibers behind. The 9x9 size is useful and the bag count is generous. I haven’t had any tearing issues so far.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: BrainiLex | Clinically Tested Brain Supplement | Memory, Focus, Learn, Perform | 90 Count
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I've tried this supplement in capsule and in powder form.  I think I like the capsule form better because the powder tastes kind of fishy and has no sweet (but it's not bitter either).  These capsules cost $119.95 as of this review and the powder is about ten dollars more.  Both are a one month supply.  However, 3 capsules/day is the recommended dosage and I'm not fond of swallowing pills.  I really wish I could say, "Wow! This has dramatically improved my cognitive abilities" but I can't.  I think I'm the wrong person to test it out since my cognitive abilities are already pretty darn good.  But, I've only been taking it as directed for a little over two weeks and these things often take more time than that.  The directions on the side of the powder container say to take a cognitive test before starting and then after 30 days take another one to see how well your cognitive skills have improved.  I don't have that much time to wait before I can write a review.  I will say that it doesn't make me sick to my stomach or gives me any unwanted side effects at all.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’m always a little skeptical of “clinically tested” brain supplements because a lot of them are basically caffeine in a fancy bottle, so I tried BrainiLex for a few weeks to see if I noticed anything obvious.

The capsules are easy to swallow and I didn’t notice any stomach upset or jittery feeling. I also didn’t get that “wired then crash” feeling that I get from anything too stimulant-heavy, so in that sense it fit into my routine fine.

As far as memory/focus/learning goes, I can’t say I had a big “wow” moment. I did feel a little more steady when sitting down to read and get through tasks, but it was subtle enough that I can’t swear it wasn’t just a good stretch of sleep and normal motivation. This is one of those things where I’d want a longer run (and maybe taking it on/off) before claiming it really moves the needle.

I also don’t know the current price as I’m writing this, so I can’t judge whether it’s a good deal or not for a 90 count bottle, especially since these types of supplements can get expensive fast if you stay on them.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: BEAM be amazing Collagen Peptides (Unflavored, 20 Servings)
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I think this container of collagen is kind of pricey coming in at $39.98 (as of this review).  I've reviewed other high quality collagens that cost less and were also flavorless.  That said, I think this is a good collagen product for collagens I and III.  It dissolves well in liquids and has no taste that I can detect.  I've been taking collagen on a daily basis for about 8 months and have finally started to notice improvement in the skin on my arms and face.  It takes a long time to see results so consistency is needed.  My skin is a lot more flexible and plumper.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
This collagen is truly unflavored (no weird stevia/vanilla taste) and mixes well in my coffee and even in plain water with no clumping. The serving size works out to 20 servings per container, so you do go through it fast if you use it daily.

As far as results, don’t expect anything dramatic in a week or two. I’ve taken collagen for long stretches before and for me it’s more of a long-horizon “keep doing it” thing for joints/skin, so I’m not going to pretend I can prove much yet from this container alone. The main pass/fail for me is whether it causes any gastric distress, and so far it hasn’t. I’ll update if anything changes.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: BIOHM Super Reds Superfood Powder - 30 Servings - Probiotics, Super Beets, Tart Cherry Extract, and 17 Red Whole Foods Packed with Antioxidants, Allergen Free, Non-GMO, Vegetarian
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I think this is a very good red superfood powder but I can't see paying $46.99 (the cost of one can as of this review) for it.  I have reviewed several other red superfood powders and I don't recall any of them costing that much.  I have several red superfood mixes that contain many of the same ingredients as this but the cost is much less.  That said, I think this is an excellent mix, it has a lot of very good ingredients that are very beneficial for good health,  it dissolves well with little clumping, the color is pretty and the flavor is good.  I've only mixed it in water and like it best that way.  I'm trying to drink more water during the day and I always want either a red or green or a mixture of both powdered superfood in it.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I like keeping a “reds” powder around and this BIOHM Super Reds is easy to use. It mixes up quickly in cold water with no clumps and I didn’t need a blender. The taste is tart berry/cherry with a little beet earthiness. It’s not overly sweet or perfumey and I didn’t notice any weird aftertaste, which matters because some of these powders are hard to get down.

I’ve used it most days for about a week and I can’t honestly claim any dramatic health effect yet (I’m probably the wrong person to “feel” antioxidants). For me it’s more about whether I can take it consistently, and this one is easy to drink.

As of this review I don’t know the price, but if it’s in line with other 30-serving reds powders I think it’s a good one. If it’s priced high, the main value here is the good flavor and mixability, not some immediate noticeable boost.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Candle Electric Lighter Arc Windproof Flameless USB Rechargeable Lighter with Safe Button,for Home Kitchen Candles Gas Stoves Camping BBQ Hiking Adventure (6.3 INCH, Black)
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: As of this review this electric candle lighter costs $7.99, which I think is a reasonable price for it.  It works well but is not as durably constructed as some other ones that I've reviewed.  However, it works very well and is nice to have when needing to light candles or even the gas ignitor on a stove.  It comes with a USB cord for charging, but the cord I got wouldn't fit into the port on the end of the lighter.  But, since most of us have about 1,000 of USB cords lying around you will find another to fit if yours also doesn't fit.  I'm using another cord and all is well.  Also, I like that it has a small slide switch on the end of the lighter that has to be in the "on" position before it will work.  I just think that makes it a little safer in case a child should play with it.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I got this mostly for lighting jar candles and the occasional gas stove burner, because I’m tired of matches and disposable butane lighters. What arrived is a USB‑rechargeable electric arc lighter with a safety button/lock (so don’t buy it expecting a “torch” flame). For that job it works well.

The arc is strong enough to catch a candle wick quickly, but you do have to learn exactly where the arc sits at the tip and hold it close. On deep jar candles it can be a little awkward, but still doable if you angle it.

Charging is straightforward, but the included USB cable on mine was finicky (it would only charge reliably with another cable I already had). Once charged it’s been consistent for multiple lights. No smell, no fuel, and no butane taste in the air.

Overall: works as an arc lighter and I’ll keep using it, but check the cable and don’t expect it to behave like a flame lighter.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Spotlight Oral Care Toothpaste for Sensitive Teeth
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I have sensitive areas where the dentin is not completely covered by enamel so if a toothbrush bristle hits it I go electric.  I've been using sensitive toothpastes for years so when this one came up on my list of items I could review I decided to try it.  The only thing I don't like about it is that it contains fluoride since I'm trying to eliminate fluoride from my diet as much as possible.  I've used the Spotlight sensitive toothpaste twice a day for about a week and if my teeth were going to react to it they would have by now.  I have not had any sensitivity issues with this toothpaste.  Also, it tastes fine and my teeth really feel nice and clean afterwards.  As of this review I don't know how much it costs.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I bought this Spotlight toothpaste because my teeth have gotten more sensitive with age (especially to cold) and I wanted something gentle that still feels like it’s actually cleaning.

It has a nice, smooth texture and isn’t overly minty or “burny,” which I appreciate because strong flavors can make my mouth feel irritated. After about a week of twice daily use I noticed less zing when drinking ice water, not 100% gone but definitely improved. It foams a normal amount and rinses clean without leaving a weird film.

I do wish the ingredient info/certifications were easier to find right on the listing/box since I’m picky about what’s in oral care products. The tube seems fine and hasn’t leaked in my drawer. Price is a little high compared to drugstore sensitive toothpastes, but it’s working well enough for me to keep using it.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Elaine Wellness Hydrolyzed Collagen Powder Packets for Women, Convenient Single Travel Size Supplement for Skin, Hair, Joints and Nails, Protein Peptides Dissolve Easily, Unflavored (10 Gram, 7 Pack)
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: These travel packs only contain collagen from one source - bovine and they are very pricey.  As of this review 7 travel packs of 10 grams each costs nearly $20.00.  I don't think the convenience of having 10 grams of collagen in a pack is worth all the extra money.  I have reviewed a lot of collagen products and the latest one I've reviewed only costs $24.95 for 324 grams.  And it contains collagen from 5 different sources. The ELAINE collagen only contains bovine collagen.  All that said, it mixes in with my hot coffee completely and I couldn't even tell that it was there.  It's totally flavorless and did not clump at all.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
These collagen packets are super convenient for travel and they keep the dose simple (10 grams each) without having to measure.  The powder is truly unflavored and I’ve mixed it into water and into soup stock without it turning things sweet or weird tasting.  It dissolves easily with a little stirring and I didn’t get gritty sludge left in the bottom.

My only complaint is value.  This is a 7-pack, so you’re really paying a premium for single-serve packaging.  I buy collagen in larger tubs and the cost per 10 grams is noticeably less, so I wouldn’t use these as my everyday collagen unless the price drops.  For trips or keeping a few packets at work, though, they do exactly what they’re supposed to do and they’re easy to get down.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Dr Amy Myers Beef Bone Broth Collagen Powder - Type II Collagen Protein Powder from Organic Beef Bone Broth - Supports Hair, Skin, Nails, Bones & Joints Health - Keto Friendly, 20 Servings
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: The absolute best bone broth is that which you make yourself using cracked bones and boiling them for several hours.  Drain and filter and freeze what you don't use right away.  However, that is time consuming and messy.  Though I know I get the best quality bone broth by making it myself, a powder is so nice and easy.  I've added this powder it to soup stock and into my meatloaf recipe.  It's savory and not sweet or chocolate flavored (a lot of them are and I really get tired of chocolate everything) and can be added to a lot of dishes or drunk as is mixed with hot water.  It tastes fine and though it's kind of pricey, you get a big container of it.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been taking collagen daily for a while and wanted to try a bone-broth based powder. This one mixes easily in hot coffee/tea or actual broth with just a spoon. In cold water it takes more effort and I still got a couple small clumps. Flavor is mild/savory (not sweet or chocolatey, which I personally prefer) and I didn’t notice any odd aftertaste.

What holds me back is the value. It’s only 20 servings and priced like a premium product, but I can’t see a clear reason it’s “better” than less expensive collagen powders I’ve used, including bigger multi-source blends. If you specifically want a savory bone broth style collagen and plan to use it in hot liquids, it does that job well.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Soothing Mushroom Tea with Reishi and Chaga Mushroom for Relaxation, Stress Support and Sleep - 20 Servings Per Box - Dual Extracted Mushrooms, Magnesium and L-theanine
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: This Freshcap Unwind instant drink mix tastes very good.  It's slightly sweet and no where near being too sweet.  Some people may want to add a little more sweetener to it.  I mixed up a tube in some boiling water and it completely dissolved leaving no mushroom sediment at the bottom of my cup.  It nearly has the color of coffee and when I added a little cream to it it looked exactly like coffee and even sort of mimics the taste of coffee but without the caffeine.  The product page says it contains 1000 mg of dual mix (I guess that means Reishi and Chaga) but it doesn't say per serving.  Nor does it say anything on the box about how many mgs you are getting per serving.  I'm assuming that you get 1000 mg of Reishi and Chaga per tube.  I looked at a couple of bottles of mushroom capsules I have and the label said two capsules delivered 1000 mg and so I guess one tube also contains 1000 mg.  Overall I think this is a good product, especially useful right before bedtime to promote a better night's sleep, and it tastes good too.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’m always a little skeptical of “sleep” teas, but I wanted to try this one because it’s got reishi + chaga plus magnesium and L-theanine (both of which I tend to respond to).

One packet in a mug of hot water mixes up easily with a spoon. I also did the usual check for sludge at the bottom, and there was only a light, fine sediment after it sat for 10 minutes — nothing gritty.

Flavor is very earthy/mushroom-forward. It’s not nasty and it isn’t perfumey or overly sweet, but if you hate mushroom taste you probably won’t be happy unless you add a little honey.

As far as effects, it doesn’t “knock me out” like a sleep med. What I do notice is that it takes the edge off and I feel more settled about 30-45 minutes after drinking it, which makes it easier to fall asleep. I’ve used it several nights now and it feels consistent.

I do wish the label were clearer about the actual per-serving amounts for the mushroom extracts (not just a big blended number), because that’s what I use to compare these to other mushroom products I already take.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: B-12 Drops
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: These B12 drops don't taste quite as good as some others that I've reviewed, but they pack a lot of B vitamins per ml.  These drops contain B2, B3, B5, B6 and B12 which make for a very good complex.  I have found from personal experience over the past few years, that a high daily dose of B12 significantly improves my cognitive abilities.  I haven't had brain fog in a long time and my memory is sharp when I take a good B complex, especially B12.  I think these are excellent drops though they aren't as candy flavored as some others but they don't taste bad either.  These drops are very easy to use and are an excellent alternative to pills.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I like these B-12 drops mainly because I don’t need to swallow another pill. The dropper makes it easy to be consistent (I do a full dropper under the tongue) and the taste is mild/slightly sweet, not that harsh “vitamin” taste. No stomach upset for me.

What matters to me with B-12 is the actual payload per serving, and this seems strong enough that I can tell when I’ve skipped it for a few days (a little more foggy/draggy) and then restart. I’m not getting a “caffeine jolt,” just a more normal baseline. If you’re sensitive to flavors, this is easy to take every day.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Kion Colostrum | Grass-Fed Goat Milk | Gut Health and Immune Support | 30 Servings
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I've got a problem with  colostrum. It may be perfectly good for human consumption, but the soul that is supposed to consume it is the baby goat. I'm kind of sorry I selected this product for review because after reading up on it I don't think it's very ethically sourced. And it's pricey too. I'm finding it hard to believe that a $59.00 bottle of colostrum capsules will give any more health benefits than many other less expensive supplements.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: I wanted to try goat colostrum for gut/immune support and this seems like a clean, grass-fed product. I’ve used it daily for a little over a week and can’t claim any obvious change yet, but I also had zero stomach upset (which is a big pass/fail gate for me). The main drawback is the price for only 30 servings. I’m not convinced there’s enough added benefit here versus cheaper gut-support options unless you know colostrum agrees with you.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Wellmo Natural Vitamin D3 Soft Gels in Fast-Absorbing Coconut Oil for Men, Women, and Children - All-Natural, Vitamin D3 for Bone, Skin, and Immune Health - 60 Soft Gels (60 Day Supply)
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: 125 mcg equals 5,000 IU which is about what I take on a daily basis, more or less.  These vitamin D3 pills are very small and easy to swallow.  D3 is fat soluble and should stay in your system longer.  I think one small capsule/day is plenty, at least for me.  The bottle is too big for the quantity because it looks like the bottle is nearly empty but it contains 60 little vitamin D3 pills.  The vitamin D3 is in coconut oil.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These D3 softgels are tiny and easy to take. I take one a day with breakfast in the winter and I like that it’s in coconut oil since D is fat soluble. No fishy burps or stomach upset. The label is clear: 125 mcg per softgel (that’s 5,000 IU), which is a solid daily dose for me. The bottle is a bit oversized for 60 gels and looks half empty when opened, but the count was right.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Wellmo Elderberry Immunity Gummies for Men, Women, and Children - All-Natural Elderberry Extract Gummies with Vitamin C and Zinc for Immune System Support - 75mg of Elderberry Extract - 60-Day Supply
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: Elderberry is a very potent anti-viral and I use it whenever I feel the symptoms of a cold or flu coming on.  I always feel good the next day.  I think these elderberry gummies with vitamin C and zinc are very tasty and are an excellent daily preventative to keep your immune system nice and strong.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These elderberry gummies taste very good (sweet/tart berry) and the texture is nice, not sticky. I’ve been taking them daily and they seem to be a solid preventative for me during cold/flu season. Each serving says 75mg elderberry extract plus vitamin C and zinc, which is enough for me as a daily add-on without having to take separate gummies. No weird aftertaste so far, so they’re easy to keep in the routine.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Fractionated Coconut Oil, 100% Pure & Natural Body Oil for Massage & Aromatherapy, Carrier Oil for Essential Oils, Non-Greasy Hair & Skin Care Moisturizer by Shiny Leaf 16 fl. oz.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: Coconut oil is often a solid at room temperature (except during the hot months) so I was glad to see this oil available for review.  Our house is kind of cold and it has remained a nice, clear and easy to use liquid.  It's easy to pump out and apply.  I can't say it's the best moisturizer I've ever used because coconut oil is naturally a little dehydrating.  I still like it though and have been using this oil everyday after showering.  It does not smell like coconuts at all and has no scent that I can detect.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
This is a very good fractionated coconut oil. It’s clear, basically no scent, and it stays liquid in my (sometimes cool) house, which makes it much more usable than regular coconut oil that turns into a white brick. It pours easily so I can get a small amount in my palm without making a mess.

I’ve been using it on my arms and legs after a shower and it spreads very well and absorbs fairly fast without feeling greasy. That said, coconut oil (even fractionated) isn’t what I’d call the “best” moisturizer for everyone since it can feel a little drying on my skin if I rely on it alone, so I tend to layer a real lotion over it when I need more hydration. As a carrier oil for essential oils and for massage slip, it does exactly what I want.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Iwi Immunity Gummies for Kids & Adults for A Stronger Immune System | Vegan Algae Omega 3, Vitamin A, C, D, E & Zinc | Gluten & Sugar Free | Natural Berry Flavor | 30 Day Supply
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: Normally I just love gummy vitamins but these don't taste very good.  I like the vitamins and omega-3's they contain, all that is very good.  They just don't taste good.  I immediately get a fishy flavor (got to be from the algae they are made from) followed by some serious tartness.  Though they are still palatable, why consume these when there are other gummy vitamins that are just as good and taste a lot better?  If you don't mind tartness and a little fishy flavor then I highly recommend these.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I got these mainly for the algae omega 3 plus the A/C/D/E and zinc. The berry flavor is fine (not medicinal) and there’s no fishy/algae aftertaste, which is usually the deal breaker for me with omega products. Gummies aren’t sticky and they’re easy to chew.

In our house these have worked more like a dependable daily preventative than a “maybe.” When I felt a scratchy-throat cold trying to start, I took these consistently and it never turned into much. Same for my husband.

Only nit is cost—there are cheaper immune gummies, but most don’t include omega 3.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Elderberry Gummies with Zinc Vitamin C
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: These are very good elderberry gummies. These deliver 100 mg elderberry/2gummies, has 90 mg of vitamin C and 7.5 mg of zinc citrate. Taking two of these is a good way to start the day by giving your immune system a jump start against cold and flu viruses. I've been trying to accumulate a lot elderberry supplements because elderberry is a very powerful anti-viral. The gummies taste very good - sweet and tart and the texture is good.  These are a great preventative if taken every day.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These elderberry gummies taste good (sweet/berry) and don’t have that weird vitamin aftertaste. I’ve been taking 2 a day with breakfast for about a week and they’re easy on my stomach. Elderberry tends to work fast for me when I feel a cold coming on, and having elderberry plus zinc + vitamin C in one gummy is convenient when I’m around grandkids. The bottle is kind of big for what’s inside (looks half empty), but the gummies themselves are good and I’ll keep using them.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Black Seed Oil Organic Cumin-Capsules Black Cumin Seed Oil, Cold Pressed, Nigella Sativa Extract, Kalonji Oil, Black Seed, Hair&Skin Omegas - 60CT Black Seed Oil Capsules, Herbal Goodness, USA Made
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: Nigella Sativa extract is a very powerful anti-oxidant, anti-carcinogen and has been proven to be effective against 97 out 144 superbugs. And this is just a few of it's health benefits.  The dosage is two capsule per day which delivers 700 mg of nigella sativa extract.  I think this is a great supplement to keep in your medicinal arsenal along with Oregano oil and Olive leaf extract. This is one of few nigella sativa powders I've reviewed but I find it to be as good as, and maybe better than black seed oil.  It costs more than black seed oil.  As of this review one bottle of 60 nigella sativa powder capsules costs nearly $40.00.  I've bought black seed oil frequently so whenever I see nigella sativa on my list of items I can review I instantly select it because it is one mine and my husband's favorite supplements.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve used black seed oil (Nigella sativa) in liquid form before and wanted capsules because I can’t deal with the oil long-term. I received a 60-count bottle and have been taking 2 capsules a day for about two weeks.

I can’t honestly claim any big, obvious change yet (black seed is more of a long-game supplement for me), but I also haven’t had any stomach upset or reflux from these, which is always my first gate with oils. The bottle I got was sealed and the capsules looked consistent.

My main complaint is the listing/labeling: it’s described a few different ways (oil vs “extract”), but the bottle doesn’t make it crystal clear what the actual black seed oil amount is per capsule. Without that, it’s hard to compare dose/value to other brands I’ve used. Decent so far, but I wish the payload math was clearer.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Elderberry Gummies for Kids & Adults Immune Support - Vitamin C Echinacea Propolis & Sambucus Black Elderberry Gummy Vitamins - Organic Nature Booster Non-GMO Gluten Free - USA…
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: These elderberry gummies are very near the top of my list of favorite elderberry gummies.  These taste very good but my main reason for liking them so much is that they include propolis, echinacea and vitamin C.  These don't have any zinc and I'm glad of that because it seems like zinc is included in so many other supplements.  These gummies have about twice the amount of elderberry of many other gummies that I've reviewed and I've learned from experience what an incredible anti-viral elderberry is.  I haven't got sick at all this cold and flu season.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
These elderberry gummies are a good way to get immune-support ingredients in without pills. I’ve been taking 2 a day for a couple of weeks and have had no stomach upset, and no weird aftertaste or “vitamin” smell. Flavor is berry and not overly sweet, and the texture is soft (not that hard, stick-to-your-teeth gummy).

What I like is the formula stack: elderberry plus vitamin C, echinacea and propolis. Elderberry is one of the main things I reach for when I feel a cold coming on, and this is an easy way to keep some on hand as a daily preventative. I didn’t see anything on the label that worried me, and it’s non-GMO/gluten free which I prefer.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Daarcin 3 Sizes 60pcs Mixed Resealable Foil Pouch Smell Proof Bags,Mylar Ziplock Food Bags for Daily Life Storage
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: We had an infestation of pantry moths in our pantry and had to throw out a lot of dried food like crackers, cereals, some dried figs and items that are high in carbohydrates.  The rotten little monsters could smell what I had in zip-loc bags and burrowed right through them to the food.  Besides waging serious war with the moths, I've placed a lot of food items in these smell-proof bags in the hopes if there are any moths left that we didn't eradicate that they will not be able to smell the food through the bags and thus burrow into them.  So far it seems to be working but we also having found any moths stuck to the fly strips we hung up in there either so that's a good sign we might have gotten them all.  These bags seem well made and should last a long time.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
These are nice mylar zip pouches and you get a good mix of 3 sizes. They arrived clean (no weird odor) and the zip closures feel sturdy and line up well, and after a bunch of open/close cycles they’re still sealing the same way.

For me the real question is what they’re actually “proof” against. I don’t store anything illegal, but I do store very strongly scented herbs/tea and also things that attract pantry pests. With the zipper pressed firmly all the way across, they did a good job containing smell in a drawer, and the thicker foil seems much harder to chew through than the thin sandwich bags that moths/bugs can get into. I wouldn’t call anything 100% smell-proof if you don’t seal it perfectly, but used carefully these work well and seem well made.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Trace Minerals Liquid Gut Health (32 oz) | 10mg Humic and Fulvic Acid | Protects Gut Lining, Digestion and Immunity Support, Detoxification | Cognitive Function and Mood, Metabolism | Unflavored
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I've been consuming fulvic and humic acids pretty much daily for the past couple of years in a shilajit form, which is dark, sometimes very thick and it never tastes good by itself.  So, when I saw Trace Minerals humic and fulvic acid on my list of items I could review I wanted to try it.  I've purchased several Trace Mineral product before, mainly their concentrated trace mineral drops and have found them to be very good quality.  I think this Trace Mineral Liquid Gut Health is a very good product and delivers some fulvic and humic acids that are so good for health and so rare to find in the basic American diet.  This is not as concentrated as what you get in shilajit, but it's a big bottle, one teaspoon is the daily dose, and it has no flavor.  It tastes like water.  I think this is a very good supplement though I don't know if it will help with gut health or not but is still a healthy supplement. Shilajit can be very expensive (a couple of hundred dollars depending on the quality) but it contains a LOT of trace minerals.  But for a much less expensive substitute I think the Trace Minerals is a good choice.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been curious about humic/fulvic acid products for general gut support, so I tried this Trace Minerals Liquid Gut Health. It’s pretty close to unflavored compared to some other mineral-type liquids I’ve tried, and I can take it without having to brace myself. I usually add it to a glass of water and just drink it down.

As far as results, I can’t honestly say I can prove it’s doing all the things on the label (detox, mood/cognition, etc.). I’m using it more as a “nutritional insurance” type add-on and will keep using it and see if anything becomes more obvious over a longer run.

What I do like is the serving math: 10 mg of humic/fulvic per serving, and a 32 oz bottle should last a while if you stick to the directions. I’ve looked at a few more expensive options in this space (including shilajit products) and for what you get in a big bottle this seems like a reasonable way to try this category without paying premium prices.

No complaints so far and I’d consider repurchasing if the price stays in this range.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Ohuhu Instant Dissolving Laundry Detergent Strips, 32 Count Eco-friendly, Biodegradable, High detergency, Maintains Clothes Original Colors, Protable Size Great for Travel, Dorm, Home, Fresh Scent
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I think these detergent sheets would be great for kids living in dorms at school, or when you are taking lengthy trips somewhere. But for home use I'd rather have my big jug of liquid laundry detergent.  I've used several of these and they seem to clean clothes just fine and don't leave any perfume scent behind on them.  However, I have to use 3 sheets at a time since my laundry loads are big.  At that rate, I only get about 10 or 11 loads done and that just isn't all the economical since one box costs $10.59.  I can get a big jug of liquid for about that much money.  All that said, these clean fine, are easy to carry (especially good for those who can't carry much stuff) and don't leave a perfume scent behind.  These would be great for those with a very limited storage space in their laundry area.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I wanted to try detergent strips mainly for the convenience (easy to store, easy for travel) and to get away from big plastic jugs. I’ve used these Ohuhu strips for several loads now (towels, darks, and a mixed load) and they do clean well, with a light fresh scent that doesn’t hang around too aggressively.

The main “gate” for me is dissolving: in warm water the strip disappears quickly, but in cold water I got the most repeatable results by tearing a strip into a few smaller pieces and putting them in the drum before the clothes. Once I did that, I didn’t find any residue stuck to fabric.

Value depends on your washer and load size. For a normal household doing bigger/dirty loads, I can see going through more than one strip at times, which makes the 32 count go fast. For dorm/travel/regular loads it’s a nice, simple format.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Ancestral Roots Organic Turmeric Powder - 4oz - 100% Pure, USDA Certified Organic Turmeric Powder (Pack of 3)
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I buy a LOT of turmeric powder either from Amazon or from my nearest multi-cultural food market.  I make what is called, "Golden Paste" out of turmeric, coconut oil, water and freshly ground black pepper.  It has helped me and my husband immensely with bone and joint health.  I'm 62 and play very competitive tennis, singles and doubles against opponents less than half my age and I often win.  I am a true believer in the miracle of Golden Paste as my previously super sore and creaky knees have been transformed into those of a 25 year old's - no kidding.  Please google "golden paste" and see how easy it is to make at home and give it a try.  When this ORGANIC turmeric came up on my list of items I could review it took less than a nano second to select it.  I think it's wonderful, it comes in 3 smaller bags instead of one big one and it seems to be among the best organic turmeric I've used.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I use turmeric powder pretty much daily and the real test for me is whether it works in my “golden paste” routine. I simmer turmeric with a little black pepper and a bit of fat (usually coconut oil) and then keep it in the fridge and take a small spoonful each day in warm milk or in food. This Ancestral Roots turmeric is a deep golden color, smells fresh (not musty), and it thickened into paste the way good turmeric should, so I’m comfortable using it as a daily staple.

The packaging is also practical. Three 4oz bags is a nice format because I can open one, keep the other two sealed, and I’m not stuck with one big bag going stale. It mixes fine in smoothies too (turmeric never “dissolves” perfectly, but it blends evenly). No adverse stomach issues for me so far, and I like that it’s plain turmeric with no weird add-ins.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Vegan Organic Apple Cider Vinegar Gummies Vitamins by Clever Life Rx, Dietary Supplement - Raw, Unfiltered Healthy Detox & Weight, Support Immunity Gummy (60 Count with The Mother) B9, B12 Cranberry
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I've reviewed a lot apple cider vinegar gummies and these are my least favorite of all of them.  The flavor is just o.k. and the texture is not very gummy-like.  I like that these have the mother, but that's about all they have going for them except for a little B9 and B12 vitamins.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These apple cider vinegar gummies are easy to take and the mild cranberry flavor is pleasant, with none of that harsh vinegar burn.  That said, the texture is more like a soft candy than a chewy gummy and I don’t really enjoy eating them.  I also can’t tell if I’m getting a meaningful amount of ACV per serving because “with the mother” sounds good but doesn’t translate into anything I can feel.  They’re fine, just not my favorite.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Youthology™ Premium Lion's Mane Mushroom Extract Complex | Lions Mane 4:1 1000mg + Chaga 4:1 100mg + Maitake 4:1 100mg + Shiitake 4:1 100mg + Reishi 30% 100mg | Premium Fruiting Body Mushroom Extracts
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: Hubs and I have been taking a blend of mushroom powder everyday for months and I think it has really helped with our immune system. When this mushroom supplement came up on my list of items I could review I instantly selected it. I like the variety of mushrooms included in this mushroom supplement and am always glad to see Chaga mushroom included.  I buy Chaga separately as a powder because it is so incredibly good for the immune system. All of these mushrooms in this supplement are extremely good for health and there is nothing I can find negative to say about it.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ve been trying different lion’s mane blends for focus and “brain fog” and this one is a nice combo since it includes chaga/maitake/shiitake/reishi too. Capsules are easy to swallow and didn’t upset my stomach, even taken with just a little food. After about a week of daily use I noticed a mild improvement in mental clarity (nothing dramatic, but noticeable enough that I kept taking it).

I do like that it says fruiting body extracts and gives the 4:1 ratios, but I still wish the label/product page was clearer about third-party testing/heavy metals, since mushrooms can accumulate things. Price seems fair for the potency, but I wouldn’t call it a miracle supplement.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Replacement Toothbrush Heads Compatible with Philips Sonicare:Brush Heads for DiamondClean 4100 5100 W3 HX6064 and All Snap-On System Toothbrush Handles,8 PCS,Black
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: These brush heads fit my Philips Sonicare just fine and cost a lot less than name brand replacement heads.  I can't find anything to complain about.  They work great and get my teeth nice and clean.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: These replacement heads snap onto my Philips Sonicare handle and have been staying put. Bristles feel fine (not scratchy) and my teeth feel clean after a normal cycle. No odd plastic odor out of the box. For 8 heads, the cost seems fair compared to name brand refills.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Westside Barbell Smelling Salts, Ammonia Inhalant for Athletes, Weight Lifting, Power Lifting, Increase Focus and Alertness (2 Oz, 3 Pack)
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: The last time I ever smelled smelling salts was when I was a kid and another kid in the neighborhood had a bottle of them for some reason.  We all smelled it and I haven't forgotten how strong it smelled.  These Westside Barbell smelling salts are just as strong.  However this time, I used the technique I learned in chemistry class in college which was NOT to stick my nose right over the flask, beaker or whatever container, but to hold it out from my face and waft the air over it with my hand towards my snout.  These are very good smelling salts.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
These Westside Barbell smelling salts are no joke. I’m not a power lifter, but I wanted something for an occasional “snap out of it” before a workout and these definitely do that. For me one quick waft at arm’s length is plenty and the effect is immediate—very sharp, very intense, and it clears the mental fog fast.

This is not something to inhale deeply or treat like a scent. If you get too close it’s just harsh and unpleasant, so the only “protocol” I’d suggest is: open, waft once, close. Used that way, it does exactly what it’s supposed to do.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Lavender Essential Oil, MAYJAM Premium Pure Essential Oils, 3.38FL.OZ Lavender Oil for Diffusers Soap Candle Making, Ideal for Home Office Car Yoga Use
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: This lavender essential oil already has 102 reviews with a high star rating so I doubt I can add anything unique to say about it.  It has a lovely lavender scent and I will be using this to scent my hand made soaps.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review: I think this is a very good lavender essential oil. It smells like real lavender (not a fake perfume scent) and it’s strong enough that I only use a few drops in my diffuser. The scent is calming and clean and, importantly for me, it doesn’t give me a headache. So far the bottle has poured cleanly with no leaking.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Balance Breens Fisetin + Quercetin 650mg - Supports Cognitive Function, Vitamin Supplement for Graceful Aging - Immune Support Powerful Antioxidant - 30 Non-GMO Vegan Capsules
