AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I've done some research on the health benefits of fisetin but can't really find a lot about it.  It hasn't had many studies and the results of the ones done weren't very conclusive.  But, the reason I selected this product for review was not for the fisetin content but the quercetin.  Quercetin is absolutely wonderful at getting zinc into cells to fight off and kill viruses.  It really goes very well with vitamin C, D3 and zinc and I take all of those daily.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I’m always interested in quercetin and I wanted to try a fisetin/quercetin combo, but I’m also picky about what I’m actually getting. The front says 650 mg but I don’t see a clear breakdown of how much is fisetin vs quercetin per capsule, and that makes it hard to compare to other products or build a consistent protocol.

The capsules I got were intact and easy to swallow and I’ve had no stomach upset or other side effects so far (about 2 weeks). I can’t honestly say I feel any cognitive difference yet, but that’s a subtle claim and I won’t pretend I can prove it quickly.

AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.product_description: Immune booster supplements :The immune system, unlike other system of the body, is very complex. It is an amazing array of cells and tissues that work together throughout your entire body. You can think of it almost like an army of little immune defense soldiers - constantly patrolling the perimeter (24/7) and always on the ready (365 days of the year) - acting as a natural immunity defense system to support and defense you from harmful invaders as you live each moment of your day. It's actually pretty amazing to think about and realize all that our immune system does to keep us healthy and happy. Like any army, there is some work involved in keeping it running like a smooth operating machine, especially if it is operating around the clock! If soldiers are depleted of good nutrition because rations are low, soldiers can become weak and possibly unable to do their job as effectively as they could if they were strong and well fed. In a similar way, if we do things to maintain our immune system defense "soldiers" in a strong and balanced state, we can support this incredibly hard-working system to perform at its very best.* There are many ways to boost and support your immune health, including getting proper rest, eating nutritious foods, exercising daily, meditating, etc. Many people include vitamins and supplements pills in their daily regimen to booster their bodies with extra nutritional support to fill in any possible nutritional gaps and to help keep things running smoothly. This is where Potent' Garden's Immune Support Supplement comes in. This 15-in-1 blend immune defense vitamins is formulated to support the immune system with quality ingredients - immune supplements like vitamin c zinc vitamin d elderberry ashwagandha olive leaf and more - that your immune system can use as it works hard to help you.* *LEGAL DISCLAIMER: These statements have NOT been evaluated by the Food and Drug Administration. This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or preve
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.review_text: I kind of consider this supplement as a back-up to my other supplements and vitamins because although it contains very good things, it doesn't contain some of them in high enough quantities for me.  For instance, I normally take at least 250mcg D3 10,000 IU) daily, and this supplement only contains 3.3 mcg which is a mere fraction and that requires 2 capsules.  But, I like a lot that it contains 167 mg of quercetin, and 167 mg of elderberry, both extremely important in keeping one's immune system strong and healthy.  Overall, I think this is a very good supplement for the immune system but more D3, C, and zinc should be taken along with this.
AEYVPPWR4CIKWX4BGYKCBCDL2CZQ.best_prediction_review:
I like the idea of a 15-in-1 immune blend and this one includes most of what I already keep around (vitamin C, D, zinc, elderberry, olive leaf, ashwagandha, etc.). I took it daily for about 2 weeks with breakfast and it caused no stomach upset, no weird aftertaste or “burps,” and no jittery feeling, which matters to me with anything I might take every day.

For immune support I don’t expect to “feel” anything, so I’m not going to claim it kept me from getting sick. What I did check is whether the amounts make sense for my routine, and some ingredients look more like light add-ins than my target doses, so I’d still keep my separate D3 and zinc on hand if those are important to you. As a convenient all-in-one baseline with good tolerability, I think it’s fine.

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: USUIE Neck Warmer Device for Pain Relief, 3 Modes, Composed of 32 630nm Chips, Portable Neck Warmer Device , Intelligent and Heated Neck Warmer Device , Hot Compress & Red Light Therapy Manipulation
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text: There are red lights around the entire inside of the collar and an infrared heat coil over the spine in the back segment of the collar. It's easy to open and slide around your neck (which I tend to do from the front or side, then rotate the collar into place) and then short-press the power button on the front outside of the collar to start it. You can long-press to stop it at any time or short-press to cycle through 3 heats levels. The red lights appear to have only one intensity (yes! I wouldn't want them to dim). Even the lowest heat (#1) is pretty toasty, and I'm a gal who normally runs infrared as hot as it goes. After about a minute, it's too hot to press directly against my neck, but the collar design allows me to shift it slightly back and it'll stay that way. Overall, I'm pleased. Most red light devices don't allow good coverage of the neck, but this one does.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review:
I bought this mostly for the heat and to see if the 630nm LEDs did anything noticeable for my neck stiffness.

It behaves more like a wraparound hot compress than a flat pad you keep repositioning. You put it around your neck, press the power button, and then cycle through the modes/settings with the same button. It heats up quickly and it gets genuinely hot; I don’t use the highest level because it can feel like “too much” if you sit still. The shape is comfortable for me and the warmth hits the sides/front of the neck well, but it’s not a perfect fit for every posture—if I slouch, I have to re-seat it so the pressure/heat feels even.

As far as the red light, I can’t claim a dramatic effect yet, but I do feel looser right after a session in a way that matches what I’d expect from heat plus light. For me the main win is that it’s easy to use and actually gets hot enough to matter.

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: Sandalwood Essential Oil 100ML,100% Pure Organic Sandalwood Essential Oils for Aromatherapy, Diffuser, Massage, Skin Care, Bath
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text: This is the Boswellia Serrata variety of Frankincense. I regularly use the Boswellia carteri variety but have had a quality Boswellia serrata many years ago. This doesn't smell like I remember it, though it does smell pleasant. I also have a small bottle of Boswellia serrata from another brand that does smell like I remember. I don't know; this just smells sweet and like a perfume. I'm willing to accept that there might be some frankincense essential oil in this, but I feel doubtful about the claim that's all that in it (especially at $16 for nearly 4 fl. oz., now that I noticed the low pricing). I guess I'll use it as an air fragrance or like they suggest in soaps or bath bombs.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review:
This is a large 100ml bottle and I bought it mainly for diffuser use. At 4–5 drops in a full tank it throws scent fine, but the smell is not the dry/creamy sandalwood note I’m used to from other essential oils. To me it reads warmer and a little sweet with a “cologne/perfume” edge, which is pleasant enough but doesn’t feel like a straight sandalwood single-note oil.

Because of that, I’m treating it more like a general woody fragrance/blending oil (it mixes nicely with lavender) rather than something I’d pay premium sandalwood pricing for. If you’re expecting an authentic sandalwood profile, this one may disappoint; if you just want a strong woody aroma, it’s usable.

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: FeelGood Organic Superfoods Maca Root Powder, Vegan, Non-GMO, Keto, Sourced from Peru for Maca Tea Lattes, 7 oz
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text: This is a 7 oz bag of USDA Certified Organic maca root. It looks and smells like I expected. It smells and tastes pretty fresh to me.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review: This maca smells and tastes like plain maca should (earthy/malty), not that sweet-perfumey note that makes me doubt what I’m getting. Color and texture look like a normal fine powder. In smoothies and warm maca lattes it mixes about like other brands, with the usual slight graininess.

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: ULTREMA Red Light Therapy, 5-in-1, Red Light Therapy Device for Joints Pain Relief & Anti-inflammatory, 660nm Red and 850nm NIR Five-Chip Combo
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text:
The device comes with one battery. You unscrew the bottom of the device, put the battery in, and screw it back together. Then you unscrew the top and use the included USB charging cord to charge the battery whenever it's low. A red light next to the USB port indicates charging and green is charged. You screw on the top to use. The button is at one end and the lights at the other. The three LED therapy lights are very small, but they seem quite powerful. They shine brightly through my hand joints. My initial impressions are that this does help with my arthritis inflammation. The light should be used against bare skin and can be held directly against the skin. One of the lights is infrared (heat), so the head can get warm and you may need to let it cool (while off) before continuing to use it. It has 10 minutes before automatic turn-off, and the head only gets mildly warm by the end of that time . The therapy area (lighted area at the end) is 0.75" across, so the downsize is the limited area that you can treat at one time while the advantage is that it's battery-powered portable. The power button sequence is: one press = on, two = off, three = strobe light, four = off. I don't like how fast the strobe light pulses and so will never use it. You never have to see the strobe if you just hit once to turn on and once to turn off (and wait before turning it back on).

When placed on a hard surface, the little stand will hold the light pointed where you want it. I see no point to using the stand as distance decreases the penetrating power of the light hitting your skin.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review:
I’ve used this ULTREMA red/NIR unit on a cranky wrist and one knee for about 10 days. What I wanted was a simple 660nm + 850nm combo light I could aim at a small joint without having to “hunt” for a tiny spot, and this behaves more like a small panel than a single-diode wand.

What arrived was the light head plus a power cord/adapter. No battery option on mine, so this is a plug-in device and you need an outlet nearby.

Operation is straightforward: plug it in, press the power button, and it comes on and stays on (no pulsing or cycling that I could detect). The “5‑in‑1” marketing reads more like mode/feature language than five separate tools; in real use I’m basically treating it as one combined red + NIR emitter.

The useful part is the geometry. The emitting face is large enough that I can cover most of the wrist joint or a section of knee without constant repositioning. I’ve been doing 8–12 minute sessions with the light held close to the skin (a little distance drop-off seems obvious with these types of lights). It does generate some warmth, but not like a heating pad.

Effect-wise, I’m not going to claim it “fixed” anything in 10 days, but I do feel a modest same-day calming of that irritated, inflamed feeling after a session, especially on the wrist. The knee is harder to judge because it’s a bigger area and I’m probably under-dosing it with this small panel.

No complaints on build so far, but it’s not a grab-and-go gadget. If you want something to hit small joints with repeatable placement, this setup makes that easy.

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: XUYU Far Infrared Heating Pad Mat for Back, Neck, Shoulders, Cramps, Pain Relief with Natural Jade and Tourmaline, Auto Shut Off, Smart Timer and Heat Settings, 16” x 21
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text: This heating pad only puts out heat along the center columns of rock: about 1" wide x 10.5" long column of rock with a 0.75" space between the columns of rock. There's a 2.5" border around the edges of the pad which doesn't heat and isn't highly flexible. It works ok on your back but can't be used on the tummy unless you're lying flat on your back. The area that gets warm is 15" x 10.5". It works alright in the sense that you plug the pad in, select the temperature and timer length, and it heats up. However, I had the pad on my lap, a pillow on top, and my Kindle on top of that (several inches above the back of the pad). I noticed that the battery power on the Kindle dropped 10% in just a few minutes (which is really, really fast) and I had to reboot the Kindle to fix the battery drain. It seems like the pad messed with my Kindle's battery which makes me wonder what it's doing to me. Natural jade sounds good, but they're using electricity to heat the stones to being with.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review:
This is a 16" x 21" far-infrared jade/tourmaline mat, and in use it’s more like a semi-rigid/heavy panel than a floppy heating pad. That’s good for not bunching up, but it also means you’re working with its geometry. The warmed area feels centered and there’s a cooler border, so it’s not really edge-to-edge coverage; for shoulders/neck I have to position it intentionally or I end up heating the upper back more than the edges.

The controller is straightforward: plug it in, power on, then choose a heat level and timer, and it does auto shut-off. Heat feels like a steady “dry” warmth rather than a squishy blanket pad, and for localized muscle tightness it’s been helpful as long as I stay on the hot zone.

One safety note: I had my Kindle sitting near/partly on the mat once and it started draining fast and then needed a reboot. I can’t prove what’s going on there, but it was repeatable enough for me to treat this as a “keep other electronics well away” item, and I’m not comfortable leaving it running unattended because of that.

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: Paint Brush Cleaner Soap Solid Cleaner Cleanser Preserver for Masters Pens Brushes Oil Watercolor Acrylic Painting Makeup Cosmetic Conditioner Natural Scented 100g
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text: You get a jar of solid soap for use on painting brushes. They suggest using this for oil, watercolor, and acrylic paintbrushes that need cleaning. You unscrew the top and rub the wet, dirty brush against the soap to work the soap into the brush. Obviously, you then rinse the brushes. It seems to work fine and seems comparable to the brush soap that I currently use. I allow the soap to dry out before screwing the cap back on.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review:
This is basically a little puck of brush soap (mine weighed right around 100g). I’ve been using it to clean paint brushes (acrylic/water-based): wet the bristles, swirl on the soap a few times, work it through with my fingers, then rinse until the water runs clear. It does a good job getting pigment out without leaving the bristles feeling coated or stiff.

It’s simple and fast enough that I’ll actually do it after a session. I just leave the tin open for a bit so the soap can dry back down before closing it up, and it’s been fine.

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: Aspen Naturals Maca Root Powder - Organic, Raw, Gluten Free, Gelatinized Superfood, Keto and Vegan Friendly, Easily Add to Your Smoothies, Baking, Ice Cream, and Coffee. 30 Day Supply
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text: This bag contains 10.6 ounces of certified organic maca powder. That is the only ingredient. It tastes and smells like I would expect. It seems equal in quality to the other brands that I've tried. Since it's a fine powder, it acts like a fine powder when you're scooping some out or stirring it in. I like the somewhat caramel taste that it adds to my chia puddings.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review: This is a single-ingredient organic maca powder that looks/smells like maca should and tastes fairly mild and a little malty. The “gelatinized” part matters for me because it sits better than raw maca, and I can actually use it daily without that heavy/earthy aftertaste. It blends fine in smoothies and oatmeal; in hot coffee it wants to clump unless I whisk it or pre-mix into a small slurry. No dramatic “energy” effect here, but it’s pleasant and easy to keep in the routine.

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: Ksooly Silicone Face Cleanser and Massager Brush | Manual Facial Cleansing Brush with Deep Pore Foaming Sponge | Handheld Mat Scrubber for Exfoliating and Sensitive, Dry Skin, Set of 3
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text:
These are nice for using on your face, but they do not hold enough soap to clean larger parts of the body. The two pads measure 3" x 2.5" and have soft silicone bristles that felt great on my face. You squirt a little liquid soap on these and rub it on a wet face, and the soap suds up nicely and cleans well. The only problem is that soap can get on the other side and make the pad slippery to hold. I just grasp the knob between two fingers (rather than sticking my fingers in the pocket), and then I can keep a pretty good grip.

There is also a scrubber that has a sponge inside. You can squirt soap on the sunny face, then squeeze the sponge to create a fine, white foam from the soap. These bristles are a little bit firmer than the pads, but they are still very nice feeling on the face. The reverse side has thicker, stiffer silicone bristles. The sponge is pretty easy to squeeze and pull out of the hole. It dries quickly when removed. The scrubber is a nice size to easily hold and squeeze while using. It is 2 inches tall by 2 inches wide.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review:
You get a set of 3 small silicone facial scrubber/foaming sponge brushes. One side is silicone with very fine, soft nubs, and the other side is a little sponge pad that holds cleanser and makes it foam up fast.

On my reactive/sensitive skin this is usable as long as I keep the pressure light and don’t treat it like a “scrub.” The silicone nubs feel more like a gentle massage than an exfoliating pad, and I don’t get that over-scrubbed sting that I can get from rougher brushes. The sponge side definitely builds more foam than my hands do, so it’s easier to spread cleanser evenly and get around the sides of the nose without overworking one spot.

These are also simple to use: wet it, add a dab of cleanser to the sponge side, work up the foam, then flip to the silicone side if you want a little more agitation. Because they’re small, I see these as face-only tools (not enough surface area or “soap reservoir” to make sense for body use), but for quick, gentle daily cleansing they’ve been convenient.

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: Silicone Body Scrubbers Brush for Shower-2 in 1 Exfoliating Body Brush Bath Loofah Sponge Face Cleansing Brush Facial Cleanser Mat For Mens Women Exfoliator Massage (Blue)
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text:
While I really like this design in some ways, the sponge is somewhat difficult to remove and replace because it is dense and likewise never seems to dry out because it is so dense. The sponge appears to be made from cellulose. The idea is that you squirt liquid soap onto the side with holes that lead down to the sponge. This creates a reservoir of soap that can be released when you squeeze the sponge. The soft silicone bristles feel nice on the skin. There is a slit in the bottom of the silicone casing where you can pull the sponge out. I initially tried hanging this bath brush in an airy spot with the sponge inside, but the sponge hadn't dried at all after 24 hours. I pulled the sponge out and put it in an airy spot, but it still took two days to dry out. So, if you use this every day, it's never going to dry out and will probably start to mold.

I like the size as it is easy for me to hold while using. The side that doesn't have holes has projections that are a little stiffer and rougher, presumably get rid of dead skin or stubborn dirt. There is a loop for hanging or to put over your wrist when using it. The scrubber can be used without the sponge by squirting the soap on the bristles, but the scrubber doesn't hold its shape well without the interior sponge and so is more difficult to hold. I'm thinking of trying to find a softer, easier to squeeze sponge that might fit in this. The main body of the scrubber is 5" long by 3" wide by 1.75" deep. Made in China.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review:
This is one of those flat-ish silicone scrubber pads with nubs on both sides (coarser on one side, finer on the other) and a hand strap. It’s flexible enough to wrap around an arm/leg and get decent contact, and it doesn’t turn into a soggy, collapsing ball like a traditional loofah.

In real shower use, the main limiter for me is handling when everything is soapy. Once soap gets on the back/strap side, it can get slippery and I don’t fully trust the strap alone. I ended up pinching the strap between two fingers to keep control, which works, but it’s a workaround you should expect.

The nub feel is more “daily gentle scrub/massage” than a serious exfoliator. The larger nubs feel nice on shoulders and upper back, but if you’re trying to replace a rough washcloth or scrubby mitt for stubborn areas, this isn’t that.

Mine is the 2-in-1 style with a sponge insert, and that’s my biggest hygiene concern: the sponge takes a long time to fully dry. I squeezed it out hard and left it in an open area, and it still felt damp later. For me that’s a mold-risk design unless you can store it somewhere with excellent airflow (or you’re willing to pull the sponge out / skip that part).

AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.product_description: APOLLO SUN Vitamin D3 Cholecalciferol 50 mcg Vitamin D 2000 Units (120 Softgels) 4 Month Supply
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.review_text: This bottle contains 120 softgels and each softgel contains 2000 IU of vitamin D3 in extra-virgin olive oil. The softgels are small in size and so are pretty easy to swallow. If you choose to chew the softgel, it does not taste bad. The softgel capsule is made of bovine gelatin, glycerin, and water. They are self certified as non-GMO, and I appreciate any product that is non-GMO.
AGRCXV4NM763VGZGD6BQ2VGNCJNA.best_prediction_review:
These are basic 2000 IU (50 mcg) vitamin D3 softgels. The bottle I received is labeled 120 softgels, so it’s an easy 1-a-day routine without doing any serving math.

The softgels are small and smooth, and I like that the label is clear about what it is (cholecalciferol) and the other ingredients/carrier oil so I’m not guessing about what I’m taking. I’m not using this as an “I felt it” supplement, just a straightforward way to keep vitamin D in the rotation.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: Beauty Biotin Supplement for Hair, Skin, and Nail Support Multivitamin 30 Capsules by BeautyLux
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
I usually take biotin and ran out.
Thought this would be the same taking these.
This has allot of other vitamins in it that I take separate, but the other vitamins In this
Are not strong enough ! Same problem with taking multi vitamins , allot but not enough!
You have to take these for a while to notice if there working, as with most vitamins, and these are pricey for 30 days.
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I ordered this BeautyLux Biotin as I’m getting older and my hair and nails are thinner. 30 capsules is only a month so the price per month matters to me.

Capsules are easy to swallow and no aftertaste.

That said, for something called a “multivitamin” it feels light, and I already take D3/K2 and magnesium. If the biotin amount and the other vitamins/minerals are not strong enough, then it’s not really a good value, and I can’t say it will do much for hair/nails.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: True Grace Omega-3 Fish Oil - 60 Softgels - 1400 mg EPA + DHA - Brain, Heart, Joint & Immune Health - Organic, Gluten Free, Soy Free
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
Before I ordered these Omega 3 fish oil caps I wanted to check them out first to make sure these where a good high quality.
Not from farm raised chemical farms.
Sustainably sourced, wild caught,
Not from a country with filthy water, Not a bunch of unhealthy strange fish mixed in etc.
This is wild Alaskan Pollock, that is as high in omega 3 as Wild Salmon.

This True Grace omega 3 came through higher then what I expected.
The soft caps are easy
To swallow, no burping. .

If you do not want a heart problem, and want to keep your brain sharp , you have to eat healthy, walk, and take these omega 3s.
Eat allot of veggies, no processed food, less salt, organic, lite on the meat.
It paid off for my hubby after two heart by passes, and not traveling and eating out anymore.

5 stars.
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I take omega 3 for joints and heart, and I wanted one that says organic and no gluten/soy.

These softgels are a good size for me, and I have not had fishy burps with these when I take with food.

What I care about most with fish oil is where it comes from and if it’s clean. This one reads better to me than a lot of the cheap fish oils, but I still think the bottle should clearly say the source (wild vs farm) and any testing, because that’s the whole reason people buy “better” fish oil.

Price is a bit high for 30 days, and that’s how I judge it since I already take other supplements.

I will keep using it, but I’m not calling it a miracle, it’s just a decent omega 3 that I can tolerate.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: 8 Pack OralClass Replacement Toothbrush Heads Compatible with Oral B Braun Toothbrushes, Extra Soft Electric Toothbrush Heads Refills for Sensitive Gums, Fits Oral b Pro,Vitality,Genius and More
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
I have a old electric Braun oral B tooth brush I was not using.
Now I decided to recharge it and use it, but I thought
My older brushes were too stiff , so I ordered these as it says there soft!
There just as hard as my old Braun tooth brushes, but these have smaller brushing area on Brussels.
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I ordered these for my Oral-B Vitality because my gums get sore easy and I need a truly soft brush head.

They do fit and click on fine, and they clean ok, but to me these “extra soft” are not extra soft like the Oral-B Sensitive heads. The bristles feel a little more stiff at the gum line, so I can feel it, and I won’t use them everyday for sensitive gums.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: Teeth Whitening Kit - Safe for Sensitive Teeth & Braces - Effective Whitening Treatment - Non Sensitivity Gel Bleaching Kit - Complete Home Whitening System - Includes LED Accelerator Light & Tray
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
This teeth whiting kit has everything in this , it’s complete.
My husband is a coffee and tea drinker, even though we get our teeth cleaned ever
4 to 5 months, ( we are older) He doesn’t like that his teeth are not as white as they should be.

He has used this teeth whiting kit 3 times, and his teeth are looking whiter, although
This cannot get teeth as white as the dentist does.
My teeth are very sensitive , and I won’t try this.
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I have sensitive teeth and wear a retainer, so I’m careful with any whitening, I don’t want burning or sore gums.

This kit is easy because everything is there, tray, gel and the little LED light, so you are not guessing what to buy. I did a thin amount of gel (less is better for sensitivity) and used it every other day for about 2 weeks. I did not get that sharp zinging pain, and my gums were ok as long as I didn’t overload it.

It did lighten my front teeth about a shade, but it’s not dentist level white, so don’t expect miracles.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: Premium MCT Oil High Octane C8 C10 MCT Oil 500ML BPA Free Glass Bottle | Best MCT Keto Coffee, Tea, Smoothies | Pure MCT Coconut Oil 60% C8 40% C10 Non-GMO Coconuts Gluten Free Unflavored
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
What a lovely presentation to give as a gift.
That said I eat almost 100 % vegan, and not looking to lose weight, as this
Says it will keep you full.
But I’m looking for eating healthy.
MCT oil is healthy for your body, and I use it for my Salads with allot of seasonings.
There is not any taste to this.
Most people use this in there coffee, which I do not drink, and
In there Tea. Although I do drink tea but I have not put this in it.
But I do put this in a smoothie when I’m in a rush and don’t have
Time to eat. Although I think smoothie are a waste as there is not
Any fiber left in a smoothie there so parred.

I do think this is pricey, and is clear that it should be.
Comes in a nice Glass clear bottle that looks like a bottle of vodka!
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I bought this MCT oil to add a clean fat to smoothies and coffee, not for the “keto energy” promises.

What I care about with MCT is how my stomach handles it. I started with 1 teaspoon for a few days and then went up to 1 tablespoon. For me this one has been fine, no cramps or urgent bathroom runs, so I can actually keep it in a routine.

It came in a BPA free glass bottle and it was sealed, no leaks, and the oil looked clear/clean. I prefer glass for oils like this, and I’m glad it’s unflavored and from non GMO coconuts, gluten free.

It’s not the cheapest bottle, but 500ml goes a long way at 1 tsp to 1 tbsp a day, so the cost per use is reasonable if it agrees with you. I would buy again.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: DDgro Lumbar Support Back Brace for Work, Lower Back Support Belt for Men/Women, Breathable Waist Protector for Back Pain (Size L)
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
This DDgro back brace is made sturdy.
It feels like there is 3 medal type of thin bars in the back, that are comfortable.
I felt good wearing this , actually for my bad posture.
My husband wears this when his back is sore, or doing some gardening, and lifting things.

My husband wears jeans that are size 32 waist, and I wear a size 8 or 10, this brace is a large, and a Medium would have been to tight for us.

Hope this helps.
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I got this back brace for yard work and lifting boxes, as we get older you have to protect your lower back.
Size L fit my waist fine, and the velcro has been holding good, it feels sturdy and not cheap.

It does give support if you keep it snug, but it’s not going to fix bad lifting, you still have to use your legs. The material is fairly breathable so I’m not drenched in sweat, but after a few hours the edges can dig in, especially if you sit or bend a lot. For short jobs it helps, for all day wear it can get uncomfortable.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: DGL Deglycyrrhized Licorice Natural Herbal GI Support - for Gastrointestinal Health - Healthy Mucosal Stomach Lining (60 Capsules)
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
These DGL are different then the chewable have taken before.
Truthfully when I ordered these I did not realize these are capsules,
Although easy to take.

That said , for me they did not help a strange indigestion I get, like the
Chewable do!
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I got this DGL licorice for stomach lining support, and for me it does take the edge off that burning/irritated feeling after meals, but it’s not a instant “fix everything” either.

These are capsules (not chewable), easy to swallow, no strong licorice aftertaste, and it’s simple to take consistently. I’m using 1–2 a day depending how my stomach is, so you do go through 60 caps pretty quick.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: BATTLEHYMN Pumice Stone for Toilet Cleaning,Toilet Pumice Stone with Handle,Pumice Stones for Limescale,Hard Water Ring,Suitable for Bathtubs,Kitchen Sink,Grill,Pool,Household Cleaning(Grey,4 Pack)
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
I live on a well for water, and get minerals in our water that gets a ring in our toilets even though I
Clean the toilets 3,4 times a week, and some bathrooms do not even get used.
I have been using pumice stones for a long time .
You get two in this package, and they have thick plastic handles that have not broken with pressure.

That said this white soft pumice is too soft for my toilet , and did not take off
The mineral ring as other gray pumice stones are harder and have easily taken the rings off.
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I bought these for a well-water hard ring in 2 toilets. These are the grey stones, but they still feel a bit too soft/less aggressive than the old school pumice sticks, so on my bowls it took more time and passes then I expected. Keeping it soaking wet helps, and go light so you don’t risk scratching, but don’t expect one-and-done on heavy mineral buildup.

The handle is decent and keeps your hand out of the water. After a few uses the stone does wear down, so if you need a really hard pumice for thick limescale, this may not be the right spec.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: Vitamin D, K2, K1, Zinc, Quercetin, Elderberry, Turmeric, Ginger & Echinacea Whole Food Supplement 8 in 1- D3 5000 IU Plant-Based from Lichen, Vitamin K (K2 MK7, MK4, K1), D3 K2 Vitamins - 60Capsules
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
This has D3 that is called the sun vitamin, and is it’s good for our bones.
As we get older we do not absorb vitamins as good, and also
The sun. Although too much sun can cause Cancer of the skin.

Before taking D3 you should get tested to
Actually know how much you need.
I now need to take 10,000 mg of D3, and I Live in California and
Garden everyday.

The K2 mK7, and Mk2 in this takes the calcium from
Your arteries to your bones and teeth that keeps the calcium from clogging
Up your arteries, Although I like
The K2-MK4 better, but that one is pretty Pricey.

Now I notice there is K1 in this which surprises me!
K1 clots your blood, and many people take a baby aspirin
To thin there blood a bit for there heart as they get older, so I feel that K1
Would defeats the reason for taking a baby aspirin.
If any one takes any type of a blood thinner they should Not take K1 at all,
Or talk to your Dr. first.

I’m only giving this a 2 stars cause of the K1 in this.
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I ordered this because I take D3 5000 IU and I am picky about the source, so I like that this is plant based from lichen, and I do think D3 should be taken with K (I prefer K2).  The label shows D3 5000 IU plus K2 (MK7/MK4) and K1.

That said this is not just a simple D3/K2. It also has zinc and a whole immune/herb mix (quercetin, elderberry, turmeric, ginger, echinacea). For some people that may be what they want, but for me it changes the “system” because I already take some of these separate and I do not want to stack things and guess what is doing what.

Also I have to mention K1. If you are on blood thinners, aspirin, or have any clotting issues, you need to be careful with K vitamins and talk to your doctor. I’m not giving medical advice, but this is not one I would hand to an older person without thinking it through.

So I think it’s a good idea for the right person, but it’s too bundled for my routine.

AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.product_description: Pacific PPE 2 Pack Household Glove Mixed Package Reusable Cleaning Dishwashing Gloves-Latex Free Waterproof PVC Gloves for Kitchen,Gardening Gloves Unlined and Flocked with Cotton Liner(Pink&Green,M?
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.review_text:
I like these Pacific gloves, there nicer then other gloves I have bought before.
The gloves are very comfortable, not the heavy thick ones that I have bought at the grocery store before, that
Have always been too big, clumsy, and uncomfortable, and I usually would only use about 3 times and forget about them.
These are a size medium, fit me, but the small would have been better, I have long thin fingers.
The inside is soft and actually feels good.

I use these Pacific gloves for doing the dishes before putting them in the dishwasher, and washing my heavy cast iron pans.
These gloves Get a good grip on heavy cookware.

I also like the sleeves are long, and the water does not run inside, and I use them for gardening.

Good price for two utility gloves.
AHJQPUQLSQZE6LMIUMY7WNRXCQQQ.best_prediction_review:
I got this 2 pack (pink & green) for dishes and cleaning, and these are PVC with the cotton flocked inside, so they feel softer and not that sticky sweaty rubber feeling. I have medium hands and the fit is pretty good, not super tight in the fingers, and they come up enough on the wrist that I’m not getting water running in right away.

Grip on wet plates and heavier pans is better then the really thin disposable type, and the gloves feel thicker like they will hold up for regular use without tearing. I like having 2 pairs so one can dry out, and for the price it’s a good value if you want reusable latex free gloves that are comfortable enough to actually keep using.

AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.product_description: Neurogan Apple Cider Vinegar Gummies - 60 Vegan ACV Gummies 1550mg - Supplement for Healthy Metabolism, Digestion, Detox, Immune - Powerful Cleanser & Appetite Suppressant Non-GMO
AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.review_text:
The flavor is slightly tart ACV = but mostly a mix of natural elderberry / strawberry.
A 2 gummie serving = 1550 mg apple cider vinegar, and 26.7 DV Vit C

The gummies are a small 1/2 square and 2 gummies equal
approx. the other brands (apple shaped) gummies.
> But yet delivering more ACV than most others do.
> That translates to me that I'm getting more nutrition and less tapioca = good
AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.best_prediction_review:
These taste like a mild apple gummy with a little vinegar tang, not harsh, and I had no stomach burning or nausea from them.

The “1550mg” claim is the main reason I bought this brand = it feels like a more serious dose compared to a lot of ACV gummies, and 2 a day is easy to stick with. I’m not expecting miracles from “detox/appetite suppressant” wording, but as an ACV gummy it does the job for me and is pleasant enough that I’ll actually take it.

AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.product_description: *Statements regarding dietary supplements have not been evaluated by the FDA and are not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease or health condition.
AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.review_text:
This is delicious dark chocolate, slightly sweetened with organic coconut sugar.
I think it's the best tasting I've had. All organic and high end ingredients.
EG = organic sunflower lecithin - not soy

I wasn't aware till now that dark chocolate has a natural effect of
supporting the body with it's inherent anti-oxidants.
> And after a bit of looking = it also contains gut healthy prebiotics.
> Earth Echo has added the probiotic of bacillus coagulans which combined
with prebiotics, increases the population of good gut bacteria for over-all health.
((( bacillus coagulans = not naturally found in the body - but produces good
lactic acid in the gut.)))

If you love dark chocolate this is excellent,
The square melts in your mouth with smooth, rich flavor to the end.

The cost seems high but all of Earth Echo products I've had have been pricey.
>> Likewise = all have been particularly high quality and healthy.
AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.best_prediction_review:
The Amazon listing for this supplement is basically just the generic FDA disclaimer, and that’s not enough for me to safely use or recommend anything I’m ingesting.

I need at minimum a full Supplement Facts panel (exact mg/mcg per serving), serving size, inactive ingredients (capsule material/fillers), allergen statements, and country of origin. If those aren’t shown, I can’t do basic dose math, can’t screen for sensitivities, and can’t judge if the price is for “actives” or just excipients.

Since I couldn’t verify what’s actually in it from the listing, I’m not comfortable taking it, and I’m not leaving a star rating based on guesses. If the seller updates the page with clear label photos and full ingredient disclosure, I’d revisit.

AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.product_description: SPLENDA Diabetes Care Shakes - Meal Replacement Shake, 32 Fluid Ounces Per Bottle (Milk Chocolate, 6 Pack)
AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.review_text:
The drink is a fairly thick creamy milk chocolate.
It's a little sweet but not overpowering.

This is said to be a meal replacement, and the drink does fill me up.
While this is good in a pinch, it doesn't contain much vitamins / minerals.
There's 16g protein in each 8oz serving.
That's 4 servings per bottle = 24 servings per case.

This is targeted for Diabetes care so the drink doesn't contain sugar.
((( But the sweetener used (Splenda) may cause some unwanted health issues.
EG: It is possible to cause sugar gravings which may encourage weight gain.
> It may effect gut health and cause gI issues.
> It can cause inflammation in the bowels which can be a risk for some types of cancer )))

I'm not a fan of the style of labeling. The bottle is completely covered in plastic,
you need to use your thumb nail OR a dull knife to poke through it at the neck of the bottle.
Refrigerate after opening - use within 7 days
AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.best_prediction_review:
These are 32 oz bottles, so for us it’s more than one “shake” unless you’re truly replacing a whole meal. I’ve been doing 8–10 oz over ice and then capping/refrigerating the rest.

Milk chocolate is decent and not overly sweet, but the defining ingredient is still sucralose/Splenda and you taste it. For me it’s not just “aftertaste” — diet sweeteners can be a cravings trigger and I’m cautious about making that a daily routine.

Texture is smooth (not gritty) and it does take the edge off hunger for a few hours, but I don’t consider this a complete meal replacement. It’s basically calories + protein with a light vitamin/mineral feel, so if you’re counting on “meal replacement” to mean nutritionally rounded, read the label carefully.

No stomach upset for me in the small serving, but I wouldn’t use this as my primary nutrition source.

AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.product_description: Alpha Lion Pre Workout, Sample Tubs, Increases Strength & Endurance, Powerful, Clean Energy Without Crash (14 Servings) (Hulk Juice)
AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.review_text:
The Hulk Juice flavor is named sour gummie bear which I don't think fits.
This is a sweet / SLIGHTLY sour drink that's quite good. Sour isn't predominate

The powder mixes especially well with a spoon.
It's quickly - thoroughly blended but does settle a tiny bit, not as much as many,
but may need to swirl around the last swallow.

For me the drink kicks in energizing in 1/2 hour and lasts through the day.
This is good for a workout - general work - PC. - and without jitters.

This is a small tub of 4.47 oz. - I measured = there's 14 scoops - scoop included.
Serving for beginners = one scoop in 6-8 oz water = 14 servings
> Advanced = two scoops in 8-10 oz water = 7 servings.

I use ginseng / energy shots / drinks / gummies / capsules / etc.
So I'm kinda aware whats enough for me = and one scoop is it.
Two scoops would likely be fine but I'd be flying and nervous.

Among other ingredients to support the speed and energy of the body and mind
> the powder contains 150 mg caffeine per scoop (as Anhydrous caffeine) meaning
it has no water as to not impede it's impact.
> Plus a tiny bit more in the green coffee bean / green tea extract
So with a cup of perked coffee containing 90 mg this is approx 2 cups of coffee.

Drink 15-30 minutes before repeated action. (OK = I made up the repeated action)
THEY say "before lifting things up and punting them down repeatedly'

Very small print
--------------
This flavor has the same ingredients as their Miami Vice =
Pina Colada / Strawberry Daiquiri which i also think is good.
https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09SQ8KGJ6
AGNUU44ETNXLBOUB53LJGFJP3SQA.best_prediction_review:
I picked up the Hulk Juice sample tub to see if it’s actually “clean energy / no crash” without committing to a big container.

Serving is 1 scoop. I mixed 1 scoop in ~12-14 oz cold water. It dissolves better than a lot of pre-workouts, but if you let it sit you’ll get a little grit at the bottom (not a deal breaker). Taste is sweet/fruity and drinkable, not the chemical-burn some of these have.

For effect = I’m using this as a training-day caffeine replacement, not an everyday drink. One scoop hits in about 20-30 minutes for me and feels like roughly 1.5–2 cups of coffee worth of “awake” (cleaner than coffee for my stomach). I didn’t get the shaky/overheated feeling and I also didn’t get a hard crash later, as long as I’ve eaten something. I wouldn’t personally do 2 scoops because that’s where I start to feel like the risk/reward flips (sleep + heart rate).

I did notice better workout drive/endurance on a normal lift session, but I’m not claiming miracle strength gains off a few uses. The “no crash” part seems real for me so far, but it’s still stimulant-based so timing matters (don’t take it late).

Value-wise, the sample tub makes sense if you’re sensitive and want to test tolerance first before paying for a full size.

AEG3O5TEJXVSBDTAXRAOZPPIY5DA.product_description: Keto Friendly Blue Spirulina Plant Based Vegan Protein Powder > Delicious Mix of Blue Spirulina, Pea protein, Hemp protein, Banana Extract for Potassium, Monk Fruit > No Added Sugar - 28 grams Protein
AEG3O5TEJXVSBDTAXRAOZPPIY5DA.review_text: [[VIDEOID:7566569ce2ff8360f91e231e17b4d1dc]] Returned, I get a replacement with the same problem,: no seal ,open container, all spilled  ( see picture with the second order )  Unsafe, expensive,  not recommended product
AEG3O5TEJXVSBDTAXRAOZPPIY5DA.best_prediction_review: Got this free for review. Seal was intact. Used it a few days in smoothies (fruit/ice/water); no stomach upset or headaches. It’s pricey for the amount you get, so check the net weight. I do wish they showed clearer third‑party testing/certs.

AEG3O5TEJXVSBDTAXRAOZPPIY5DA.product_description: Antioxidant Drink Boost Maqui Berry Powder - 30 Servings - Improve Cognitive, Joint & Heart Health - Standardized to 35% Anthocyanins - Organic, Vegan - Add to Smoothies, Shakes, Salads & Food
AEG3O5TEJXVSBDTAXRAOZPPIY5DA.review_text: [[VIDEOID:d7f69bbfe5ad075d6c952517b310577f]] I ordered this supplement free for a review, for my curiosity and it didn't disappointed us. The only drawback is the price, otherwise it seems to be a great quality product. I used it in combination with other powdered supplements ,frozen fruits ,ice cubes and cold filtered water, so the taste of Marqui Berries didn't really came up, but I tasted  separately and it was really pleasant, also has a nice color. No bad side effects after taking it for a few days. Be prepared to get just 30 g. total of 30  packages. See my unboxing video.
AEG3O5TEJXVSBDTAXRAOZPPIY5DA.best_prediction_review:
Tried this Maqui berry powder in smoothies and yogurt (about 1 scoop/day). The color is a very deep purple (good “proof cue” it’s actually in there), and the taste is mild/tart, not perfumey or overly sweet, so it doesn’t wreck the mix. I’m not going to invent cognitive/joint/heart results off a short run, but I had no stomach upset.

One QC issue: my jar had powder up in the lid like it shifted, and I didn’t see a clear inner seal when I opened it. Not a total dealbreaker for me, but it’s worth noting.

AEG3O5TEJXVSBDTAXRAOZPPIY5DA.product_description: VitaScience Vita-Cholesterol Support - Antioxidant, Cholesterol & Blood Flow Supplement with Coq10, Artichoke Extract, Chromium, Guggul & Beta Sitosterol - 90 Capsules
AEG3O5TEJXVSBDTAXRAOZPPIY5DA.review_text: [[VIDEOID:fae18b958d2a002647736ae537113176]] I guess it's better to spend some💰for alternative medicine instead of Rx stains. Premium supplement. Was free for a review, but will buy soon this brand. See my unboxing video above.
AEG3O5TEJXVSBDTAXRAOZPPIY5DA.best_prediction_review: I’m taking this for cholesterol support as part of my “natural first” routine. No stomach upset or weird aftertaste, but I’m not going to pretend it moved numbers without labs yet. For the price, 90 capsules goes fast at 2/day, so value is questionable.
